title: Compute Weighted Ensemble Predictions from Multiple Parameter Sets — calc_model_ensemble
text: config Base configuration object (provides observed data template). parameter_seeds Numeric vector seeds sample_parameters. seed generates different parameter set. configs List pre-sampled configuration objects (direct mode). Mutually exclusive parameter_seeds. parameter_weights Numeric vector importance weights, length parameter_seeds configs. Normalized internally sum 1. NULL, parameter sets weighted equally. n_simulations_per_config Integer. Stochastic LASER reruns per parameter set. Default 10L. envelope_quantiles Numeric vector quantiles confidence intervals. Must even length form lower/upper pairs. Default c(0.025, 0.25, 0.75, 0.975) 50 95 percent CIs. PATHS List paths get_paths. Required sampling mode. priors Priors object parameter sampling. Required sampling mode. sampling_args Named list additional arguments sample_parameters. n_cases_warmup_mask Integer. Number LEADING cases timesteps initial-condition warm-transient (seeded E flushing new_symptomatic SEIR dynamics settle). Default 2L, matching plot_model_ensemble. value applied returned series ; recorded returned artifact_mask element downstream scoring (R2/bias) can exclude positions. Set 0L record "cases warm-mask". mask_final_deaths_step Logical. TRUE (default, matching plot_model_ensemble), record FINAL deaths timestep laser-cholera structural zero (reported_deaths written tick leading-trimmed, last slot never written; laser issue #82). value applied returned series ; recorded returned artifact_mask element downstream scoring. score_idx_cases, score_idx_deaths Integer (1-based). Per-channel scored time-window START index (burn-/ deaths-era start). Columns strictly indices unscored recorded artifact_mask R2/bias scoring drops . Default 1L (-op). applied returned series . parallel Logical. Use parallel cluster simulations. Default FALSE. n_cores Integer NULL. Number cores parallel = TRUE. root_dir Character. MOSAIC root directory. Required parallel = TRUE. precomputed_results Optional list pre-gathered LASER results (e.g. Dask). element must $param_idx, $stoch_idx, $reported_cases, $reported_deaths, $success. verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default TRUE.

title: Compute Weighted Ensemble Predictions from Multiple Parameter Sets — calc_model_ensemble
text: S3 object class "mosaic_ensemble" containing: cases_mean Matrix (n_locations x n_time_points) weighted mean cases. cases_median Matrix weighted median cases. deaths_mean Matrix weighted mean deaths. deaths_median Matrix weighted median deaths. ci_bounds List CI pairs, $lower $upper matrices. obs_cases Observed cases matrix config. obs_deaths Observed deaths matrix config. cases_array 4-D array (n_locations x n_time_points x n_param_sets x n_stoch). deaths_array 4-D array matching cases_array dimensions. parameter_weights Normalized weight vector. seeds Integer vector per-member simulation seeds, aligned parameter dimension cases_array (member <-> seeds[]). Bound parameter set produced member consumers (e.g. medoid selection) need rely positional alignment external vector. n_param_sets Number parameter sets. n_simulations_per_config Stochastic runs per parameter set. n_successful Number successful simulations. location_names Character vector location names. date_start Simulation start date. date_stop Simulation end date. envelope_quantiles Quantiles used CI envelopes. artifact_mask List recording engine-artifact masking spec downstream scoring: $cases_warmup (integer, leading cases timesteps exclude), $deaths_final (logical, exclude final deaths timestep), $score_idx_cases/$score_idx_deaths (integer, 1-based per-channel scored-window start; columns dropped). central/quantile/array fields RAW (unmasked); spec contract scoring sites use drop artifact positions.

title: Calculate Effective Sample Size (ESS) — calc_model_ess
text: Computes effective sample size using either Kish formula perplexity (entropy-based) method.

title: Calculate Effective Sample Size (ESS) — calc_model_ess
code: calc_model_ess(w, method = c("kish", "perplexity"), na_rm = TRUE)

title: Calculate Effective Sample Size (ESS) — calc_model_ess
text: w Numeric vector nonnegative weights (normalized ). method Character string specifying calculation method: "kish" (default) "perplexity". na_rm Logical; remove non-finite entries w. Default TRUE.

title: Calculate Effective Sample Size (ESS) — calc_model_ess
text: Numeric scalar: effective sample size.

title: Calculate Effective Sample Size (ESS) — calc_model_ess
text: Two methods available: Kish method (method = "kish"): Uses standard Kish ESS formula: \(\mathrm{ESS} = (\sum w)^2 / \sum w^2\). provides measure much information retained using weighted samples compared unweighted sample size. Perplexity method (method = "perplexity"): Uses exponential entropy: \(\mathrm{ESS} = \exp(-\sum w_i \log w_i)\), weights first normalized sum 1. measures effective number models based entropy weight distribution. Also known perplexity exponential entropy. methods yield values 1 (weight one model) \(n\) (uniform weights), measure slightly different aspects weight concentration. Kish formula commonly used survey sampling, perplexity common information theory model averaging.

title: Calculate Effective Sample Size (ESS) — calc_model_ess
text: Kish, L. (1965). Survey Sampling. Wiley.

title: Calculate Effective Sample Size (ESS) — calc_model_ess
code: # Uniform weights w_uniform <- rep(1/10, 10) calc_model_ess(w_uniform, method = "kish")       # 10 #> [1] 10 calc_model_ess(w_uniform, method = "perplexity") # 10 #> [1] 10  # Concentrated weights w_conc <- c(0.9, rep(0.01, 10)) calc_model_ess(w_conc, method = "kish")       # ~1.2 #> [1] 1.233046 calc_model_ess(w_conc, method = "perplexity") # ~1.5 #> [1] 1.742536

title: Calculate Parameter-Specific ESS — calc_model_ess_parameter
text: Computes effective sample size (ESS) individual parameters using one two methods: binned marginal Kish ESS (default) KDE-based marginal posterior estimation.

title: Calculate Parameter-Specific ESS — calc_model_ess_parameter
code: calc_model_ess_parameter(   results,   param_names,   likelihood_col = "likelihood",   n_bins = 100,   n_grid = 100,   method = c("kish", "perplexity"),   marginal_method = c("kde", "binned"),   verbose = FALSE )

title: Calculate Parameter-Specific ESS — calc_model_ess_parameter
text: results Data frame containing simulation results param_names Character vector parameter names analyze (required) likelihood_col Character name column containing log-likelihood values (default: "likelihood") n_bins Integer number bins binned method, NULL adaptive sqrt(n) scaling. Fixed bin count (e.g. 100) removes sample-size dependence ESS estimates. Default: 100. used "binned" method. n_grid Integer number grid points KDE evaluation (default: 100, used "kde" method) method Character string specifying ESS formula: "kish" "perplexity" marginal_method Character string specifying marginal weights constructed: "kde" (default, KDE-based marginal posterior estimation) "binned" (conservative, directly sensitive importance weight distribution — recommended final production runs). verbose Logical whether print progress messages (default: FALSE)

title: Calculate Parameter-Specific ESS — calc_model_ess_parameter
text: Data frame columns: parameter: Parameter name type: Parameter type ('global' 'location') iso_code: ISO code location parameters (NA global) ess_marginal: Marginal ESS parameter

title: Calculate Parameter-Specific ESS — calc_model_ess_parameter
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic usage with simulation results ess_results <- calc_model_ess_parameter(   results = simulation_results,   param_names = c("tau_i", "mu_j_baseline", "gamma_2") )  # With custom likelihood column name ess_results <- calc_model_ess_parameter(   results = simulation_results,   param_names = c("tau_i", "mu_j_baseline", "gamma_2"),   likelihood_col = "log_lik" )  } # }

what: Compute the total model likelihood — calc_model_likelihood
title: Compute the total model likelihood — calc_model_likelihood
text: Scores model fits observed data using Negative Binomial (NB) time-series log-likelihood per location outcome (cases, deaths) weighted MoM dispersion estimate k_min floor.

title: Compute the total model likelihood — calc_model_likelihood
code: calc_model_likelihood(   obs_cases,   est_cases,   obs_deaths,   est_deaths,   weight_cases = NULL,   weight_deaths = NULL,   weights_location = NULL,   weights_time = NULL,   weights_obs_cases = NULL,   weights_obs_deaths = NULL,   config = NULL,   nb_k_min_cases = 3,   nb_k_min_deaths = 3,   verbose = FALSE,   weight_peak_timing = 0,   weight_peak_magnitude = 0,   weight_cumulative_total = 0,   weight_wis = 0,   sigma_peak_time = 1,   sigma_peak_log = 0.5,   wis_quantiles = c(0.025, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.975),   cumulative_timepoints = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1) )

title: Compute the total model likelihood — calc_model_likelihood
text: obs_cases, est_cases Matrices n_locations x n_time_steps. obs_deaths, est_deaths Matrices n_locations x n_time_steps. weight_cases, weight_deaths Scalar weights case/death blocks. Default 1. weights_location Length-n_locations non-negative weights. weights_time Length-n_time_steps non-negative weights. weights_obs_cases, weights_obs_deaths Optional per-observation confidence-weight matrices (n_locations x n_time_steps, values [0,1]; NA corresponding cell NA). supplied, per-cell weight multiplies weights_time NB cases/deaths term respectively, resulting per-location weight vector renormalized preserve current masked-weights_time mass trust SHAPE matters (cross-location balance stays weights_location). Default NULL (per-cell weighting; exact unweighted code path used, byte-identical prior behavior). row -1 finite-obs cells also routed exact unweighted path. NB cases/deaths terms weighted; shape terms (v1). config Optional LASER config list (location_name, date_start, date_stop). nb_k_min_cases Minimum NB dispersion floor cases. Default 3. nb_k_min_deaths Minimum NB dispersion floor deaths. Default 3. verbose TRUE, prints component summaries per location. weight_peak_timing, weight_peak_magnitude Weights peak terms (T-normalized). Default 0 (). Set > 0 enable; 0.25 = 25 percent NB core influence. weight_cumulative_total Weight cumulative progression (T-normalized). Default 0 (). Cumulative helper /end_idx normalized weights scale shape terms. weight_wis Weight WIS term (T-normalized). Default 0 (). Ablation tests show 0.10 provides trajectory-shape regularization. sigma_peak_time SD (weeks) peak timing Normal; default 1. sigma_peak_log Base SD log-scale peak magnitude; default 0.5. wis_quantiles Quantiles WIS enabled. cumulative_timepoints Fractions cumulative progression.

Shape terms internally T-normalized weight parameters share common scale: weight = 0.25 means term contributes roughly 25 percent much NB core. Peaks scaled T / N_peaks, cumulative WIS T (return per-evaluation averages). Non-finite per-location LL values replaced -Inf (zero importance weight). NB likelihood naturally produces negative scores bad fits without needing artificial guardrails.

title: Gibbs-Posterior Model Weights — calc_model_weights_gibbs
text: Converts numeric vector model scores (treated loss-like, .e., lower--better) normalized posterior weights using Gibbs (generalized Bayes) formulation: $$w_m(\eta) = \frac{\exp(-\eta \, x_m)}{\sum_{j=1}^K \exp(-\eta \, x_j)} \,,$$ \(x_m\) loss (information criterion) model \(m\) \(\eta \ge 0\) inverse temperature (sharpness) parameter.

title: Gibbs-Posterior Model Weights — calc_model_weights_gibbs
code: calc_model_weights_gibbs(x, eta, verbose = FALSE, ...)

title: Gibbs-Posterior Model Weights — calc_model_weights_gibbs
text: x Numeric vector model scores (treated loss-like; lower better). eta Non-negative scalar inverse temperature \(\eta\). Higher values concentrate weight best models; lower values flatten weights. Typical choices: 0.5 \(\Delta\)AIC (Akaike weights), 1 \(-\log L\). verbose Logical; TRUE, prints brief diagnostics (range x, applied shift, entropy, effective number models). ... Reserved backward compatibility. deprecated argument temperature accepted mapped eta warning.

title: Gibbs-Posterior Model Weights — calc_model_weights_gibbs
text: numeric vector length length(x) containing weights sum 1. Names preserved present.

title: Gibbs-Posterior Model Weights — calc_model_weights_gibbs
text: function agnostic specific form \(x\); smaller values assumed indicate better models. Common choices include: \(x = \Delta \mathrm{AIC}\) \; (use eta = 1/2 recover Akaike weights), \(x = -\log L\) \; (use eta = 1 normalized likelihood weights), Cross-validated losses (e.g., RMSE, MSLE), proper scoring rule. start higher--better score (e.g., log-likelihood), pass x = -score lower--better holds. Numerical stability: implementation subtracts \(\min(x)\) prior exponentiation; shift leaves normalized weights unchanged helps avoid underflow/overflow. Special cases: eta = 0: uniform weights. eta = Inf: hard selection (ties share weight equally). x equal: uniform weights.

title: Gibbs-Posterior Model Weights — calc_model_weights_gibbs
text: Bissiri, P. G., Holmes, C. C., & Walker, S. G. (2016). general framework updating belief distributions. Journal Royal Statistical Society: Series B, 78(5), 1103–1130. doi:10.1111/rssb.12158

title: Gibbs-Posterior Model Weights — calc_model_weights_gibbs
code: # Three models with Delta AIC = (0, 2, 6): Akaike weights via eta = 0.5 calc_model_weights_gibbs(x = c(0, 2, 6), eta = 0.5) #> [1] 0.70538451 0.25949646 0.03511903  # From negative log-likelihoods: normalized likelihood weights with eta = 1 calc_model_weights_gibbs(x = c(120.3, 121.1, 124.8), eta = 1) #> [1] 0.68472611 0.30766727 0.00760662  # Using a CV loss (lower is better) with moderate sharpness set.seed(1) losses <- c(A = 0.83, B = 0.81, C = 0.92) calc_model_weights_gibbs(losses, eta = 3, verbose = TRUE) #> Gibbs weights with eta = 3 #> Input range: min = 0.81, max = 0.92, spread = 0.11 #> Stabilization: subtract min(x) = 0.81 before exponentiation. #> Entropy = 1.0890; effective number of models = 2.971 #>         A         B         C  #> 0.3539552 0.3758426 0.2702022   # Eta extremes calc_model_weights_gibbs(c(1, 2, 5), eta = 0)      # uniform #> [1] 0.3333333 0.3333333 0.3333333 calc_model_weights_gibbs(c(1, 2, 5), eta = Inf)    # hard selection #> [1] 1 0 0

title: Calibrate Daily Suitability on the Logit Scale (vector; optional causal EWMA + time offset + NA filling) — calc_psi_star
text: Transforms daily suitability series \(\psi_t \[0,1]\) logit-scale affine calibration, optional causal EWMA smoothing, optional time offset \(k\). missing values introduced shift (pre-existing) filled using either LOCF (default) linear interpolation.

title: Calibrate Daily Suitability on the Logit Scale (vector; optional causal EWMA + time offset + NA filling) — calc_psi_star
code: calc_psi_star(   psi,   a = 1,   b = 0,   z = 1,   k = 0,   eps = 1e-06,   fill_method = c("locf", "linear"),   warn_k_rounding = TRUE )

title: Calibrate Daily Suitability on the Logit Scale (vector; optional causal EWMA + time offset + NA filling) — calc_psi_star
text: psi Numeric vector \([0,1]\) (daily suitability \(\psi_t\)). Numeric (>0). Shape/gain logit scale (\(>1\) sharpens; \(<1\) flattens). b Numeric. Scale/offset logit scale (baseline /). z Numeric \((0,1]\) controlling causal EWMA smoothing; 1 = smoothing. k Numeric integer; time offset days. Positive = forward/right; negative = backward/left. Non-integers rounded nearest integer. eps Small positive number clip psi away {0,1} qlogis. fill_method Character; one c("locf","linear") controlling NA gaps filled. warn_k_rounding Logical; TRUE (default), warns k rounded nearest integer. Set FALSE suppress warning.

title: Calibrate Daily Suitability on the Logit Scale (vector; optional causal EWMA + time offset + NA filling) — calc_psi_star
text: Numeric vector \(\tilde\psi^{\star}_t\) (length psi): calibrated, offset, NA-filled, smoothed z<1.

title: Calibrate Daily Suitability on the Logit Scale (vector; optional causal EWMA + time offset + NA filling) — calc_psi_star
text: Offset (index shift, days): \(\psi^{(k)}_t = \psi_{t-k}\). Positive \(k\) shifts series forward/right (delay); negative \(k\) shifts backward/left. Non-integer \(k\) rounded nearest integer. Calibration (odds-space power & scale): $$\psi_t^{\star}=\sigma\!\big(\cdot \mathrm{logit}(\tilde\psi_t)+b\big),\quad \tilde\psi_t=\min\{\max(\psi^{(k)}_t,\varepsilon),\,1-\varepsilon\}$$ \(\text{odds}^\star_t = e^{b}\,(\text{odds}_t)^{}\), \; \(\text{odds}_t=\tilde\psi_t/(1-\tilde\psi_t)\). Optional causal smoothing (look-ahead): $$\tilde\psi^{\star}_t = z\,\psi^{\star}_t + (1-z)\,\tilde\psi^{\star}_{t-1},\quad z\(0,1],\ \tilde\psi^{\star}_1=\psi^{\star}_1.$$ NA filling (calibration, smoothing): Choose via fill_method: "locf" — forward fill (LOCF) backward pass leading gaps. "linear" — linear interpolation interior gaps constant edge extension. values NA shifting, function falls back constant baseline \(\sigma(b)\).

title: Calibrate Daily Suitability on the Logit Scale (vector; optional causal EWMA + time offset + NA filling) — calc_psi_star
code: # Basic usage with integer k (no warnings) psi <- c(0.1, 0.3, 0.7, 0.9, 0.5) result1 <- calc_psi_star(psi, a = 1.2, b = 0.5, k = 2)  # Non-integer k with warning (default behavior) result2 <- calc_psi_star(psi, a = 1.2, b = 0.5, k = 2.744) #> Warning: `k` (2.744) is not an integer; rounding to 3.  # Non-integer k without warning result3 <- calc_psi_star(psi, a = 1.2, b = 0.5, k = 2.744, warn_k_rounding = FALSE)  # With EWMA smoothing result4 <- calc_psi_star(psi, a = 1.2, b = 0.5, k = 2, z = 0.8)

title: Calculate spatial importation hazard — calc_spatial_hazard
text: Computes daily probability least one new infection introduced destination location j day t using gravity‐style coupling infectious prevalence across locations.  formulation adapted Bjørnstad & Grenfell (2008) () folds gravity weights directly prevalence term instead using separate coupling constant, (ii) allows ‑reporting location‐specific parameter tau. Concretely, time t destination j hazard $$\mathcal H_{jt} \,=\, \frac{\beta_{jt}\,S^{\*}_{jt}        \bigl[1-\exp\{-(S^{\*}_{jt}/N_{jt})\,\bar y_{jt}\}\bigr]}       {1+\beta_{jt}\,S^{\*}_{jt}},$$ \(S^{\*}_{jt} = (1-\tau_j)\bigl(S_{jt}+V^{\mathrm{sus}}_{1,jt}+V^{\mathrm{sus}}_{2,jt}\bigr)\) locally susceptible pool (adjusted ‑reporting tau<1). \(\bar y_{jt}\) gravity‑weighted prevalence infection entire metapopulation (including destination): $$\bar y_{jt} = \frac{ (1-\tau_j)(I_{1,jt}+I_{2,jt}) +         \sum_{\neq j}\tau_i\,\pi_{ij}\,(I_{1,}+I_{2,}) }        {\sum_{k} N_{kt} }.$$ Note: specification differs two‑stage form original paper used later work mobility matrix carries full coupling strength.

title: Calculate spatial importation hazard — calc_spatial_hazard

Reporting proportion \(\tau_j \(0,1]\) location.  value 1 means perfect reporting; smaller values ‑weight local susceptibles ‑weight non‑local infections. pie Numeric J × J matrix gravity‑model mobility weights \(\pi_{ij}\) origin destination j.  Diagonal entries ignored. N Numeric T × J matrix total population \(N_{jt}\). S Numeric T × J matrix fully susceptible individuals \(S_{jt}\). V1_sus Numeric T × J matrix vaccine‑derived susceptibles one dose. V2_sus Numeric T × J matrix vaccine‑derived susceptibles two doses. I1 Numeric T × J matrix symptomatic infectives. I2 Numeric T × J matrix asymptomatic infectives. time_names Optional character vector length T output row names. location_names Optional character vector length J output column names.

title: Calculate spatial importation hazard — calc_spatial_hazard
text: Bjørnstad, O. N., & Grenfell, B. T. (2008). Hazards, spatial transmission timing outbreaks epidemic metapopulations. Journal Theoretical Ecology, 1, 145‑153.

title: Calculate spatial importation hazard — calc_spatial_hazard
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(123) T_steps <- 10; J <- 5 beta <- matrix(runif(T_steps * J), nrow = T_steps, ncol = J) tau <- runif(J) pie <- matrix(runif(J^2), J, J); diag(pie) <- 0 N <- matrix(sample(200:400, T_steps * J, replace = TRUE), nrow = T_steps, ncol = J) S <- matrix(sample(100:200, T_steps * J, replace = TRUE), nrow = T_steps, ncol = J) V1_sus <- matrix(sample(0:50, T_steps * J, replace = TRUE), nrow = T_steps, ncol = J) V2_sus <- matrix(sample(0:50, T_steps * J, replace = TRUE), nrow = T_steps, ncol = J) I1 <- matrix(sample(0:10, T_steps * J, replace = TRUE), nrow = T_steps, ncol = J) I2 <- matrix(sample(0:10, T_steps * J, replace = TRUE), nrow = T_steps, ncol = J)  H <- calc_spatial_hazard(beta, tau, pie, N, S, V1_sus, V2_sus, I1, I2,                          time_names = paste0("day_", 1:T_steps),                          location_names = paste0("loc_", 1:J))  plot_spatial_hazard(H) } # }

title: Per-country logit-scale bias-correction of suitability (psi) predictions — calibrate_psi_predictions
text: pred_df Data frame predictions least iso_code, date, pred_col. obs_df Data frame observed targets iso_code, date, obs_col (\([0,1]\)). fit_date_stop Date (coercible); fit uses date <= fit_date_stop. pred_col Prediction column correct (default "pred_smooth"). obs_col Observed-target column (default "transmission_intensity"). out_col Name corrected output column (default "pred_bias_corrected"; renamed v0.33 "pred_calibrated"). min_train Minimum outbreak (non-zero observed) weeks per country fit; otherwise identity (default 8). eps Clamp applied predictions observations qlogis keep logit transform finite (default 1e-6). slope_range Length-2 numeric c(lo, hi); per-country logit slope clamped range applied (default c(0.25, 4)). offset_range Length-2 numeric c(lo, hi); per-country logit offset clamped range (default c(-4, 4)). amp_range Length-2 numeric c(lo, hi); corrected series' logit-scale sd constrained within multiple input prediction's logit-scale sd (default c(0.5, 2)), shrinking affine toward identity violated. min_pred_sd Minimum logit-pred standard deviation (outbreak weeks) country eligible fit; , identity (default 0.05).

title: Per-country logit-scale bias-correction of suitability (psi) predictions — calibrate_psi_predictions
text: fit logit(obs) ~ logit(pred) (v0.34; v0.33 method raw-scale lm(obs ~ pred) hard \([0,1]\) clip). Two changes matter: Outbreak-weeks-fit. Zero observed weeks carry level information scale/offset correction make logit(obs = 0) degenerate zero-week majority, fit uses weeks date <= fit_date_stop obs > 0. Logit-scale, monotone, hard clip. fit applied weeks mapped back plogis, monotone bounded \((0,1)\) construction — low predictions map near 0 without hard-\([0,1]\)-clip truncation distorted psi_bar-relative deviation feeding LASER. country fewer min_train outbreak weeks (includes zero-history countries, none) logit-pred variance falls back identity transform (output = input prediction) warning. defined behavior low-incidence / zero-history countries: psi uncorrected (region-FiLM-modulated) model output.

title: Per-country logit-scale bias-correction of suitability (psi) predictions — calibrate_psi_predictions
text: unregularized, unbounded per-country lm corrupts degenerate / low-signal countries: near-flat logit-pred predictor yields wild slope either collapses country's psi constant blows amplitude many-fold (prediction rank-deficient fit warning). prevent , per-country fit screened affine guarded: Degeneracy screen. Fits rank-deficient, -low logit-pred standard deviation (min_pred_sd), distinct logit-pred values, non-finite / explosive slope fall back identity transform. Bounded affine. estimated slope clamped slope_range offset offset_range (logit scale) applied, country's correction can steeper / offset plausible scale/level fix. Amplitude clamp. corrected series' logit-scale standard deviation constrained within amp_range times input prediction's logit-scale standard deviation (shrinking affine toward identity -collapse -inflate amplitude), country's psi flattened constant inflated beyond sane range. Well-behaved countries (ample outbreak weeks, real logit-pred variance, slope/offset/amplitude inside guard ranges) unaffected — guard -op .

title: Check for overdispersion in a count time series — check_overdispersion
text: y numeric vector non-negative integer counts (e.g., cases deaths). date vector class Date corresponding y. Required seasonality = TRUE. seasonality Logical; TRUE, also fits GLM 2-harmonic seasonal terms. Default = FALSE. period Integer; seasonal period (default = 365 daily data). print Logical; TRUE, prints summary results. Default = TRUE.

title: Check for overdispersion in a count time series — check_overdispersion
text: list dispersion metrics: dispersion_ratio Variance--mean ratio pearson_dispersion_intercept Pearson statistic intercept-model pearson_dispersion_seasonal Pearson statistic seasonal model (applicable) overdispersed_intercept Logical; TRUE intercept model dispersion > 1.5 overdispersed_seasonal Logical; TRUE seasonal model dispersion > 1.5 (applicable) n_obs Number non-missing observations

what: Amplitude guard for post-correction suitability (psi) — check_psi_amplitude
title: Amplitude guard for post-correction suitability (psi) — check_psi_amplitude
text: Lightweight, non-fatal sanity check per-country amplitude produced suitability series psi. Implements long-referenced (previously unwritten) "amplitude guard": flags countries whose post-correction psi amplitude collapsed toward constant inflated implausibly relative input model prediction. distortions introduced degenerate per-country bias-correction fit (see calibrate_psi_predictions) weighting -weights outbreak (non-zero) class.

title: Amplitude guard for post-correction suitability (psi) — check_psi_amplitude
code: check_psi_amplitude(   psi_df,   psi_col = "psi",   pred_col = "pred_smooth",   amp_range = c(0.5, 2),   collapse_abs = 0.02,   eps = 1e-06,   warn = TRUE )

title: Amplitude guard for post-correction suitability (psi) — check_psi_amplitude
text: psi_df Data frame least iso_code psi_col. psi_col Name produced suitability column (default "psi"). pred_col Optional name pre-correction prediction column used reference amplitude (default "pred_smooth"); ignored absent. amp_range Length-2 numeric c(lo, hi); country flagged collapsed logit-sd ratio reference lo, inflated hi (default c(0.5, 2)). collapse_abs Absolute logit-sd floor; country flagged collapsed regardless reference (default 0.02). eps Clamp applied qlogis (default 1e-6). warn Logical; emit warning() country flagged (default TRUE).

title: Amplitude guard for post-correction suitability (psi) — check_psi_amplitude
text: Invisibly, per-country data frame columns iso_code, n, sd_psi_logit, sd_ref_logit, amp_ratio, collapsed, inflated, flag ("ok", "collapsed", "inflated").

title: Amplitude guard for post-correction suitability (psi) — check_psi_amplitude
text: check intentionally cheap never errors: emits single warning() listing flagged countries () returns per-country diagnostic data frame callers can log / inspect . Amplitude measured standard deviation series logit scale (scale bias correction operates), using eps clamp keep qlogis finite. country two flags computed: collapsed — corrected logit-sd collapse_abs (near-constant absolute terms) amp_range[1] times input prediction's logit-sd (lost shape relative model). inflated — corrected logit-sd exceeds amp_range[2] times input prediction's logit-sd (amplitude blown relative model). pred_col supplied present, relative checks use input prediction reference amplitude; otherwise absolute collapse check applies.

title: Combine WHO and GTFCC Vaccination Data — combine_vaccination_data
text: PATHS list containing file paths input output data. list include: MODEL_INPUT: Path directory containing processed vaccination data files combined data saved. date_tolerance Number days tolerance matching campaign dates sources (default: 60 days) dose_tolerance Proportion tolerance matching doses sources (default: 0.2 = 20%)

title: Combine WHO and GTFCC Vaccination Data — combine_vaccination_data
text: function saves combined vaccination data CSV file directory specified PATHS$MODEL_INPUT. also returns combined data data frame use R.

title: Combine WHO and GTFCC Vaccination Data — combine_vaccination_data
text: function performs intelligent campaign matching using following approach: Load Processed Data: - Reads GTFCC processed vaccination data files Intelligent Matching: - Matches campaigns country, approximate date (within tolerance), dose similarity - Uses multiple passes different tolerance levels maximize accurate matching - Identifies truly unique campaigns present GTFCC Data Combination: - Prioritizes GTFCC data (marked source = "GTFCC") - Adds unique campaigns (marked source = "") - Includes campaigns present (marked source = "GTFCC_WHO_matched") Quality Assurance: - Validates data structure matches downstream requirements - Ensures required columns present - Maintains date ID formatting standards

title: Combine WHO and GTFCC Vaccination Data — combine_vaccination_data
code: # Example usage PATHS <- get_paths() #> Error in get_paths(): Cannot find root_directory combined_data <- combine_vaccination_data(PATHS) #> ========================================== #> Combining WHO and GTFCC Vaccination Data #> ========================================== #> Loading processed vaccination data... #> Error: object 'PATHS' not found  # With custom tolerance settings combined_data <- combine_vaccination_data(PATHS, date_tolerance = 30, dose_tolerance = 0.1) #> ========================================== #> Combining WHO and GTFCC Vaccination Data #> ========================================== #> Loading processed vaccination data... #> Error: object 'PATHS' not found

what: Compile Environmental Suitability Data for Cholera Transmission — compile_suitability_data
title: Compile Environmental Suitability Data for Cholera Transmission — compile_suitability_data
text: Compiles multiple processed data sources (climate, ENSO, cholera surveillance, demographics, WASH) single feature-engineered training dataset environmental suitability modeling. Computes derived covariates including rolling windows, anomalies, climate / WASH interaction terms.

title: Compile Environmental Suitability Data for Cholera Transmission — compile_suitability_data
code: compile_suitability_data(   PATHS,   cutoff,   use_epidemic_peaks = FALSE,   date_start = "2000-01-01",   date_stop = NULL,   forecast_mode = TRUE,   forecast_horizon = 3,   include_lags = FALSE,   include_flood_prob = TRUE,   backfill_case_gaps = TRUE,   backfill_max_weeks = 2L,   backfill_method = "linear" )

title: Compile Environmental Suitability Data for Cholera Transmission — compile_suitability_data
text: PATHS list containing paths data stored. Typically generated get_paths() function include: DATA_CLIMATE: Path directory processed climate data stored. DATA_ENSO: Path directory ENSO data stored. DATA_CHOLERA_WEEKLY: Path directory containing processed combined weekly cholera cases data (+JHU+SUPP sources). cutoff Numeric threshold case counts. Used use_epidemic_peaks = FALSE. Weeks cases >= cutoff considered outbreak periods environmental suitability calculation. Default behavior uses value create binary indicator. use_epidemic_peaks Logical. TRUE, uses epidemic peak periods est_epidemic_peaks() define environmental suitability using exact peak_start peak_stop dates. Provides deterministic outbreak detection without modifications lead-periods. Default FALSE backwards compatibility. date_start Start data window. Accepts year (e.g. 2000) date string (e.g. "2000-01-01"); year used filtering. Default "2000-01-01". caps far back panel extends (important AI source supplies pre-2000 reconstructions). Pass NULL auto-detect start case data (one year prior earliest case observation). date_stop End data window (year date string). NULL (default), auto-detected latest raw ENSO data availability. forecast_mode Logical. TRUE, includes variables can support forecasting specified horizon. (v0.30.26 case-dependent epidemic memory spatial-import blocks removed entirely, flag now affects final summary-message text climate-anomaly leading-edge behavior.) Default TRUE. forecast_horizon Numeric. Forecasting horizon months. Variables dependent surveillance data beyond horizon excluded forecast mode. Default 3 months. include_lags Logical. TRUE, includes time-lagged climate variables epidemiologically-informed lag periods specific variable. Default FALSE. include_flood_prob Logical. TRUE (default), imputes continuous emdat_flood_prob column every (iso_code, year, week) row via binomial GAM trained observed emdat_flood_active events (see impute_flood_probability), adds four rolling-window aggregates (emdat_flood_prob_4w_max, emdat_flood_prob_12w_max, emdat_flood_prob_12w_sum, emdat_flood_prob_anom). probability covariates populated historical training forecast windows, downstream LSTM consumes feature definition regimes. backfill_case_gaps Logical (default TRUE). Linearly interpolate short interior gaps weekly case series (e.g. holiday non-reporting) suitability target built, via backfill_weekly_case_gaps, missing weeks inside active outbreak stamped false zeros downstream NA->0 sanitiser. backfill_max_weeks Integer (default 2L). Maximum interior gap length (weeks) interpolate; longer gaps left zero-fill. backfill_method Interpolation method passed backfill_weekly_case_gaps (default "linear").

title: Compile Environmental Suitability Data for Cholera Transmission — compile_suitability_data
text: function processes data merges climate, ENSO, cholera cases data single dataset. creates cases_binary column indicating environmental suitability based case patterns using sophisticated temporal logic. processed dataset saved CSV file.

title: Compile Environmental Suitability Data for Cholera Transmission — compile_suitability_data
text: v0.30.26 vaccination, epidemic-memory (cases-derived), spatial-import (mobility-network) blocks longer computed. already excluded est_suitability()'s LSTM covariate list – vaccination signal downstream intervention (deployed response cholera, anticipation environmental suitability), case-derived epidemic-memory features target leakage model whose response case-derived binary. function performs following key steps: Loads merges climate data (temperature, precipitation, etc.) multiple countries Loads ENSO IOD climate index data Loads combined weekly cholera case surveillance data , JHU, supplemental sources Merges datasets country, year, week Creates environmental suitability indicator (cases_binary) using either epidemic peak-based threshold-based methods Saves complete merged dataset use environmental suitability modeling Environmental Suitability Methods: Epidemic Peak-based (use_epidemic_peaks = TRUE): Uses deterministic outbreak detection est_epidemic_peaks() using exact peak_start peak_stop dates without modifications Threshold-based (use_epidemic_peaks = FALSE): Uses simple case count cutoffs temporal lead-logic epidemic peak-based method provides deterministic, epidemiologically meaningful outbreak periods recommended consistent environmental suitability modeling.

title: Default LASER Configuration — config_default

title: Default LASER Configuration — config_default
code: config_default

title: Default LASER Configuration — config_default
text: named list built data-raw/make_config_default.R script persisted data/config_default.rda. Elements include: Metadata – metadata$version, metadata$date, metadata$description provenance tracking; Scalars – biological constants (phi_1, gamma_1, epsilon, …); Vectors – initial populations (S_j_initial, I_j_initial, …), initial proportions (optional: prop_S_initial, prop_I_initial, …), coordinates (longitude, latitude), mobility coefficients, etc.; Matrices – day--day series human--human transmission (b_jt), environmental suitability (psi_jt), birth/death rates; Character paths – locations required input data (see ).

title: Default LASER Configuration — config_default
text: Unlike lightweight simulation configs exported make_simulation_epidemic_LASER_config_files.R make_simulation_endemic_LASER_config_files.R, default configuration assumes presence external data files arranged canonical MOSAIC directories: MODEL_INPUT Directory containing baseline CSV inputs (population, rainfall, vaccination, WASH, etc.). DATA_WHO_DAILY Directory processed daily cholera line-list aggregated case/death counts. ENV_RASTERS (Optional) Path geotiff rasters used build spatial priors environmental suitability. object intended full-scale analyses therefore large (tens megabytes) self-contained – error file paths references exist user's machine.  tutorials automated tests, consider using config_simulation_epidemic config_simulation_endemic, run without external dependencies. Note Initial Condition Formats: configuration includes count (*_j_initial) proportion (prop_*_initial) representations initial conditions. count fields required LASER model simulation, proportion fields optional provided statistical analysis convenience. formats automatically maintained sync parameter sampling operations.

text: single-row data.frame : Scalar parameters become single columns original names Vector parameters multiple locations become columns named "parameter_location" (e.g., "beta_j0_env_ETH", "beta_j0_env_KEN") Node-level parameters (matching population nodes) expanded node indices (e.g., "N_1", "N_2", etc.)

title: Convert Config to DataFrame — convert_config_to_dataframe
text: function intelligently handles different parameter types: Character/numeric scalars: Direct conversion columns Named vectors/lists: Expanded location-specific columns Unnamed vectors matching node count: Expanded node-indexed columns Date fields: Converted character representation NULL values: Skipped Location-specific parameters identified names match ISO3 country codes. Node-level parameters identified length matching number population nodes (N parameter).

title: Convert Config to Numeric Vector for Matrix Storage — convert_config_to_matrix
text: function efficient convert_config_to_dataframe() goal store parameters numeric matrix, avoids overhead creating intermediate data.frame. processes parameters directly numeric vector maintaining naming convention. function handles: Scalar parameters: Direct numeric conversion Location-specific vectors: Expanded location suffixes Node-level parameters: Expanded numeric indices Logical values: Converted 0/1 NULL values: Skipped Non-numeric values: Converted NA warning

title: Convert Config to Numeric Vector for Matrix Storage — convert_config_to_matrix
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Convert sampled config for matrix storage config_sampled <- sample_parameters(PATHS, seed = 123) vec_params <- convert_config_to_matrix(config_sampled)  # Use in calibration workflow n_sim <- 100 n_params <- length(vec_params) results_matrix <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n_sim, ncol = 4 + n_params) results_matrix[1, 5:ncol(results_matrix)] <- vec_params  # Compare with dataframe approach (slower) df_params <- convert_config_to_dataframe(config_sampled) vec_params_slow <- as.numeric(df_params) all.equal(vec_params, vec_params_slow)  # Should be TRUE } # }

text: function reconstructs config object : Starting config_base template Parsing parameter names identify structure (e.g., "beta_j0_hum_ETH") Checking sampling_flags determine parameters updated Updating parameters originally sampled (sampling_flags provided) Handling scalar vector parameters appropriately IMPORTANT: sampling_flags parameter addresses critical bug parameters intended fixed (sample_* = FALSE) overwritten matrix reconstruction, making appear stochastic downstream analyses. Parameter naming conventions expected: Scalar parameters: "param_name" (e.g., "phi_1", "gamma_2") Location-specific: "param_name_LOCATION" (e.g., "beta_j0_hum_ETH") Indexed parameters: "param_name_1", "param_name_2", etc.

title: Convert Matrix Row to Config Object — convert_matrix_to_config
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Legacy usage (updates all parameters) config_orig <- sample_parameters(PATHS, seed = 123) param_vec <- convert_config_to_matrix(config_orig) config_reconstructed <- convert_matrix_to_config(param_vec, config_orig)  # Preserving sampling intent (recommended) sampling_flags <- list(alpha_1 = FALSE, phi_1 = TRUE, beta_j0_hum = TRUE) config_best <- convert_matrix_to_config(param_row, config_base,                                         sampling_flags = sampling_flags)  # Use with calibration results param_row <- results[best_idx, col_params] names(param_row) <- param_names config_best <- convert_matrix_to_config(param_row, config_base,                                        sampling_flags = original_sampling_flags) } # }

title: Infer posterior fitting family from a uniform prior's bounds — .infer_posterior_family_uniform
text: v0.28.1: non-Beta branch returns "truncnorm" instead "lognormal" / "normal". Truncnorm preserves uniform's [min, max] support posterior fit across subsequent calibration stages (family-match guard update_priors_from_posteriors.R). Previously, uniform priors non-[0,1] supports fit unbounded Lognormal Normal, silently erasing prior's bounds stage-2+ posteriors. Bound plumbing: calc_model_posterior_distributions.R falls back min/max truncnorm /b fields absent prior entry, changes needed uniform priors .

what: Count active Dask workers via Python (snapshot-staleness-safe) — .mosaic_count_dask_workers
title: Count active Dask workers via Python (snapshot-staleness-safe) — .mosaic_count_dask_workers
text: client$scheduler_info()$workers relied count Dask workers STALE, lagging client-side snapshot workers dict: workers join time (Coiled spin-case – request 800, scale gradually) workers dict reflect live count reports -low number (e.g. 5 hundreds actually running). verified directly local LocalCluster: scaling 1 6 workers left len(scheduler_info()['workers']) reporting 5 every fresh source (client.nthreads(), scheduler_info()['n_workers'], scheduler's len(s.workers)) correctly reported 6. (earlier comment attributed bug reticulate-conversion "per-worker field count 5"; explanation factually wrong – STATIC cluster len(scheduler_info()$workers) returns correct count. real cause snapshot staleness workers dict workers join.)

title: Count active Dask workers via Python (snapshot-staleness-safe) — .mosaic_count_dask_workers
text: helper reads FRESH source server-side. PRIMARY: len(client.nthreads()) – live scheduler RPC stable public API returns one entry per worker (chosen client.run_on_scheduler(lambda s: len(s.workers)) needs lambda serialization, observed fail environments, lighter call). FALLBACK 1: scheduler_info()['n_workers'] present (also fresh, counts threads threads-per-worker > 1). FALLBACK 2: len(scheduler_info().get('workers', {})) (stale last-resort original behavior). tiny Python helper returns plain int, py_run_string(..., convert = TRUE) reliably converts R integer; -1 signals Python-side failure R tryCatch maps NA_integer_.

what: Gather Dask futures while emitting periodic progress lines — .mosaic_gather_with_heartbeat
title: Gather Dask futures while emitting periodic progress lines — .mosaic_gather_with_heartbeat
text: Wraps client$gather(futures) polling loop wakes every interval_sec seconds, counts completed vs pending futures via single round-trip Python helper, emits one structured [PROGRESS] log line per heartbeat. Eliminates silent--gather window otherwise leads tailing operator (human AI) wrongly conclude pipeline hung.

title: Gather Dask futures while emitting periodic progress lines — .mosaic_gather_with_heartbeat
code: .mosaic_gather_with_heartbeat(   client,   futures,   log_fn = log_msg,   phase = "gather",   interval_sec = 30L )

title: Gather Dask futures while emitting periodic progress lines — .mosaic_gather_with_heartbeat
text: client reticulated dask.distributed.Client object. futures List Dask future objects. Empty list returns empty list. log_fn Logging function compatible log_msg(msg, ...). phase Short slug phase= field progress line (e.g. "calibration_batch", "postca_ensemble"). interval_sec Heartbeat interval. Defaults 30 s – long enough log spammed fast gathers, short enough hung gather detected within minute.

title: Gather Dask futures while emitting periodic progress lines — .mosaic_gather_with_heartbeat
text: list returned client$gather(futures).

title: Gather Dask futures while emitting periodic progress lines — .mosaic_gather_with_heartbeat
text: Note: earlier version used dask.distributed.wait(timeout=...) wake completion--timeout. API raises TimeoutError timeout (return DoneAndNotDoneFutures docs suggest first read), aborted gather first heartbeat. now poll future.status Python single batched call (cheap – status mirrored locally scheduler push updates, round-trip per future) sleep iterations. gather error helper swallow – lets caller's tryCatch handle diagnostics (first-future status inspection).

code: epidemic_peaks

title: Cholera Epidemic Peaks Data — epidemic_peaks
text: data frame 6 variables: iso_code ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code peak_start Start date epidemic period (Date) peak_date Date peak incidence (Date) peak_stop End date epidemic period (Date) reported_cases Number reported cholera cases peak (numeric) outbreak_interval_days Number days since previous peak country (numeric)

title: Cholera Epidemic Peaks Data — epidemic_peaks
text: Generated cholera surveillance data using est_epidemic_peaks() function combined daily surveillance data sources.

title: Cholera Epidemic Peaks Data — epidemic_peaks
text: Scope: dataset full historical detection record surveillance time series (2010+) pre-trimmed particular simulation window. Consumers score peaks specific config window (e.g. calc_model_likelihood(), Python likelihood port, LASER config builders) must filter [date_start, date_stop] first – otherwise .min(abs(date_seq - peak_date)) silently snaps --window peaks t=1 t=N biases peak-shape likelihood terms. internal helper MOSAIC:::.filter_epidemic_peaks() canonical filter applied build time inside make_config_default.R, runtime inside run_MOSAIC()'s Dask injector, defensively inside calc_model_likelihood(). Epidemic peaks identified using following methodology: Time series smoothing 28-day running mean window Local maxima detection 10-day comparison windows Prominence-based filtering (minimum 8\ countries; country-specific overrides ) Minimum peak height threshold 3 smoothed cases (default) Minimum 75-day separation consecutive peaks Peak boundaries defined incidence drops 1/3 peak height Country-specific adjustments applied : Niger (NER): Lower prominence threshold (1.5\ Cameroon (CMR): Adjusted threshold (4\ Ethiopia (ETH): Lower threshold (3\ Manual corrections applied known issues including: Ethiopia 2024: February peak corrected March (sustained outbreak) DRC 2023: Added January peak (filtered due proximity) Nigeria 2024: Added October peak Kenya 2022-2023: Added December 2022, removed June 2023 minor peak Mozambique: Added February 2024 March 2025 peaks Somalia: Added major April 2017 peak 2024-2025 peaks Zambia: Added January 2018 peak Tanzania: Added January 2017 peak

title: Cholera Epidemic Peaks Data — epidemic_peaks
code: data(epidemic_peaks) head(epidemic_peaks) #>     iso_code peak_start  peak_date  peak_stop reported_cases #> 118      AGO 2018-01-01 2018-01-04 2018-01-31             24 #> 1        AGO 2025-03-29 2025-04-28 2025-05-28            303 #> 2        AGO 2025-09-25 2025-10-13 2025-11-05            149 #> 3        AGO 2026-04-06 2026-04-20 2026-05-03            106 #> 120      BDI 2016-08-01 2016-08-21 2016-10-31              9 #> 119      BDI 2017-10-15 2017-10-17 2017-10-19              6 #>     outbreak_interval_days #> 118                     30 #> 1                       60 #> 2                       41 #> 3                       27 #> 120                     91 #> 119                      4  # Countries with epidemic data unique(epidemic_peaks$iso_code) #>  [1] "AGO" "BDI" "BEN" "CAF" "CIV" "CMR" "COD" "COG" "ETH" "GHA" "GIN" "KEN" #> [13] "LBR" "MOZ" "MWI" "NER" "NGA" "RWA" "SDN" "SLE" "SOM" "SSD" "TCD" "TGO" #> [25] "TZA" "UGA" "ZMB" "ZWE"  # Recent peaks (2024-2025) recent_peaks <- epidemic_peaks[epidemic_peaks$peak_date >= as.Date("2024-01-01"), ] table(recent_peaks$iso_code) #>  #> AGO BDI COD COG ETH KEN MOZ NGA RWA SDN SOM SSD TGO TZA UGA ZMB ZWE  #>   3   4   2   1   3   3   3   2   1   4   2   2   1   3   3   2   2   # Peak duration calculation epidemic_peaks$duration <- as.numeric(   epidemic_peaks$peak_stop - epidemic_peaks$peak_start ) summary(epidemic_peaks$duration) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>    2.00   16.00   28.00   30.79   47.00  105.00

text: PATHS List paths get_paths(). priors Prior distributions parameters (e.g., priors_default). config Configuration object location codes date_start. Must include config$location_name config$date_start. n_samples Number Monte Carlo samples (default 1000). t0 Target date estimation (default config$date_start). lookback_days Days surveillance data use (default 21). verbose Print progress messages (default TRUE). parallel Enable parallel processing Monte Carlo sampling n_samples >= 100 (default FALSE). Uses parallel::mclapply() available cores. Note: supported Windows. variance_inflation Factor create variance Beta distributions (default 2). Sets ci_lower = mean_val / variance_inflation ci_upper = mean_val * variance_inflation. Values > 1 create wider distributions around sample mean. > 1.1 meaningful variance.

title: Estimate Initial S Compartment from Other Compartment Priors — est_initial_S

code: est_initial_S(   PATHS,   priors,   config,   n_samples = 1000,   t0 = NULL,   variance_inflation = 0,   verbose = TRUE,   min_S_proportion = 0.01 )

title: Estimate Initial S Compartment from Other Compartment Priors — est_initial_S
text: PATHS List containing paths data directories (compatibility) priors Prior distributions object containing estimated priors V1, V2, E, , R config Configuration object containing location codes n_samples Integer, number Monte Carlo samples uncertainty quantification (default 1000) t0 Date object, target date estimation (default NULL, used metadata) variance_inflation Numeric factor inflate variance fitted Beta distributions (default 1 = inflation). Values > 1 increase uncertainty preserving mean. example, 2 doubles variance. verbose Logical, whether print progress messages (default TRUE) min_S_proportion Numeric, minimum allowed S proportion prevent negative values (default 0.01 = 1%)

title: Estimate Initial S Compartment from Other Compartment Priors — est_initial_S
text: List priors_default-compatible structure containing: metadata Information estimation run including summary statistics parameters_location Parameter hierarchy matching priors_default: prop_S_initial: Beta distribution parameters S/N location parameter contains $parameters$location$ISO_CODE shape1 shape2

title: Estimate Initial S Compartment from Other Compartment Priors — est_initial_S
text: function implements constrained residual approach: Method Overview: Sample estimated Beta priors V1, V2, E, , R compartments Calculate S constrained residual: S = 1 - (V1 + V2 + E + + R) Apply constraints: ensure S ≥ min_S_proportion handle -allocation Fit Beta distribution S samples using CI expansion method Return priors-compatible structure metadata Constraint Handling: compartments sum > (1 - min_S_proportion), proportionally scale Ensures S always ≥ min_S_proportion biological realism Maintains mathematical consistency: compartments sum 1 Fallback Behavior: estimated priors unavailable compartment, uses default prior parameters Locations without estimated compartments use independent S prior (Beta(30, 7.5)) Graceful degradation ensures function always returns valid results Uncertainty Propagation: S uncertainty incorporates uncertainty compartments Variance inflation applied using CI expansion method consistent est_initial_* functions Countries high vaccination/immunity naturally get lower certain S estimates

title: Estimate Initial S Compartment from Other Compartment Priors — est_initial_S
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # After running est_initial_E_I, est_initial_R PATHS <- get_paths() priors_updated <- priors_default  # With updated V1,V2,E,I,R compartments  initial_S <- est_initial_S(   PATHS = PATHS,   priors = priors_updated,   config = config_default,   n_samples = 1000,   variance_inflation = 2  # Double the variance for less constrained priors )  # Access results for a location eth_s_params <- initial_S$parameters_location$prop_S_initial$parameters$location$ETH eth_s_mean <- eth_s_params$metadata$mean } # }

what: Estimate V1/V2 Initial-Condition Beta Priors from OCV Campaign History — est_initial_V1_V2
title: Estimate V1/V2 Initial-Condition Beta Priors from OCV Campaign History — est_initial_V1_V2
text: Builds country-specific Beta priors prop_V1_initial prop_V2_initial reading raw GTFCC OCV request log, classifying doses regimen (single-dose Euvichol-S vs. two-dose Shanchol/Euvichol/Euvichol+), pairing rounds within campaign, applying omega_1 / omega_2 waning administration date simulation start, moment-matching resulting country-level proportions Beta distributions.

title: Estimate V1/V2 Initial-Condition Beta Priors from OCV Campaign History — est_initial_V1_V2
code: est_initial_V1_V2(   PATHS,   config,   date_start = NULL,   cv = 0.4,   omega_1 = NULL,   omega_2 = NULL,   t_lag = 14,   vacc_ceiling_frac = 0.7,   fallback_shape1_V1 = 0.5,   fallback_shape2_V1 = 49.5,   fallback_shape1_V2 = 0.5,   fallback_shape2_V2 = 99.5,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Estimate V1/V2 Initial-Condition Beta Priors from OCV Campaign History — est_initial_V1_V2
text: PATHS list paths (returned get_paths). Used locate ees-cholera-mapping/data/cholera/epicentre/gtfcc/cholera_vacc_requests.csv via PATHS$ROOT. config MOSAIC config list (e.g. config_default). Used location_name, N_j_initial, date_start. date_start Simulation start date (Date character "YYYY-MM-DD"). Defaults config$date_start. campaigns delivered strictly date contribute V1/V2 initial conditions. cv Coefficient variation Beta prior (natural scale). Default 0.40. Tighter values produce narrower priors; looser values permit data-driven posterior movement. omega_1 One-dose waning rate (per day). Defaults natural-scale mean priors_default$parameters_global$omega_1. omega_2 Two-dose waning rate (per day). Defaults natural-scale mean priors_default$parameters_global$omega_2. t_lag Protection onset lag (days). Waning starts event_date + t_lag. Default 14. vacc_ceiling_frac Hard ceiling combined V1+V2 coverage (fraction population). Default 0.70 (v0.28.7; 0.60 v0.28.6). waned dose sum exceeds , V1 V2 scaled proportionally. Rationale 0.70: published OCV campaigns routinely reach 65-80% coverage (Abubakar et al. 2018 DRC 65%; Qadri et al. 2015 BGD 75%; Luquero et al. 2014 Haiti 80%+). lower ceiling -captures real emergency responses. fallback_shape1_V1, fallback_shape2_V1, fallback_shape1_V2, fallback_shape2_V2 Beta shape parameters used fallback country OCV history CSV. Defaults match pre-v0.22.11 uninformative priors (Beta(0.5, 49.5) V1, Beta(0.5, 99.5) V2). verbose Logical; emit per-country diagnostics. Default TRUE.

title: Estimate V1/V2 Initial-Condition Beta Priors from OCV Campaign History — est_initial_V1_V2
text: list nested structure priors_default$parameters_location: $prop_V1_initial$location[[iso]] $prop_V2_initial$location[[iso]] carry list(distribution = "beta", parameters = list(shape1, shape2)).

title: Estimate V1/V2 Initial-Condition Beta Priors from OCV Campaign History — est_initial_V1_V2
text: Biological notes: V1/V2 MOSAIC/LASER model administrative compartments (dose received). LASER's vaccinated.py independently splits initial counts immune (Vkimm) susceptible (Vksus) substates via phi_1/phi_2. function therefore multiply phi_1/phi_2 — double-count effectiveness, bug present pre-v0.22.11 implementation. two-dose regimens, R01 attendees return R02 transition V1\(\\)V2 R02 date. Non-returners (R01_doses \(-\) R02_doses, positive) remain V1 omega_1 waning R01 onward. Single-dose Euvichol-S campaigns contribute V1. Blank/unknown vaccine products treated conservatively two-dose (pre-2022 campaigns two-dose Shanchol). Waning model: single exponential. Real OCV waning biphasic (fast decay months 0-12, slower thereafter; see Xu et al. 2024, Bi et al. 2017). Single exponential biases V1/V2 counts upward roughly 10-20% campaigns 2-3 years pre-date_start relative biphasic model. considered acceptable initial-condition purposes; implement biphasic separately needed. Coverage ceiling: vacc_ceiling_frac = 0.70 reflects observed peak OCV coverage published campaigns: DRC Katanga 65% (Abubakar et al. 2018), Bangladesh 75% (Qadri et al. 2015), Haiti 80%+ (Luquero et al. 2014). lower ceiling -captures real high-coverage emergency responses.

what: Estimate Prior Distribution for Environmental Half-Saturation Constant (kappa) — est_kappa_prior
title: Estimate Prior Distribution for Environmental Half-Saturation Constant (kappa) — est_kappa_prior
text: function compiles meta-analysis published Vibrio cholerae infectious dose (ID50) estimates fits lognormal prior distribution environmental dose-response half-saturation constant \(\kappa\) used MOSAIC environmental force--infection term: $$\lambda_W \propto W / (\kappa + W)$$ \(W\) environmental bacterial concentration \(\kappa\) concentration (CFU) probability infection 50\

title: Estimate Prior Distribution for Environmental Half-Saturation Constant (kappa) — est_kappa_prior
code: est_kappa_prior(PATHS)

title: Estimate Prior Distribution for Environmental Half-Saturation Constant (kappa) — est_kappa_prior
text: PATHS list paths generated get_paths(). Must include: MODEL_INPUT: directory parameter CSVs saved. DOCS_FIGURES: directory summary figure saved.

title: Estimate Prior Distribution for Environmental Half-Saturation Constant (kappa) — est_kappa_prior
text: Invisibly returns list elements: data: source meta-analysis table. fit: fitted lognormal parameters (meanlog, sdlog, median, mode, ci_lower, ci_upper). param_df: long-format parameter data frame (saved disk).

title: Estimate Prior Distribution for Environmental Half-Saturation Constant (kappa) — est_kappa_prior
text: function assembles literature table ID50 estimates spanning direct human-volunteer challenge studies (Hornick 1971, Cash 1974, Levine 1981/1988, Tacket 1999), QMRA Beta-Poisson fits (Haas, Rose & Gerba 1999), expert-review summaries (Nelson et al. 2009, Kaper et al. 1995), values adopted convention cholera modelling literature (Codeco 2001, Hartley et al. 2006). source weighted relevance endemic / buffered exposure context relevant sub-Saharan African transmission settings. lognormal fit weighted maximum-likelihood log10 scale. MOSAIC dose-response follows Codeco (2001) Hartley et al. (2006): infection hazard saturating Michaelis-Menten function environmental concentration half-saturation \(\kappa\). Historical cholera models fixed \(\kappa = 10^6\) cells, value traceable Sack (1971) Hornick et al. (1971) unbuffered water-exposure healthy adults. Modern re-analyses buffered-exposure volunteer studies (NaHCO3 neutralisation, representative typical endemic exposure food contaminated drinking water) consistently support lower central estimate \(\sim 10^5\) CFU, substantial -study heterogeneity. Fitting: source contributes point estimate \(\log_{10} \kappa_i\) weight \(w_i\) reflecting evidentiary quality. Weighted mean standard deviation log10 scale converted lognormal meanlog sdlog. resulting prior reconciled fit_lognormal_from_ci() meanlog consistent weighted mode 2.5th / 97.5th percentiles match empirical spread.

title: Estimate Prior Distribution for Environmental Half-Saturation Constant (kappa) — est_kappa_prior
text: Hornick RB, et al. (1971). Broad Street pump revisited: response volunteers ingested cholera vibrios. Bull N Y Acad Med 47(10):1181-91. Cash RA, et al. (1974). Response man infection Vibrio cholerae. . Clinical, serologic, bacteriologic responses known inoculum. J Infect Dis 129(1):45-52. Levine MM, et al. (1981). Volunteer studies development vaccines cholera enterotoxigenic Escherichia coli: review. : Holme T, et al., eds. Acute Enteric Infections Children. Levine MM, et al. (1988). Safety, immunogenicity, efficacy recombinant live oral cholera vaccines, CVD 103 CVD 103-HgR. Lancet 2(8609):467-70. Tacket CO, et al. (1999). Initial clinical studies CVD 112 V.cholerae O139 live oral vaccine. Infect Immun 67(12):6346-49. Haas CN, Rose JB, Gerba CP (1999). Quantitative Microbial Risk Assessment. Wiley, New York. Codeco CT (2001). Endemic epidemic dynamics cholera: role aquatic reservoir. BMC Infect Dis 1:1. Hartley DM, Morris JG, Smith DL (2006). Hyperinfectivity: critical element ability V. cholerae cause epidemics? PLoS Med 3(1):e7. Kaper JB, Morris JG, Levine MM (1995). Cholera. Clin Microbiol Rev 8(1):48-86. Nelson EJ, Harris JB, Morris JG, Calderwood SB, Camilli (2009). Cholera transmission: host, pathogen bacteriophage dynamic. Nat Rev Microbiol 7(10):693-702. QMRA Wiki: Vibrio cholerae dose-response models. https://qmrawiki.org/experiments/vibrio-cholerae

title: Estimate Prior Distribution for Environmental Half-Saturation Constant (kappa) — est_kappa_prior

title: Estimate Environmental Suitability (psi) for Cholera Transmission — est_suitability
text: PATHS list containing paths data stored. Typically generated get_paths() function include: DATA_CHOLERA_WEEKLY: Path directory containing processed combined weekly cholera cases data (+JHU+SUPP sources). DATA_ELEVATION: Path directory elevation data stored. MODEL_INPUT: Path save processed model inputs outputs. DOCS_FIGURES: Path save generated plots. fit_date_start Date string NULL. Start date model fitting period. NULL, auto-detects first cholera case data. fit_date_stop Date string NULL. End date model fitting period. NULL, auto-detects last date cholera cases complete ENSO data. pred_date_start Date string NULL. Start date prediction period. NULL, uses fit_date_start. pred_date_stop Date string NULL. End date prediction period. NULL, auto-detects last date complete ENSO data. feature_set Named covariate set, shared architectures: "v7.3" (default; 38 screening-informed features, see MINFEAT_V7_3_FEATURE_SET) "default" (full production candidate set). SOLE public, schema-guarded feature selector. Errors "v7.3" feature absent suitability CSV (schema-drift guard). response_var Training target column, shared architectures (default "transmission_intensity", v0.34 default). lstm_v2 path maps internally sandbox "intensity" recipe (log1p(cases)/log1p(cases_99th) TRAIN-cases_99th anchor). Alternatively name pre-computed [0,1] target_* column present suitability CSV (e.g. "target_C_rate_global"). bias_correct Logical (default TRUE), shared architectures. Apply post-hoc per-country affine bias correction (calibrate_psi_predictions), fit training window applied predictions; populates diagnostic pred_bias_corrected column feeds canonical psi column. (Renamed v0.33 calibrate; old name accepted via ... deprecation message.) run_MOSAIC() Bayesian calibration. architecture One "lstm_v2_hierarchical_film" (default, v0.34) "lstm_v1_legacy" (frozen v0.33 path). Validated match.arg. See Architectures section. arch_control NULL (default) named list lstm_v2 hyperparameter overrides. NULL loads checked-fixture inst/fixtures/B4_rolling_cv_spec.yml applies two production defaults (n_seeds = 5, region_map = "snf_k5"). supplied list merged via modifyList (overrides keys provided). Key fields: n_seeds (ensemble size; production 5, fixture 3), region_map (one "snf_k5" (default), "csv", "seasonal_v1", "hydro_v1", "snf_k4"), use_confidence_weight, timesteps, partial_pool_lambda, exclude_covariates (lstm_v2 ablation override), execution knob parallel_seeds (integer, default 1L = serial; model parameter). Ignored legacy path. plot_country_diagnostics Logical (default FALSE). Per-country base-R diagnostic plots smoothing loop (legacy path ). ... Absorbs deprecated v0.33 arguments backward compatibility: calibrate (mapped bias_correct), frozen legacy knobs n_splits, seed_base, fine_tune_epochs, fine_tune_lr, split_method, train_prop, exclude_covariates (ignored deprecation message).

title: Estimate Environmental Suitability (psi) for Cholera Transmission — est_suitability
text: function processes climate cholera case data, fits LSTM model, makes predictions environmental suitability (psi), saves predictions covariate data. also generates plot showing model fit (MAE loss training epochs) saves specified directory.

title: Estimate Environmental Suitability (psi) for Cholera Transmission — est_suitability
text: Common architectures, function: Loads processed climate cholera data. Determines appropriate date ranges fitting prediction based data availability. Validates data completeness within specified date ranges. Scales climate covariates using training-data statistics. Builds temporal LSTM sequences respecting country boundaries temporal gaps. Trains LSTM model, predicts environmental suitability (psi) full prediction period, writes predictions covariate data. training/validation regime differs architecture: default lstm_v2_hierarchical_film uses expanding-window rolling-origin cross-validation (random split) hierarchical-FiLM trunk, frozen lstm_v1_legacy path uses random 60/40 split sequential fine-tuning. See Architectures section full description path.

title: Estimate Environmental Suitability (psi) for Cholera Transmission — est_suitability
text: LSTM model uses climate variables predict cholera suitability. model's predictions saved CSV file plot showing model fit (MAE loss) generated.

title: Estimate Environmental Suitability (psi) for Cholera Transmission — est_suitability
text: lstm_v2_hierarchical_film (default, v0.34) – hierarchical-FiLM LSTM, rolling-origin CV. 3-stack LSTM trunk (128->64->32 units, recurrent dropout) maps weekly covariate window (timesteps=13) shared climate-response latent z. Country/region identity modulates z via hierarchical FiLM: region embedding produces (gamma_r, beta_r) zero-initialized country deviation embedding produces (gamma_c, beta_c), applied z_r = (1+gamma_r) * z + beta_r z_c = (1+gamma_c) * z_r + beta_c. gamma use tanh (gain [0,2], identity init); L2 partial-pool penalty shrinks data-sparse countries toward region. sigmoid head outputs psi [0,1]. Trained BCE loss balanced_uniform sample weights (correcting zero-week imbalance – ~72% -MOSAIC training weeks zero, higher per high-incidence country) plus optional per-row confidence-weight overlay. Training uses expanding-window rolling-origin CV (step validates strictly forward time 4-week embargo, early-stopping records best_epoch); model refit full -sample data median(best_epoch). n_seeds fits averaged logit scale yield prediction (canonical psi column), plus seed-dispersion quantiles (diagnostic , predictive intervals). regime fixes v0.33 random-split temporal leak collapsed --sample forecasts. Defaults pinned B4 fixture; override via arch_control. Honest framing. lstm_v2 converts structurally-collapsed flat psi (Pearson ~0.09, ~3\ directional signal (median cross-country Pearson ~0.22, wide per-cell range large anti-correlated minority – psi can worse climatology countries). strictly dominates incumbent shape; decision-grade forecaster. psi reaches downstream LASER engine TWO channels – fractional deviation (psi - psi_bar)/psi_bar (timing/shape) absolute level (environmental-reservoir decay) – response anchor bias_correct can move outbreak magnitude, just timing. Report per-country spread, just median. default response_var "target_D_rate_per_country_floored" (per-capita, per-country anchor), selected "transmission_intensity" 15-country psi->LASER calibration case-skill comparison: D lifts cases-R2 priority/saturated cluster (COD 0.51->0.73, SOM 0.34->0.82, ETH 0.61->0.80) cost regressions 5 low-burden countries (RWA/MWI/AGO/NAM/SSD), accepted global default. region_map="snf_k5", bias_correct=TRUE; snf_k5 ships unvalidated FiLM region map reverts "csv" beat . lstm_v1_legacy (v0.33, frozen) – shared LSTM, random split + sequential fine-tuning. single 3-stack LSTM (128->64->32) country/region identity (one shared model countries). Trained random 60/40 split across weeks MSE logit-transformed target, followed sequential fine-tuning. Frozen canonical v0.33 settings (tunable; legacy knobs hard-coded internally). Retained reproducibility/rollback. Known limitation: random split places temporally-adjacent weeks sides train/val boundary, --sample forecast collapses amplitude – failure lstm_v2 fixes.

title: Estimate OCV Vaccination Rates — est_vaccination_rate
text: function performs following steps: Load Vaccination Data: - Reads processed vaccination data GTFCC filters relevant columns (iso_code, campaign_date, doses_shipped). Redistribute Doses: - Redistributes shipped doses day day based maximum daily rate (max_rate_per_day). - Ensures duplication distribution_date within iso_code. Validate Redistribution: - Checks redistributed doses sum total shipped doses. Ensure Full Coverage: - Ensures ISO codes data across full date range (date_start date_stop), filling missing dates zero doses. Calculate Population Metrics: - Merges population data 2023, calculates cumulative doses, computes proportion population vaccinated. Save Outputs: - Saves redistributed vaccination data vaccination rate parameter data frame.

title: Estimate Vaccine Effectiveness Decay Parameters — est_vaccine_effectiveness
text: function performs following steps: Creates vaccine effectiveness data Xu et al (2024) meta-regression results Fits exponential decay models VE(t) = phi * exp(-omega * t) separately one-dose two-dose regimens Estimates parameters mean effectiveness confidence intervals using constrained optimization Infers beta distributions initial effectiveness (phi) gamma distributions decay rates (omega) Saves data points, predictions, parameter distributions CSV files model parameters : phi_1, phi_2: Initial vaccine effectiveness one-dose two-dose regimens omega_1, omega_2: Daily decay rates one-dose two-dose regimens

what: Estimate Prior Distribution for Symptomatic Shedding Rate (zeta_1) — est_zeta_1_prior
title: Estimate Prior Distribution for Symptomatic Shedding Rate (zeta_1) — est_zeta_1_prior
text: Compiles meta-analysis published Vibrio cholerae shedding anchors (stool concentration x time-averaged daily stool volume) symptomatic infections fits lognormal prior per-person-per-day shedding rate used MOSAIC environmental reservoir update: $$W(t+1) \mathrel{+}= (1 - \theta_j) \cdot \mathrm{Poisson}(\zeta_1 \cdot I_{\mathrm{sym}})$$ \(\zeta_1\) expected total V. cholerae cells shed per symptomatic infected person per day shedding phase. \(\gamma_1\) parameter handles shedding-phase duration; \(\zeta_1\) daily rate, total-per-infection.

title: Estimate Prior Distribution for Symptomatic Shedding Rate (zeta_1) — est_zeta_1_prior
code: est_zeta_1_prior(   PATHS,   severity_mix = c(severe = 0.2, moderate = 0.4, mild = 0.4) )

title: Estimate Prior Distribution for Symptomatic Shedding Rate (zeta_1) — est_zeta_1_prior
text: PATHS list paths generated get_paths(). Must include: MODEL_INPUT: directory parameter CSVs saved. DOCS_FIGURES: directory summary figure saved. severity_mix Named numeric vector entries severe, moderate, mild sum 1. Used compute severity-weighted pool anchor. Defaults c(severe = 0.2, moderate = 0.4, mild = 0.4) (endemic mix). severe fraction (0.2) informed Harris 2012's narrative approximately 20 percent symptomatic cholera cases severe; moderate/mild split expert estimate exposed sensitivity argument.

title: Estimate Prior Distribution for Symptomatic Shedding Rate (zeta_1) — est_zeta_1_prior
text: Invisibly returns list elements: data: source meta-analysis table. fit: fitted lognormal parameters (meanlog, sdlog, median, mode, mean, ci_lower, ci_upper, n_sources, mu_log10, sd_log10). param_df: long-format parameter data frame (saved disk). prediction: density grid linear log10-x scales. sensitivity: leave-one-anchor-sub-fits (include_calib, volume_peak, volume_low).

title: Estimate Prior Distribution for Symptomatic Shedding Rate (zeta_1) — est_zeta_1_prior
text: function assembles literature table per-person-per-day shedding anchors constructed stool concentration (\(c\), cells per mL) times time-averaged daily stool volume (\(V\), mL per day) defined severity classes (severe, moderate, mild). severity-weighted pool also constructed using severity_mix argument. weighted lognormal fit maximum-likelihood log10 scale. Assembly equation. literature entry contributes \(\zeta_{1,} = c_i \times V_i\) lower upper bounds taken spread concentration volume anchors severity class. Time-averaged stool volumes used (V_sev = 8, V_mod = 4, V_mild = 0.5 L/day) LASER Poisson update multiplies rate current I_sym compartment, already integrates shedding course via \(\gamma_1\). Peak purging rates (~1 L/h, ~14-24 L/day) treated upper-bound sensitivity rows, central anchor. Severity-weighted pool caveat. default severity mix (0.2 / 0.4 / 0.4) severe class dominates ~94 percent arithmetic \(c_{\mathrm{sev}} = 10^8\) 100x larger \(c_{\mathrm{mild}} = 10^6\). acceptable: surveillance observes severe cases disproportionately, effective \(\zeta_1\) data constrains close severe value anyway. severity_mix argument exposed callers can run sensitivity analyses (e.g. outbreak mix 0.35 / 0.40 / 0.25). Weighted MLE log10 scale. Weighted mean SD log10 scale converted lognormal meanlog sdlog: $$\mu_{\log_{10}} = \frac{\sum w_i \log_{10}(\zeta_{1,})}{\sum w_i}, \quad       \sigma^2_{\log_{10}} = \frac{\sum w_i (\log_{10}(\zeta_{1,}) - \mu_{\log_{10}})^2}{\sum w_i}$$ meanlog = mu_log10 * ln(10) sdlog = sigma_log10 * ln(10). Sensitivity returns. $sensitivity list contains three alternative fits: include_calib (refits MOSAIC calibration posterior row weight 0.25, producing much wider sdlog Frame-B-calibrated value 7 OOM biological-units literature anchors), volume_peak (rescales severe-class V=8 L/day rows V=12 L/day), volume_low (rescales V=6 L/day). Per-day vs per-infection. zeta_1 per-day shedding rate symptomatic infectious period; \(\gamma_1^{-1}\) (~10 d) governs duration. cumulative cells shed per symptomatic infection therefore approximately zeta_1 / gamma_1, per-infection contribution ratio vs asymptomatic zeta_ratio * (gamma_2 / gamma_1) (~5x per-day ratio current gamma priors). comparing prior literature quotes cumulative stool output per infection, apply duration multiplier. Relation documented priors. Earlier MOSAIC-docs narrative states zeta_1 ~ Uniform(10^4, 10^8). range derives Frame-B calibration rescaling superseded lognormal literature-derived prior. new prior's 95\ well stale Uniform upper bound. Primary source notes stored claude/zeta_priors_update/zeta_sources/ (14 PDFs + 32 source notes). See claude/zeta_priors_update/plan_zeta_priors_implementation.md full provenance.

title: Estimate Prior Distribution for Symptomatic Shedding Rate (zeta_1) — est_zeta_1_prior
text: Nelson EJ, Harris JB, Morris JG, Calderwood SB, Camilli (2009). Cholera transmission: host, pathogen bacteriophage dynamic. Nat Rev Microbiol 7(10):693-702. Merrell DS, Butler SM, Qadri F, et al. (2002). Host-induced epidemic spread cholera bacterium. Nature 417(6889):642-45. Harris JB, LaRocque RC, Qadri F, Ryan ET, Calderwood SB (2012). Cholera. Lancet 379(9835):2466-76. Nelson EJ, Grembi JA, Chao DL, et al. (2020). Gold standard cholera diagnostics tarnished lytic bacteriophage antibiotics. J Clin Microbiol 58(9). Kaper JB, Morris JG, Levine MM (1995). Cholera. Clin Microbiol Rev 8(1):48-86. Codeco CT (2001). Endemic epidemic dynamics cholera: role aquatic reservoir. BMC Infect Dis 1:1. Nelson EJ, Chowdhury , Flynn J, et al. (2008). Transmission V. cholerae antagonized lytic phage entry aquatic environment. PLoS Pathog 4(10):e1000187. Fung IC (2014). Cholera transmission dynamic models public health practitioners. Emerg Themes Epidemiol 11:1. Kirpich , Weppelmann TA, Yang Y, et al. (2015). Cholera transmission Ouest Department Haiti. PLoS NTD.

title: Estimate Prior Distribution for Asymptomatic Shedding Rate (zeta_2) — est_zeta_2_prior
text: function assembles small literature table per-person-per-day asymptomatic shedding anchors. evidence base thin: Nelson 2009 gives verbatim primary-source anchor ("approximately 10^3 vibrios per gram stool") Kaper 1995 provides expert-review range (10^3 10^5 cells per gram). Given single primary source, fit applies hard floor sdlog >= 2.0 honestly reflect \(n_{\mathrm{independent}} = 1\). Assembly equation. entry contributes \(\zeta_{2,} = c_i \times V_i\) V = 150 g/day taken normal adult physiology (100-250 g/day); cholera-specific asymptomatic stool-volume measurement exists. Single-source caveat sdlog floor. central anchor rests single primary source (Nelson 2009). fit applies hard floor sdlog >= 2.0 (raised 1.5 rev 1 per modeller review). floor preserves lognormal median (meanlog unchanged); mode shifts left consequence. median ~1.5e5 sdlog = 2.0 95 percent CI spans roughly 3e3 7.6e6 - factor ~50 side - correct spread cover +/- 1 order--magnitude error single primary source. King 2008 caveat (documentation ). King, Ionides, Pascual & Bouma (2008) describe "negligible quantity vibrio" shed asymptomatic hosts natural-vaccination model. qualitative directionally supports small zeta_2 / large zeta_ratio, give quantitative value. therefore included fitted row - treating qualitative claim quantitative data masquerade ignorance evidence. See claude/zeta_priors_update/zeta_sources/12_King_2008_Nature_inapparent.md. Leave-Nelson-sensitivity. $sensitivity$leave_nelson_out returns fit using Kaper review rows. fit effective n_primary_sources = 0 (Kaper review cites Nelson-era primary data) published warning, usable prior. n_sources caveat. returned $fit$n_sources equals nrow(fit_df) = 3 (Nelson, Kaper-lower, Kaper-upper). effective number independent primary sources 1 (Nelson 2009); Kaper 1995 review contribute independent primary data. sdlog-floor mechanism encodes single-source risk regardless n_sources count. Per-day vs per-infection. zeta_2 per-day shedding rate asymptomatic infection; \(\gamma_2^{-1}\) (~2 d) governs duration. Nelson's verbatim claim asymptomatic shedding lasts "one day" – close current gamma_2 prior. Cumulative cells per asymptomatic infection zeta_2 / gamma_2. Relation documented priors. Earlier MOSAIC-docs narrative states zeta_2 ~ Uniform(0.01, 10^3). range derives Frame-B calibration rescaling superseded lognormal literature-derived prior, whose 95\

("Asymptomatic patients typically shed vibrios one day approximately 10^3 vibrios per gram stool.") Kaper JB, Morris JG, Levine MM (1995). Cholera. Clin Microbiol Rev 8(1):48-86. (expert review range 10^3 10^5 cells per gram) King AA, Ionides EL, Pascual M, Bouma MJ (2008). Inapparent infections cholera dynamics. Nature 454(7206):877-80. (qualitative ; fitted row)
