title: Estimate Prior Distribution for the Shedding Ratio (zeta_ratio = zeta_1 / zeta_2) — est_zeta_ratio_prior
text: function produces THREE candidate lognormal fits writes precision-weighted combined prior: () Direct channel: weighted-MLE lognormal literature ratio anchors (Smith 2026 medRxiv, Nelson 2009 paired, Kaper 1995, Harris 2012, Chao 2011, Finger 2018, Righetto 2012, Sugimoto 2014, MOSAIC calibration). (B) Derived channel: paired Monte-Carlo lognormal fitted \(\zeta_1\) / \(\zeta_2\) marginals, paired-bootstrap step shared Nelson 2009 anchor. (C) Combined: precision-weighted (inverse-variance) lognormal combination () (B). written priors_default$parameters_global$zeta_ratio. Direct channel (). Weighted MLE log10 literature ratio anchors direct-ratio table. anchors span ~1.6x (Smith 2026 household per-person-day) ~10^5 (Nelson 2009 paired stool concentrations). spread reflects genuinely different quantities (household transmission probability vs stool concentration ratio) preserved fit's sdlog. Derived channel (B). \(\zeta_1 \sim \mathrm{LN}(\mu_1, \sigma_1^2)\) \(\zeta_2 \sim \mathrm{LN}(\mu_2, \sigma_2^2)\) independent, ratio exactly \(\mathrm{LN}(\mu_1 - \mu_2, \sigma_1^2 + \sigma_2^2)\). derived channel uses analytic closed form directly. Nelson 2009 shared primary source marginals, principle induce positive correlation reduce ratio's variance, correctly propagating requires decomposing marginal's variance Nelson-attributable component residual independent component – subjective attribution well-supported available literature. closed-form variance \(\sigma_1^2 + \sigma_2^2\) therefore conservative upper bound true derived-channel spread. 100,000-draw Monte Carlo sample ratio distribution generated diagnostic figure returned $diagnostics$ratio_sample; enter fit statistics. Combined channel (C). Given direct derived fits \(\mathrm{LN}(\mu_A, \sigma_A^2)\) \(\mathrm{LN}(\mu_B, \sigma_B^2)\), precision-weighted mean log scale $$\mu_C = \frac{\mu_A / \sigma_A^2 + \mu_B / \sigma_B^2}{1/\sigma_A^2 + 1/\sigma_B^2}, \quad \sigma_C = \left(1/\sigma_A^2 + 1/\sigma_B^2\right)^{-1/2}$$ Bayesian posterior log-ratio flat priors channels treated independent normal likelihoods. Smith 2026 dose-response note. Smith et al. 2026 (medRxiv) report odds ratio 0.60 (95 percent CI 0.11-3.23) household transmission asymptomatic vs symptomatic index cases, giving inverse transmission ratio ~1.6. near-linear Hill dose-response regime equal contact patterns compatible low per-person-per-day zeta_ratio. value included weight 0.5 alongside Nelson's 10^5 stool-concentration ratio reader can see tension. fitdistrplus dependency. function import fitdistrplus. diagnostic_candidate_fits = TRUE package loaded via requireNamespace() graceful fallback; follows pattern R/est_initial_R.R:704-710.

Upper bound fitted posterior support (NA yet estimated)

title: Estimated Parameters Inventory — estimated_parameters
text: inventory focuses exclusively estimated parameters - prior distributions sampled Bayesian parameter estimation. include: Fixed model constants Non-stochastic derived quantities Intermediate calculations Data inputs (observed cases, demographics, etc.) inventory organized hierarchically: Global parameters (22): value across locations Location-specific parameters (21): Vary geographic location Categories reflect biological processes cholera transmission: transmission: Population mixing, frequency dependence environmental: V. cholerae survival, shedding, dose-response disease: Recovery rates, incubation, symptom proportions immunity: Natural vaccine-induced protection surveillance: Reporting rates delays mobility: Human movement parameters spatial: Geographic covariates (WASH coverage) initial_conditions: Starting compartment proportions seasonality: Temporal transmission patterns

what: Post-hoc evaluation of a rolling-window forecast-validation artifact — evaluate_rolling_cv
title: Post-hoc evaluation of a rolling-window forecast-validation artifact — evaluate_rolling_cv
text: Reads run_rolling_cv predictions artifact computes --sample forecast skill: per-cell metrics (R2 correlation & SSE, bias ratio, 50/95\ horizons, plus skill (MAE ratio) versus seasonal-climatology persistence baselines, aggregated across cutoffs countries bootstrap CIs.

title: Post-hoc evaluation of a rolling-window forecast-validation artifact — evaluate_rolling_cv
code: evaluate_rolling_cv(   predictions,   horizons_months = c(1, 3, 5),   baselines = c("seasonal", "persistence"),   metrics = c("cases", "deaths"),   trusted_only = TRUE,   n_boot = 1000L,   seed = 1L )

title: Post-hoc evaluation of a rolling-window forecast-validation artifact — evaluate_rolling_cv
text: predictions run_rolling_cv output directory, path predictions.parquet, predictions data frame . horizons_months Cumulative OOS horizons months (default c(1,3,5)). baselines Baselines skill scores (default c("seasonal","persistence")). metrics Metrics evaluate (default c("cases","deaths")). trusted_only Drop rows observed_source == "AI" (default TRUE). n_boot Bootstrap resamples cells skill CI (default 1000). seed RNG seed bootstrap (default 1). predictions carry model column (ensemble, ensemble_opt, best, medoid), every metric computed reported per model; absent column rows treated single "ensemble" model (back-compatible).

title: Post-hoc evaluation of a rolling-window forecast-validation artifact — evaluate_rolling_cv
text: list : cells Per (cutoff x model x iso x metric x window) metrics + per-baseline skill. summary Aggregated across cells per (model x metric x window): cell count, mean/median metric, mean baseline skill bootstrap CI.

title: Post-hoc evaluation of a rolling-window forecast-validation artifact — evaluate_rolling_cv
text: post-hoc companion run_rolling_cv() — harness emits predictions ; judgement (metrics, baselines, skill) lives . Horizons cumulative (\(\le h\) months): \(\le h\) window every OOS point within h months forecast origin, computed date cutoff_date (disjoint horizon_bucket label), \(\le\)max-horizon window equals full scored OOS set. window (training fit, \(\le\) cutoff) always reported. Scoring uses trusted observed : rows whose observed_source missing "AI" (trusted_only = TRUE). Baselines, fit per (cutoff, country, metric) observed history: "persistence" (trailing 4-point mean carried forward) "seasonal" (week--year mean observations). Skill MAE ratio \(1 - MAE_{model}/MAE_{baseline}\) (positive = model beats baseline).

what: Fit Gompertz Distribution from Mode and Probability Interval — fit_gompertz_from_ci
title: Fit Gompertz Distribution from Mode and Probability Interval — fit_gompertz_from_ci
text: function estimates parameters Gompertz distribution [0, Inf) pdf f(x; b, eta) = b * eta * exp(bx) * exp(-eta(exp(bx) - 1)), given target interior mode two-sided interval (default: central 95 percent). interior mode condition enforced eta = b * exp(bmode), holds exactly Gompertz mode eta > b.

title: Fit Gompertz Distribution from Mode and Probability Interval — fit_gompertz_from_ci
code: fit_gompertz_from_ci(   mode_val,   ci_lower,   ci_upper,   probs = c(0.025, 0.975),   verbose = FALSE )

title: Fit Gompertz Distribution from Mode and Probability Interval — fit_gompertz_from_ci
text: mode_val Numeric greater 0. Target mode distribution (near zero allowed). ci_lower Numeric greater equal 0. Lower bound target interval (e.g., 2.5 percent quantile). ci_upper Numeric greater ci_lower. Upper bound target interval (e.g., 97.5 percent quantile). probs Numeric length-2 vector (0, 1). Probability levels target bounds. Defaults c(0.025, 0.975). verbose Logical. TRUE, prints diagnostic summary.

title: Fit Gompertz Distribution from Mode and Probability Interval — fit_gompertz_from_ci
text: list containing: b: Gompertz shape parameter eta: Gompertz rate parameter f0: Density zero (finite positive) fitted_mode: implied mode (matches mode_val numeric error) fitted_ci: Named vector fitted quantiles probs fitted_mean: Numerical estimate expected value via quadrature fitted_sd: Numerical estimate standard deviation via quadrature probs: probability levels used input_mode: Echo mode_val input_ci: Echo c(lower = ci_lower, upper = ci_upper)

title: Fit Gompertz Distribution from Mode and Probability Interval — fit_gompertz_from_ci
code: # Example: Fit Gompertz for small positive quantity result <- fit_gompertz_from_ci(   mode_val = 1e-8,   ci_lower = 1e-9,   ci_upper = 1e-6,   probs = c(0.025, 0.975) ) print(result) #> $b #> [1] 1919.758 #>  #> $eta #> [1] 1919.795 #>  #> $f0 #> [1] 3685543 #>  #> $fitted_mode #> [1] 1e-08 #>  #> $fitted_ci #>        lower        upper  #> 6.869447e-09 9.999450e-07  #>  #> $fitted_mean #> [1] 2.711853e-07 #>  #> $fitted_sd #> [1] 2.710226e-07 #>  #> $probs #> [1] 0.025 0.975 #>  #> $input_mode #> [1] 1e-08 #>  #> $input_ci #> lower upper  #> 1e-09 1e-06  #>

title: Fit Lognormal Distribution from Mode and 95% Confidence Intervals — fit_lognormal_from_ci
text: lognormal distribution parameters meanlog (μ) sdlog (σ): Mode = exp(μ - σ²) Mean = exp(μ + σ²/2) Variance = (exp(σ²) - 1) * exp(2μ + σ²) moment matching method estimates parameters mode CI width log scale, adjusts lognormal relationship.

what: Fourier Series Model with Two Harmonics (Sine-Cosine Form) — fourier_series_double
title: Fourier Series Model with Two Harmonics (Sine-Cosine Form) — fourier_series_double
text: function implements generalized Fourier series model two harmonics sine-cosine form. model often used capture seasonal periodic dynamics time series data, temperature, precipitation, disease cases.

title: Fourier Series Model with Two Harmonics (Sine-Cosine Form) — fourier_series_double

title: Fourier Series Model with Two Harmonics (Sine-Cosine Form) — fourier_series_double
text: t numeric vector representing time points (e.g., day year week year). beta0 numeric value representing intercept term (fixed 0 default). a_1 numeric value representing amplitude first cosine term (first harmonic). b_1 numeric value representing amplitude first sine term (first harmonic). a_2 numeric value representing amplitude second cosine term (second harmonic). b_2 numeric value representing amplitude second sine term (second harmonic). p numeric value representing period seasonal cycle (e.g., 52 weekly data year).

title: Fourier Series Model with Two Harmonics (Sine-Cosine Form) — fourier_series_double
text: numeric vector predicted values based Fourier series model.

title: Fourier Series Model with Two Harmonics (Sine-Cosine Form) — fourier_series_double
text: model defined follows: $$\beta_t = \beta_0 + a_1 \cos\left(\frac{2 \pi t}{p}\right) + b_1 \sin\left(\frac{2 \pi t}{p}\right) + a_2 \cos\left(\frac{4 \pi t}{p}\right) + b_2 \sin\left(\frac{4 \pi t}{p}\right)$$ model includes intercept term beta0 (set 0 default) two harmonics coefficients a_1, b_1, a_2, b_2. period p controls periodicity series. function based sine-cosine form Fourier series. details, see Wikipedia page Fourier series. function commonly used modeling seasonal dynamics environmental epidemiological data, periodic patterns observed.

title: Fourier Series Model with Two Harmonics (Sine-Cosine Form) — fourier_series_double
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Example usage with weekly data (p = 52 weeks in a year) time_points <- 1:52 beta0 <- 0 a_1 <- 1.5 b_1 <- -0.5 a_2 <- 0.8 b_2 <- 0.3 p <- 52  predictions <- fourier_series_double(time_points, beta0, a_1, b_1, a_2, b_2, p) print(predictions) } # }

title: Create Binary Environmental Suitability Indicator from Case Data — get_cases_binary

title: Create Binary Environmental Suitability Indicator from Case Data — get_cases_binary
text: data data frame containing cholera surveillance data columns: iso_code: ISO country code cases: Weekly cholera case counts date_start: Start date week (ordering) cutoff Numeric threshold case counts. Weeks cases >= cutoff considered outbreak periods. Default 1 (cases).

title: Create Binary Environmental Suitability Indicator from Case Data — get_cases_binary
text: input data frame additional cases_binary column containing binary environmental suitability indicator (0 = unsuitable, 1 = suitable).

title: Create Binary Environmental Suitability Indicator from Case Data — get_cases_binary
text: environmental suitability logic works follows: Primary identification: Weeks cases >= cutoff marked suitable (1) Lead-period: week immediately outbreak week marked suitable Extended lead-: two consecutive weeks cases >= cutoff, 2-3 weeks also marked suitable approach recognizes environmental conditions favorable cholera transmission typically develop weeks preceding observable outbreaks. marking lead-periods "suitable," model can learn predict environmental suitability cases reported, useful early warning systems. Missing Data Handling: Weeks missing case data (NA) preserved NA binary indicator never retroactively marked suitable lead-period logic. prevents false environmental suitability signals periods poor surveillance coverage. function processes country separately ensure temporal relationships maintained within location's outbreak history.

title: Create Binary Environmental Suitability Indicator from Case Data — get_cases_binary
text: temporal logic requires data ordered date within country. function sort data appropriately, best performance, input data already ordered iso_code date_start.

title: Create Binary Environmental Suitability Indicator from Case Data — get_cases_binary

what: Get Default Subset Tiers for Post-Hoc Optimization — get_default_subset_tiers
title: Get Default Subset Tiers for Post-Hoc Optimization — get_default_subset_tiers

title: Get Default Subset Tiers for Post-Hoc Optimization — get_default_subset_tiers

title: Get Default Subset Tiers for Post-Hoc Optimization — get_default_subset_tiers
text: target_ESS_best Numeric target ESS best subset (default: 500) target_A Numeric target agreement index (default: 0.95) target_CVw Numeric target coefficient variation (default: 0.7)

title: Get Default Subset Tiers for Post-Hoc Optimization — get_default_subset_tiers
text: named list element contains: name: Tier identifier : Target agreement index (0-1) CVw: Maximum coefficient variation weights ESS_B: Target effective sample size

title: Get Default Subset Tiers for Post-Hoc Optimization — get_default_subset_tiers
text: function generates 30 hardcoded tiers hybrid degradation strategy: Tiers 1-20 (ESS-constant): ESS_B remains constant target_ESS_best degrades 5% per tier (multiplied 0.95) CVw increases 5% per tier (multiplied 1.05) Prioritizes statistical power relaxing quality criteria Tiers 21-30 (Fallback): three criteria degrade 5% per tier ESS_B, , CVw relax simultaneously Graceful degradation ESS target achieved Example defaults (target_ESS_best=500, target_A=0.95, target_CVw=0.7):
code: Tier  1: ESS=500, A=0.950, CVw=0.700  (Stringent) Tier  5: ESS=500, A=0.773, CVw=0.817  (High quality) Tier 10: ESS=500, A=0.599, CVw=0.931  (Moderate quality) Tier 20: ESS=500, A=0.358, CVw=1.327  (Last ESS-constant) Tier 21: ESS=475, A=0.340, CVw=1.393  (Fallback begins) Tier 30: ESS=315, A=0.226, CVw=1.986  (Final fallback)

title: Get Default Subset Tiers for Post-Hoc Optimization — get_default_subset_tiers
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Get default 30 tiers tiers <- get_default_subset_tiers(   target_ESS_best = 500,   target_A = 0.95,   target_CVw = 0.7 )  # Custom starting criteria tiers_custom <- get_default_subset_tiers(   target_ESS_best = 600,   target_A = 0.90,   target_CVw = 0.75 )  # Loop through tiers in calibration with grid search for (tier_name in names(tiers)) {   tier <- tiers[[tier_name]]   result <- grid_search_best_subset(     results = results,     target_ESS = tier$ESS_B,     target_A = tier$A,     target_CVw = tier$CVw,     min_size = 30,     max_size = 1000   )   if (result$converged) break } } # }

title: Get Effective AIC Range for Gibbs Temperature Scaling — get_effective_aic_range

title: Get Effective AIC Range for Gibbs Temperature Scaling — get_effective_aic_range
text: effective range represents expected delta AIC span top X% models typical (non-dispersed) conditions. Uses heuristic maps percentiles Burnham & Anderson's AIC thresholds. Based interpretation: 1% → Δ AIC < 2 (substantial support, ~37% relative likelihood) 5% → Δ AIC ≈ 4 (boundary substantial/less support) 10% → Δ AIC ≈ 7 (upper end "considerably less support") 20% → Δ AIC ≈ 10 (boundary "essentially support")

title: Create Train/Validation Splits for LSTM Sequences — get_lstm_sequence_splits
text: function creates train/validation splits LSTM sequences using either random sampling location-month blocking. location-month blocking approach groups sequences country-month combinations randomly assigns entire blocks train/test, better preserves spatial-temporal structure tests generalization capability.

title: Create Train/Validation Splits for LSTM Sequences — get_lstm_sequence_splits
text: train_sequences List containing LSTM sequences components X, y, countries, dates (sequence structure built internally legacy est_suitability() path). split_method Character. Either "random" (default) "location_month_block" train_prop Numeric. Proportion data training (default 0.6) seed Integer. Random seed reproducibility verbose Logical. Whether print detailed information splits (default TRUE)

title: Create Train/Validation Splits for LSTM Sequences — get_lstm_sequence_splits
text: List containing train/validation indices metadata: train_indices: Indices training sequences val_indices: Indices validation sequences split_info: List split statistics metadata

title: Create Train/Validation Splits for LSTM Sequences — get_lstm_sequence_splits
text: Random Method (existing approach): Randomly samples individual sequences train/test split Good general model development comparison May allow data leakage sequences location-month Location-Month Block Method (new approach): Groups sequences iso_code + year-month combinations Randomly assigns entire location-month blocks train validation Ensures data leakage train/validation location-month Better tests spatial-temporal generalization capability realistic evaluation deployment new regions/time periods location-month blocking approach particularly valuable : Testing model generalization unseen location-time combinations Preventing overfitting specific location-month patterns Evaluating suitability early warning systems Assessing transferability across different epidemiological contexts

title: Create Train/Validation Splits for LSTM Sequences — get_lstm_sequence_splits
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # train_sequences is a list with X, y, countries, and dates components # (built internally by the legacy est_suitability() path).  # Random splitting (existing method) splits_random <- get_lstm_sequence_splits(   train_sequences,   split_method = "random",   train_prop = 0.6,   seed = 99 )  # Location-month blocking (new method) splits_blocked <- get_lstm_sequence_splits(   train_sequences,   split_method = "location_month_block",   train_prop = 0.6,   seed = 99 )  # Use the splits in LSTM training X_train <- train_sequences$X[splits_blocked$train_indices, , ] y_train <- train_sequences$y[splits_blocked$train_indices] X_val <- train_sequences$X[splits_blocked$val_indices, , ] y_val <- train_sequences$y[splits_blocked$val_indices]  # Check split information print(splits_blocked$split_info) } # }

what: Estimate Beta Prior for Rho (Care-Seeking Rate) and Save Parameter Data Frame — get_rho_care_seeking_params
title: Estimate Beta Prior for Rho (Care-Seeking Rate) and Save Parameter Data Frame — get_rho_care_seeking_params
text: pool two Wiens strata? Symptomatic cholera MOSAIC's setting spans full severity spectrum (mild watery diarrhea severe rapid dehydration). two Wiens 2025 meta-analytic strata bracket spectrum: General diarrhea (3+ loose/liquid stools): 29.9\ (95\ care-seeking broader population diarrheal episodes, appropriate mild--moderate tail symptomatic cholera (majority cases count). Severe diarrhea + cholera: 58.6\ 22 observations. Represents care-seeking symptoms severe enough flagged "severe" specifically cholera, appropriate severe tail symptomatic cholera. Anchoring severe+cholera stratum ALONE biases rho upward (represents severe tail dominated outbreak-response settings enhanced care-seeking, e.g. Haiti 2010-19 outbreak contributes 50\ ALONE biases rho downward (broader category includes many mild, self-resolving episodes reflect cholera-specific severity). Random-effects pooling two strata produces estimate honestly reflects severity spectrum substantial heterogeneity. include GEMS Nasrin 2013 separate co-anchor? Earlier versions (v0.32.5) pooled GEMS pediatric moderate--severe diarrhea (4 SSA sites, 12 stratum-level estimates) Wiens severe+cholera pooled estimate. pooling three problems: (1) GEMS measures pediatric MSD care-seeking, different population MOSAIC's -ages cholera; (2) 12 GEMS strata vs. 1 Wiens meta-analytic summary upside-dimensionally; (3) Wiens 2025 already includes GEMS-derived data (6 Study IDs tagged GEMS/HUAS), direct GEMS entries partially double-counted source populations. Dropping GEMS using Wiens's two strata directly resolves three. Methodology. Random-effects meta-analysis logit scale (DerSimonian-Laird tau^2) two Wiens strata unit pooling. 95\ pooled mean fit Beta distribution via get_beta_params. Geographic provenance (severe+cholera stratum). 23 underlying studies: 16 SSA, 3 Latin America/Caribbean (mostly Haiti 2010-19), 2 Bangladesh, 1 MENA, 1 South Asia (non-BGD). Predominantly SSA + outbreak settings - regime MOSAIC calibrates.

title: Grid Search for Best Subset with Early Stopping — grid_search_best_subset

title: Grid Search for Best Subset with Early Stopping — grid_search_best_subset
text: List elements: n: Optimal subset size (smallest n meeting criteria) subset: Data frame selected simulations metrics: List ESS, , CVw values optimal n converged: Logical indicating criteria met evaluations: Integer number n values tested

title: Grid Search for Best Subset with Early Stopping — grid_search_best_subset
text: function searches min_size max_size step_size, stopping first size three criteria met simultaneously: ESS >= target_ESS >= target_A CVw <= target_CVw Metrics calculated using Gibbs weighting dynamic temperature: Calculate AIC = -2 * likelihood subset Calculate Delta AIC (relative best subset) Calculate dynamic temperature: effective_range = 4.0 (standard Akaike range) actual_range = range(Delta AIC) temperature = 0.5 * (effective_range / actual_range) Apply Gibbs weighting: weights = exp(-Delta AIC / temperature) / Z Calculate ESS, , CVw Gibbs-weighted samples temperature adapts data's actual Delta AIC range, providing consistent discrimination across different likelihood spreads. size meets criteria, returns results max_size converged=FALSE.

title: Grid Search for Best Subset with Early Stopping — grid_search_best_subset

title: Impute Country-Week Flood Probability from EM-DAT and Climate Covariates — impute_flood_probability
text: Fits enriched binomial GAM observed EM-DAT flood events (emdat_flood_active, 0/1) function climate anomalies, multi-month antecedent precipitation, region-conditional precipitation, joint precip/soil-moisture interactions, ENSO/IOD long lags, country random effects. fitted model used predict continuous flood probability every (iso_code, year, week) row – historical forecast. imputed column lets downstream LSTM consume feature definition training inference, eliminating distribution shift raw 0/1 indicator otherwise introduce.

title: Impute Country-Week Flood Probability from EM-DAT and Climate Covariates — impute_flood_probability
code: impute_flood_probability(   d,   output_col = "emdat_flood_prob",   diagnostics = TRUE,   diag_dir = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Impute Country-Week Flood Probability from EM-DAT and Climate Covariates — impute_flood_probability
text: d data.frame one row per (iso_code, year, week). Must contain columns returned .impute_flood_probability_required(): iso_code, year, week, date; binary target emdat_flood_active (0/1; NA forecast rows fine); subregion region; precipitation channels precipitation_sum, precip_anom, precip_sum_2w/4w/8w/12w, precip_extreme_p90_count; soil moisture soil_moisture_0_to_10cm_mean, soil_moisture_anom; spei_approx; atmospheric humidity relative_humidity_2m_mean, rh_mean_12w; storm proxy wind_speed_10m_max; teleconnection indices ENSO3, ENSO34, ENSO4, IOD (lags long-window precip sums computed inline function). output_col Character. Name new probability column. Default "emdat_flood_prob". diagnostics Logical. TRUE, fits rolling-year cross-validation writes four artefacts diag_dir: flood_gam_smooths.png, flood_gam_calibration.png, flood_gam_cv_metrics.csv, flood_gam_summary.txt. diag_dir Character. Directory diagnostic outputs (created missing). Ignored diagnostics = FALSE. verbose Logical. Echo progress messages.

title: Impute Country-Week Flood Probability from EM-DAT and Climate Covariates — impute_flood_probability
text: input data.frame one added column named output_col carrying non-NA numeric [0, 1] every row.

title: Impute Country-Week Flood Probability from EM-DAT and Climate Covariates — impute_flood_probability
text: four "enriched" predictors (added v0.30.23 prior v0.30.20-21 baseline) – long-memory antecedent precipitation 24-week 52-week horizons, region-conditional medium-window precip smooth, precip-anomaly x soil-moisture-anomaly joint interaction – improve detection major cyclone-driven flood events (Idai 2019, Kenneth 2019, Eloise 2021, Ana 2022, Gombe 2022, Freddy 2023). 10-cyclone benchmark formulation lands every event top 21\ distribution (median 93rd percentile), reliable cyclone detector across rebuild strategies tested. GAM fit mgcv::bam() (mgcv's big-data variant – uses block updates, discrete covariate binning, parallel-friendly linear algebra; ~30k rows returns ~30-40 sec). family = stats::binomial(link = "logit") produces predictions naturally [0, 1] without rescaling. method = "fREML", select = TRUE (smoothness null-space shrinkage, principled mgcv mechanism driving uninformative smooths toward zero – replaces iterative -sample-p-value pruning loop v0.30.24 used). Predictors computed inline (caller need provide ): ENSO34 IOD lags 8, 16, 24 weeks (current value raw column). Long-memory antecedent precipitation: precip_sum_24w (~6-month basin saturation memory) precip_sum_52w (annual). Joint climate index: precip_anom * soil_moisture_anom (-wet already-saturated regime). Region-conditional precip-sum-4w smooth requires region column input dataframe (4-region classification: Central / East / Southern / West Africa). Sentinel handling: row whose prediction NA (e.g. lag-startup edge week 20 earliest year, row missing predictor survived upstream imputation) filled country's mean predicted probability. Rows even country mean NA fall back global mean. function asserts final column [0, 1] contains NAs. diagnostics = TRUE, function additionally runs rolling-year cross-validation: 3 recent fully-observed years Y, GAM refit rows year < Y used predict rows year == Y. Per-fold AUC, Brier score, log-loss written flood_gam_cv_metrics.csv. mean CV AUC 0.65, warning emitted function still returns column.

what: Inflate Prior or Posterior Distributions by a Variance Factor — inflate_priors
title: Inflate Prior or Posterior Distributions by a Variance Factor — inflate_priors
text: Takes priors posteriors list object inflates variance distribution multiplicative factor preserving mean. used staged Bayesian estimation broaden tight posteriors one stage passing priors next stage, preventing weight collapse importance sampling (particle filter / BFRS) consecutive stages target different objectives. technique known kernel smoothing, covariance inflation, variance tempering Sequential Monte Carlo literature (Liu & West 2001; Del Moral et al. 2006).

title: Inflate Prior or Posterior Distributions by a Variance Factor — inflate_priors
code: inflate_priors(   priors,   inflation_factor = 2,   params = NULL,   n_samples = 10000L,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Inflate Prior or Posterior Distributions by a Variance Factor — inflate_priors
text: priors List. priors posteriors object parameters_global parameters_location slots. inflation_factor Numeric scalar > 0. Variance multiplier. 2.0 doubles variance preserving mean. Default 2.0. params Optional character vector. Base parameter names inflate. NULL non-fixed/frozen parameters inflated. n_samples Integer. Number samples empirical fallback methods (truncnorm fallback, gompertz). Default 10000L. verbose Logical. Print per-parameter inflation messages. Default TRUE.

title: Inflate Prior or Posterior Distributions by a Variance Factor — inflate_priors
text: priors list inflated distribution parameters. Metadata updated.

title: Inflate Prior or Posterior Distributions by a Variance Factor — inflate_priors
text: distribution inflated using appropriate method: Analytic (exact): beta(shape1, shape2) Reduce precision s = α+β factor f. Maximum inflatable factor f_max = s+1. gamma(shape, rate) shape_new = shape/f, rate_new = rate/f. lognormal(meanlog, sdlog) CV² scaled f; meanlog adjusted preserve natural-scale mean. lognormal(mean, sd) sd_new = sqrt(f)*sd. normal(mean, sd) sd_new = sqrt(f)*sd. uniform(min, max) Bounds extended symmetrically around midpoint. Skipped warning new bounds violate positivity. Analytic empirical fallback: truncnorm(mean, sd, , b) Root-finding preserves actual truncated mean. Due active bounds, achieved variance ratio may less inflation_factor. Falls back empirical refit root-find fails. Empirical (sample → inflate → refit): gompertz(b, eta) closed-form moments. Sampled empirically, variance inflated, distribution refit via MLE. distribution Universal empirical fallback.

text: Timestamps emitted ISO 8601 format (e.g. "2026-06-05T14:32:01-0700") downstream consumer (human tail, monitoring AI agent, log shipper) can parse elapsed time without ambiguity. Lines written stdout , log file path known, also appended file. path resolved order: getOption("MOSAIC.log_file") — preferred. Set start pipeline (run_MOSAIC() automatically) every log_msg call — including inside helper functions whose calling frame dir_output variable — write file. dir_output binding calling frame (legacy fallback). file path becomes <dir_output>/run.log. preserves backward compatibility callers set option. neither source resolves path, lines go stdout .

what: Create a Configuration File for LASER — make_LASER_config
title: Create a Configuration File for LASER — make_LASER_config
text: function generates JSON/HDF5/YAML configuration file used LASER model simulation parameters. validates input parameters , output file path provided, writes parameters file. file extension determines output format used: .json .json.gz → written write_list_to_json, .h5, .h5.gz → written write_list_to_hdf5, .yaml .yaml.gz → written write_list_to_yaml.

title: Create a Configuration File for LASER — make_LASER_config
code: make_LASER_config(   output_file_path = NULL,   seed = NULL,   date_start = NULL,   date_stop = NULL,   location_name = NULL,   N_j_initial = NULL,   S_j_initial = NULL,   E_j_initial = NULL,   I_j_initial = NULL,   R_j_initial = NULL,   V1_j_initial = NULL,   V2_j_initial = NULL,   prop_S_initial = NULL,   prop_E_initial = NULL,   prop_I_initial = NULL,   prop_R_initial = NULL,   prop_V1_initial = NULL,   prop_V2_initial = NULL,   b_jt = NULL,   d_jt = NULL,   nu_1_jt = NULL,   nu_2_jt = NULL,   phi_1 = NULL,   phi_2 = NULL,   omega_1 = NULL,   omega_2 = NULL,   nu_jt_sources = c("S", "E", "Isym", "Iasym", "R"),   iota = NULL,   gamma_1 = NULL,   gamma_2 = NULL,   epsilon = NULL,   mu_jt = NULL,   mu_j_baseline = NULL,   mu_j_slope = NULL,   mu_j_epidemic_factor = NULL,   rho = NULL,   rho_deaths = NULL,   sigma = NULL,   chi_endemic = NULL,   chi_epidemic = NULL,   epidemic_threshold = NULL,   delta_reporting_cases = NULL,   delta_reporting_deaths = NULL,   longitude = NULL,   latitude = NULL,   mobility_omega = NULL,   mobility_gamma = NULL,   tau_i = NULL,   beta_j0_tot = NULL,   p_beta = NULL,   beta_j0_hum = NULL,   a_1_j = NULL,   a_2_j = NULL,   b_1_j = NULL,   b_2_j = NULL,   p = 365L,   alpha_1 = NULL,   alpha_2 = NULL,   beta_j0_env = NULL,   theta_j = NULL,   psi_jt = NULL,   psi_star_a = NULL,   psi_star_b = NULL,   psi_star_z = NULL,   psi_star_k = NULL,   zeta_1 = NULL,   zeta_2 = NULL,   kappa = NULL,   decay_days_short = NULL,   decay_days_long = NULL,   decay_shape_1 = NULL,   decay_shape_2 = NULL,   reported_cases = NULL,   reported_deaths = NULL,   epidemic_peaks = NULL,   sigfigs = 8 )

title: Create a Configuration File for LASER — make_LASER_config
text: output_file_path character string representing full file path output file. Must .json, .json.gz, .h5, .hdf5, .h5.gz, .yaml, .yaml.gz extension. NULL, file written parameters returned. seed Integer scalar giving random seed value simulation run. date_start Start date simulation period "YYYY-MM-DD" format. provided character string, converted Date object. date_stop End date simulation period "YYYY-MM-DD" format. provided character string, converted Date object. location_name character vector giving names metapopulation location. order names must match used initial population vectors. N_j_initial named numeric integer vector length equal location_name giving total initial population size location. Note total population size must sum model compartments. S_j_initial named numeric integer vector length equal location_name giving starting number susceptible individuals location. Names must match location_name. E_j_initial named numeric integer vector length equal location_name giving starting number exposed individuals location. Names must match location_name. I_j_initial named numeric integer vector length equal location_name giving starting number infected individuals location. Names must match location_name. R_j_initial named numeric integer vector length equal location_name giving starting number recovered individuals location. Names must match location_name. V1_j_initial named numeric integer vector length equal location_name giving starting number individuals vaccine compartment V1 location. Names must match location_name. V2_j_initial named numeric integer vector length equal location_name giving starting number individuals vaccine compartment V2 location. Names must match location_name. prop_S_initial Optional. named numeric vector length equal location_name giving starting proportion susceptible individuals location. Values must [0,1]. Names must match location_name. proportion fields provided analysis convenience required LASER model. prop_E_initial Optional. named numeric vector length equal location_name giving starting proportion exposed individuals location. Values must [0,1]. Names must match location_name. prop_I_initial Optional. named numeric vector length equal location_name giving starting proportion infected individuals location. Values must [0,1]. Names must match location_name. prop_R_initial Optional. named numeric vector length equal location_name giving starting proportion recovered individuals location. Values must [0,1]. Names must match location_name. prop_V1_initial Optional. named numeric vector length equal location_name giving starting proportion individuals vaccine compartment V1 location. Values must [0,1]. Names must match location_name. prop_V2_initial Optional. named numeric vector length equal location_name giving starting proportion individuals vaccine compartment V2 location. Values must [0,1]. Names must match location_name. b_jt matrix birth rates rows equal length(location_name) columns equal daily sequence date_start date_stop. d_jt matrix mortality rates rows equal length(location_name) columns equal daily sequence date_start date_stop. nu_1_jt matrix first-dose OCV vaccinations location time step. nu_2_jt matrix second-dose OCV vaccinations location time step. phi_1 Effectiveness one dose OCV (numeric [0, 1]). phi_2 Effectiveness two doses OCV (numeric [0, 1]). omega_1 Waning immunity rate one dose (numeric >= 0). omega_2 Waning immunity rate two doses (numeric >= 0). nu_jt_sources Character vector compartment names eligible first-dose vaccination (default c("S", "E", "Isym", "Iasym", "R")). Must non-empty subset c("S", "E", "Isym", "Iasym", "R"). Passed laser-cholera v0.12+ draws first doses proportionally listed compartments. iota Incubation rate E -> (numeric > 0, per day). Note rate, period – prior median ~0.71/day. engine uses iota * E flow E. gamma_1 Symptomatic shedding-duration rate I_sym -> R (numeric >= 0, per day; "severe / symptomatic" branch). gamma_2 Asymptomatic shedding-duration rate I_asym -> R (numeric >= 0, per day; "mild / asymptomatic" branch). epsilon Natural-infection immunity waning rate R -> S (numeric >= 0, per day). Distinct vaccine waning (omega_1, omega_2). mu_jt matrix time-varying probabilities mortality due infection, rows equal length(location_name) columns equal length(t). values must numeric 0 1. mu_j_baseline provided IFR parameters, mu_jt can generated using calc_deaths_from_infections(). mu_j_baseline Baseline infection fatality ratio threshold-dependent IFR model. Numeric vector length(location_name). Values must [0, 1]. mu_j_slope Temporal trend baseline IFR (proportion change simulation period). Numeric vector length(location_name). Default 0 (temporal trend). mu_j_epidemic_factor Proportional increase IFR epidemic periods (e.g., 0.5 = 50% increase). Numeric vector length(location_name). Must >= 0. rho Care-seeking rate: probability symptomatic individual presents surveillance (numeric [0, 1]). reporting fraction sigma – upstream care-seeking step surveillance cascade. rho_deaths Death detection rate: probability true cholera death captured surveillance (numeric [0, 1] NULL). Consumed engine laser-cholera v0.13+ (laser-cholera#49) produce reported_deaths; older engine versions ignore use raw simulated counts. sigma Proportion infections symptomatic (numeric [0, 1]). chi_endemic Positive predictive value among suspected cases endemic periods (numeric (0, 1]). chi_epidemic Positive predictive value among suspected cases epidemic periods (numeric (0, 1]). epidemic_threshold Isym/N point prevalence threshold epidemic regime activation. Used case reporting IFR threshold models. Numeric scalar length-n vector [0, 1]. delta_reporting_cases Symptom-onset--surveillance reporting delay days (non-negative integer). infection--report – incubation handled separately E compartment iota. delta_reporting_deaths Symptom-onset--death-report delay days (non-negative integer). longitude numeric vector longitudes location. Must length location_name. latitude numeric vector latitudes location. Must length location_name. mobility_omega Exponent weight destination population gravity mobility model. Must numeric ≥ 0. mobility_gamma Exponent weight distance decay gravity mobility model. Must numeric ≥ 0. tau_i Departure probability origin location (numeric vector length(location_name) [0, 1]). beta_j0_tot Total baseline transmission rate (human + environmental). Optional numeric vector length(location_name). provided p_beta, used derive beta_j0_hum beta_j0_env. p_beta Proportion total transmission human--human (0-1). Optional numeric vector length(location_name). provided beta_j0_tot, used derive beta_j0_hum beta_j0_env. beta_j0_hum Baseline human--human transmission rate (numeric vector length(location_name)). beta_j0_tot p_beta provided, validated beta_j0_tot * p_beta. a_1_j Vector sine amplitude coefficients (1st harmonic) location. Numeric, length = length(location_name). a_2_j Vector sine amplitude coefficients (2nd harmonic) location. Numeric, length = length(location_name). b_1_j Vector cosine amplitude coefficients (1st harmonic) location. Numeric, length = length(location_name). b_2_j Vector cosine amplitude coefficients (2nd harmonic) location. Numeric, length = length(location_name). p Period seasonal forcing function. Scalar numeric > 0. Default 365 daily annual seasonality. alpha_1 FOI mixing exponent applied infectious term (/N) (numeric (0, 1]; 1 = well-mixed contacts). Dual-mode (laser-cholera v0.16.0+): scalar applied uniformly across patches, per-location vector length length(location_name). alpha_2 Exponent N_jt FOI denominator (numeric [0, 1]). alpha_2 = 1 frequency-dependent transmission (FOI proportional /N); alpha_2 = 0 density-dependent (FOI proportional ). Dual-mode: scalar per-location vector length length(location_name). beta_j0_env Baseline environment--human transmission rate (numeric vector length(location_name)). beta_j0_tot p_beta provided, validated beta_j0_tot * (1 - p_beta). theta_j Proportion adequate WASH (numeric vector length(location_name) [0, 1]). psi_jt Matrix environmental suitability values (matrix rows = length(location_name) columns equal daily sequence date_start date_stop). psi_star_a Shape/gain parameter logit calibration psi_jt (numeric vector length(location_name) > 0). Values > 1 sharpen peaks, values < 1 flatten peaks. Default 1.0 (transformation). psi_star_b Scale/offset parameter logit calibration psi_jt (numeric vector length(location_name)). Shifts baseline /logit scale. Default 0.0 (offset). psi_star_z Smoothing weight causal EWMA calibrated psi_jt (numeric vector length(location_name) (0,1]). 1.0 = smoothing, < 1.0 = apply smoothing. Default 1.0 (smoothing). psi_star_k Time offset days psi_jt calibration (numeric vector length(location_name)). Positive values = forward/delay, negative values = backward/advance. Default 0.0 (offset). zeta_1 Shedding rate (numeric > 0). zeta_2 Asymptomatic shedding rate (numeric > 0). Derived sample_parameters() zeta_1 / zeta_ratio. kappa Concentration required 50% infection (numeric > 0). decay_days_short Time constant (days) short-term survival V. cholerae environment. Must > 0 < decay_days_long. decay_days_long Time constant (days) long-term survival V. cholerae environment. Must > 0 > decay_days_short. decay_shape_1 First shape parameter beta distribution controlling environmental suitability maps decay rate V. cholerae environment. Must numeric > 0. decay_shape_2 Second shape parameter beta distribution controlling environmental suitability maps decay rate V. cholerae environment. Must numeric > 0. reported_cases Matrix daily reported cholera cases. Must integer. NA allowed. nrow=length(location_name), ncol=length(t). reported_deaths Matrix daily reported cholera deaths. Must integer. NA allowed. nrow=length(location_name), ncol=length(t). epidemic_peaks Optional data.frame observed epidemic peaks consumed peak-timing / peak-magnitude shape terms calc_model_likelihood() (R Python port laser-cholera). Must contain two character columns: iso_code (matching entries location_name) peak_date (ISO YYYY-MM-DD strings parseable .Date). NULL allowed; NULL peak shape terms contribute 0 likelihood. sigfigs Integer; number significant figures round numeric values . Default 4.

what: Create a Reusable MOSAIC Parallel Cluster — make_mosaic_cluster
title: Create a Reusable MOSAIC Parallel Cluster — make_mosaic_cluster
text: Creates properly configured parallel cluster use run_MOSAIC. cluster handles one-time setup: library loading, thread safety, Python/LASER import, root directory propagation. can passed multiple run_MOSAIC calls (e.g. across staged estimation) avoid overhead repeated cluster creation.

title: Create a Reusable MOSAIC Parallel Cluster — make_mosaic_cluster
code: make_mosaic_cluster(n_cores = parallel::detectCores() - 1L, type = "PSOCK")

title: Create a Reusable MOSAIC Parallel Cluster — make_mosaic_cluster
text: n_cores Integer. Number worker processes (default: parallel::detectCores() - 1). type Character. Cluster type: "PSOCK" (default, platforms) "FORK" (Linux/Mac , faster startup).

title: Create a Reusable MOSAIC Parallel Cluster — make_mosaic_cluster
text: cluster object (parallel::makeCluster) ready pass run_MOSAIC(cluster = cl).

title: Create a Reusable MOSAIC Parallel Cluster — make_mosaic_cluster
text: cluster setup includes: Thread environment variables (OMP_NUM_THREADS, MKL_NUM_THREADS, TBB_NUM_THREADS, NUMBA_NUM_THREADS, OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS) set 1 main process worker prevent oversubscription. BLAS threads limited 1 per worker via .mosaic_set_blas_threads(1L). Libraries loaded worker: MOSAIC, reticulate, arrow. laser.cholera Python module imported per worker. Root directory propagated main process via set_root_directory(). caller responsible stopping cluster done: parallel::stopCluster(cl).

title: Create a Reusable MOSAIC Parallel Cluster — make_mosaic_cluster
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(MOSAIC) set_root_directory("~/MOSAIC")  # Create cluster once cl <- make_mosaic_cluster(n_cores = 8)  # Use across multiple calibration stages result_s1 <- run_MOSAIC(config, priors, "./stage_1", control, cluster = cl) result_s2 <- run_MOSAIC(config, priors_s2, "./stage_2", control, cluster = cl)  # Clean up parallel::stopCluster(cl) } # }

what: Build Complete MOSAIC Control Structure — mosaic_control_defaults
title: Build Complete MOSAIC Control Structure — mosaic_control_defaults
text: Creates complete control structure run_mosaic(). primary interface configuring MOSAIC execution settings, consolidating calibration strategy, parameter sampling, parallelization, output options. Parameters organized workflow order: calibration: run (simulations, iterations, batch sizes) sampling: sample (parameters vary) likelihood: score model fit (likelihood components weights) targets: stop (ESS convergence thresholds) parallel: Infrastructure (cores, cluster type) io: Output format (file format, compression) paths: File management (output directories, plots)

title: Build Complete MOSAIC Control Structure — mosaic_control_defaults
code: mosaic_control_defaults(   calibration = NULL,   sampling = NULL,   likelihood = NULL,   targets = NULL,   predictions = NULL,   weights = NULL,   parallel = NULL,   io = NULL,   paths = NULL,   logging = NULL )

title: Build Complete MOSAIC Control Structure — mosaic_control_defaults
text: calibration List calibration settings. Default : n_simulations: NULL auto mode, integer fixed mode n_iterations: Number LASER iterations per simulation (default: 3L) max_simulations_total: Maximum total simulations across phases (default: 100000L) batch_size_adaptive: Simulations per batch Phase 1 adaptive calibration (default: 500L) min_batches_adaptive: Minimum Phase 1 batches convergence check (default: 5L) max_batches_adaptive: Maximum Phase 1 batches (default: 8L) max_batch_predictive: Cap Phase 2 predictive batch (default: 10000L) max_batches_predictive: Maximum Phase 2 predictive batches (default: 10L) target_r2_adaptive: ESS regression R-squared target Phase 1 convergence (default: 0.90) sampling List parameter sampling flags (sample). Default : sample_tau_i: Sample transmission rate (default: TRUE) sample_mobility_gamma: Sample mobility gamma (default: TRUE) sample_mobility_omega: Sample mobility omega (default: TRUE) sample_mu_j: Sample recovery rate (default: TRUE) sample_iota: Sample importation rate (default: TRUE) sample_gamma_2: Sample second dose efficacy (default: TRUE) sample_alpha_1: Sample first dose efficacy (default: TRUE) ... (see mosaic_control_defaults() complete list 38 parameters) likelihood List likelihood calculation settings (score model fit). Default : weight_cases: Weight cases vs deaths (default: 1.0) weight_deaths: Weight deaths vs cases (default: 1.0) weight_wis: WIS regularizer weight (default: 0, try 0.10) ... (see mosaic_control_defaults() complete list) targets List convergence targets (stop). Default : ESS_param: Target ESS per parameter (default: 100) ESS_param_prop: Proportion parameters meeting ESS (default: 0.95) ESS_best: Target subset size ESS within subset (default: 100). A_best: Target agreement index (default: 0.70). Lower values allow top sims dominate. CVw_best: Target CV weights (default: 1.0). Higher values permit sharper discrimination. percentile_min: Minimum percentile best subset search (default: 0.001) ESS_method: ESS calculation method, "kish" "perplexity" (default: "perplexity") predictions List prediction generation settings. Default : n_iter_ensemble: Stochastic runs per posterior parameter set weighted ensemble (default: 10L). Total ensemble sims = N parameter sets x n_iter_ensemble. n_iter_best: Stochastic runs medoid single-config prediction plots (default: 100L). Applied identically models. runs execute internal PSOCK cluster parallel$enable = TRUE. optimize_subset: Logical; TRUE, post-ensemble optimizer (MAE / WIS / R^2+bias) refines best subset used posteriors.json, posterior_quantiles.csv, ensemble object driving downstream plots metrics. tier-selected subset preserved is_best_subset / weight_best columns samples.parquet provenance; optimized selection written new is_best_subset_opt / weight_best_opt columns. FALSE (default), tier-selected subset canonical _opt columns written. Statistical note: enabling flag yields posterior conditioned ensemble predictive performance (MAE / WIS / R^2+bias training data) rather pure likelihood-weighted posterior. optimize_min_n: Minimum subset size optimizer may select (default 30L, raised 4L guard KDE degeneracy posteriors.json). optimize_objective: Objective function, "mae" (default), "r2_bias", "wis". optimize_stride: Integer >= 1 (default 3L). 1L evaluates every candidate subset size min_n:max_n (exhaustive, bit-identical historical search). > 1L runs coarse--refine search evaluates strided grid first refines around best point. default 3L gives roughly 3x speedup negligible accuracy cost smooth score(N) curve (re-examines +/- 3 window around coarse winner); raise (5L/10L/25L) speed, set 1L force exhaustive search. Note may select slightly different optimal_n exhaustive records evaluated N's diagnostics table. (optimize_ensemble_subset function's stride default remains 1L preserve bit-identicality.) central_method: Ensemble central tendency, "median" (default; lower calibration bias) "mean" (unbiased expected counts, never collapses sparse deaths, unmasks implied-CFR bias). Scalar per-channel c(cases=, deaths=). Governs prediction trajectory + plots, canonical *_ensemble R^2/bias metrics, medoid target, subset-selection objective consistently. number parameter sets ensemble determined best subset (sims non-zero importance weights). weights List importance-weight settings. Default : floor: Minimum weight prevent underflow (default: 1e-15). iqr_multiplier: Tukey IQR outlier-detection multiplier (default: 1.5; use 3.0 extreme outliers ). parallel List parallelization settings (infrastructure). Default : enable: Enable parallel execution (default: FALSE) n_cores: Number LOCAL cores (default: 1L). Governs local-machine parallelism : LASER worker count non-Dask path, orchestrator's sampling/parquet PSOCK worker count Dask path. never sets remote Dask/Coiled worker count (dask_spec$n_workers). type: Cluster type, "PSOCK" "FORK" (default: "PSOCK") progress: Show progress bar (default: TRUE) io List /O settings (output format). Default : format: Output format, "parquet" "csv" (default: "parquet") compression: Compression algorithm (default: "zstd") compression_level: Compression level (default: 3L) paths List path output settings (file management). Default : clean_output: Remove output directory exists (default: FALSE) plots: Generate diagnostic plots (default: TRUE) logging List logging settings. Default : verbose: Enable detailed progress messages sub-functions (default: FALSE).

title: Build Complete MOSAIC Control Structure — mosaic_control_defaults
text: complete control list suitable passing run_mosaic().

title: Build Complete MOSAIC Control Structure — mosaic_control_defaults
code: # Default control settings ctrl <- mosaic_control_defaults()  # Quick parallel run with 8 cores ctrl <- mosaic_control_defaults(   parallel = list(enable = TRUE, n_cores = 8) )  # Fixed mode with 5000 simulations, 5 iterations each ctrl <- mosaic_control_defaults(   calibration = list(     n_simulations = 5000,     n_iterations = 5   ) )  # Auto mode with custom settings ctrl <- mosaic_control_defaults(   calibration = list(     n_simulations = NULL,  # NULL = auto mode     n_iterations = 3,     max_simulations_total = 50000,     batch_size_adaptive = 1000   ),   parallel = list(enable = TRUE, n_cores = 16) )  # Only sample specific parameters ctrl <- mosaic_control_defaults(   sampling = list(     sample_tau_i = TRUE,     sample_mobility_gamma = FALSE,     sample_mobility_omega = FALSE,     sample_mu_j_baseline = TRUE,     sample_iota = FALSE,     sample_gamma_2 = FALSE,     sample_alpha_1 = FALSE   ) )  # Enable WIS regularizer and peak timing ctrl <- mosaic_control_defaults(   likelihood = list(     weight_wis = 0.10,     weight_peak_timing = 0.25   ) )  # Use perplexity method for ESS calculations ctrl <- mosaic_control_defaults(   targets = list(ESS_method = "perplexity") )  # Full workflow configuration (demonstrates logical order) ctrl <- mosaic_control_defaults(   calibration = list(n_simulations = NULL, n_iterations = 3),      # How to run   sampling = list(sample_tau_i = TRUE, sample_mu_j_baseline = TRUE), # What to sample   likelihood = list(weight_wis = 0.10, weight_cases = 1.0),        # How to score   targets = list(ESS_param = 100, ESS_param_prop = 0.95),          # When to stop   parallel = list(enable = TRUE, n_cores = 16),                    # Infrastructure   io = mosaic_io_presets("default"),                               # Output format   paths = list(clean_output = FALSE, plots = TRUE)                 # File management )

what: Get an Optimal Dask/Coiled Cluster Spec for a Given Worker Count — mosaic_dask_presets
title: Get an Optimal Dask/Coiled Cluster Spec for a Given Worker Count — mosaic_dask_presets
text: Returns dask_spec list run_MOSAIC sized given number LASER workers (1 500). worker one D2s_v6 VM running one cholera sim time, n_workers also number concurrent simulations number subnet IPs cluster consume. workspace cap (1,000 total vCPUs 2 per worker = 500 workers) sets upper bound.

title: Get an Optimal Dask/Coiled Cluster Spec for a Given Worker Count — mosaic_dask_presets
code: mosaic_dask_presets(n_workers)

title: Get an Optimal Dask/Coiled Cluster Spec for a Given Worker Count — mosaic_dask_presets
text: n_workers Integer 1 500 inclusive. number LASER worker VMs provision. runs one cholera sim concurrently. Non-integer values rounded.

title: Get an Optimal Dask/Coiled Cluster Spec for a Given Worker Count — mosaic_dask_presets
text: Named list ready pass dask_spec argument run_MOSAIC.

title: Get an Optimal Dask/Coiled Cluster Spec for a Given Worker Count — mosaic_dask_presets
text: Routing rules: Worker VM family — always 2-vCPU x86 v6. Three equivalent variants passed Coiled can grab whichever capacity westus2: Standard_D2s_v6 (Intel Emerald Rapids), Standard_D2ds_v6 (Intel local SSD), Standard_D2ads_v6 (AMD Genoa local SSD). 2 vCPU / 8 GiB. cholera sim single-threaded GIL-bound Python, larger VMs idle cores; second vCPU absorbs Dask plumbing (network /O, nanny, heartbeats). Scheduler VM — 5-tier graduation worker count: Scheduler mostly connection / task-graph state, CPU-bound. Finer tiers avoid -provisioning low worker counts keeping RAM headroom high counts. wait_for_workers — absolute number workers must tasks dispatch. Equivalent 80\ pool n_workers <= 250, 90\ Passed integer (fraction) Coiled ambiguity resolve small n_workers. Coiled's fraction default 0.3 aggressive short-task calibrations: first sims dispatch onto partial pool, stall remaining workers join. timeout — cluster-creation timeout seconds. Scales max(1800, 600 + 4 * n_workers). n_workers = 500 ~43 minutes, large enough long tail Azure VM provisioning westus2 (per-VM p99 ~7 min, parallelized free) plus 90\ workspace — defaults "idm-coiled-idmad-r2". Override globally without forking helper setting options(mosaic.coiled_workspace = "-workspace"). Sizing guide (~0.7 s per LASER iteration measured ETH single-location): n_workers = 25-50: smoke tests, 1-5K sims, day--day. n_workers = 125: typical 10K-sim fixed-budget calibrations. n_workers = 250: 24K-sim predictive batches. n_workers = 500: run_10-class jobs (60K+ sims). workspace 1,000-core cap. Notes: worker consumes one Azure subnet IP. idm-coiled-idmad-r2 workspace /23 subnet (~507 usable IPs); contention tenants, large clusters can fail provisioning SubnetIsFull. happens, drop n_workers coordinate workspace admin expand subnet. 8 GiB RAM per worker fine ETH single-location similar small configs. multi-country MOSAIC (>10 locations) may need headroom — override vm_types "Standard_D4s_v6" (16 GiB), accepting cluster's cost-per-sim doubles (idle cores) IP footprint unchanged. Defaults -demand pricing. spot/preemptible VMs (~70\ spot_policy = "spot_with_fallback" ("spot") cluster falls back -demand Azure spot capacity westus2 runs .

title: Get an Optimal Dask/Coiled Cluster Spec for a Given Worker Count — mosaic_dask_presets
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # 10K-sim calibration on 125 workers result <- run_MOSAIC(   config     = get_location_config(iso = "ETH"),   priors     = get_location_priors(iso = "ETH"),   dir_output = "output_eth_10k",   control    = mosaic_control_defaults(     calibration = list(n_simulations = 10000, n_iterations = 3)   ),   dask_spec  = mosaic_dask_presets(n_workers = 125) )  # Max cluster with spot VMs for a long adaptive run spec <- mosaic_dask_presets(500) spec$spot_policy <- "spot_with_fallback"  # Multi-country: override to D4s_v6 for RAM headroom spec <- mosaic_dask_presets(250) spec$vm_types <- c("Standard_D4s_v6")  # 16 GiB / worker, ~2x cost-per-sim } # }

title: Optimize Ensemble Subset Size — optimize_ensemble_subset
text: Post-ensemble optimization evaluates top-N subsets (ranked likelihood) selects N maximizes prediction quality. Produces separate mosaic_ensemble object optimal subset size, leaving original ensemble untouched. candidate size N (min_n full ensemble), function re-computes Gibbs weights within top-N subset, re-computes weighted median predictions 4D arrays, scores selected objective function.

title: Optimize Ensemble Subset Size — optimize_ensemble_subset
code: optimize_ensemble_subset(   ensemble,   likelihoods,   seeds = NULL,   min_n = 30L,   objective = c("mae", "r2_bias", "wis"),   central_method = "median",   stride = 1L,   cl = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Optimize Ensemble Subset Size — optimize_ensemble_subset
text: ensemble mosaic_ensemble object returned calc_model_ensemble. likelihoods Numeric vector log-likelihoods, one per parameter set ensemble. Must length ensemble$n_param_sets. seeds Optional numeric/integer vector simulation seeds aligned likelihoods. supplied, function returns optimal_seeds callers can map optimized subset back original samples.parquet rows without re-deriving internal sort. Separately, ensemble$seeds present (per-member seeds carried calc_model_ensemble, aligned cases_array construction), carried sort/slice exposed ensemble_optimized$seeds cases_array-aligned consumers medoid selection. argument ensemble$seeds serve different roles kept independent. min_n Minimum subset size evaluate. Default 30L guard small-subset KDE degeneracy optimized subset drives posterior artifacts. Fox et al. (2024) report 4 statistical minimum accuracy, posterior density estimation needs larger N. objective Scoring function: "mae" (default, normalized MAE), "r2_bias" (R-squared plus bias penalty), "wis" (normalized Weighted Interval Score). central_method Central tendency used summarize per-cell ensemble distribution "mae" "r2_bias" objectives recorded R^2/bias/MAE diagnostics: "median" (default, reproduces historical selection) "mean" (weighted mean, unbiased expected counts). Scalar per-channel c(cases=, deaths=). "wis" objective quantile-based unaffected setting (point forecast remains weighted median). default "median" deliberate – preserves historical direct-call selection Tier-2 bit--bit parity guarantee; run_MOSAIC() passes resolved control$predictions$central_method (package default "median" v0.46.1) explicitly. stride Integer >= 1. 1L (default) evaluates every N min_n:max_n (exhaustive, bit-identical parity path). > 1L enables coarse--refine two-stage search (see Details). Opt-; changes N's evaluated may select slightly different optimal_n exhaustive search. cl Optional parallel cluster (make_mosaic_cluster parallel::makeCluster) used split per-cell evaluation kernel across workers. NULL (default) runs serially. supplied, result bit--bit identical serial path. Must "PSOCK" cluster (FORK unsafe point pipeline). trivially small problems function falls back serial regardless cl. verbose Logical; TRUE, emit progress messages.

title: Optimize Ensemble Subset Size — optimize_ensemble_subset
text: S3 object class mosaic_subset_optimization containing: evaluation_table Data frame one row per N evaluated. stride > 1L contains coarse + refine N's, sorted N, every N min_n:max_n. optimal_n Selected subset size. optimal_score Score optimal N. optimal_weights Re-computed Gibbs weights optimal subset. optimal_indices Integer indices original ensemble arrays. optimal_seeds Simulation seeds optimal subset likelihood-sorted order, aligned optimal_weights (seeds supplied), else NULL. ensemble_optimized Complete mosaic_ensemble object optimal N. $seeds field per-member seed aligned cases_array (member--seed aligned) ensemble$seeds present, else falls back optimal_seeds. stability_flag TRUE score profile flat. diagnostics_n Original diagnostics-selected N. diagnostics_score Score diagnostics-selected N. objective objective used. central_method Resolved per-channel central tendency used "mae"/"r2_bias" scoring recorded diagnostics.

title: Optimize Ensemble Subset Size — optimize_ensemble_subset
text: evaluation loop organized cell-outer: [location, time] cell's finite predictions gathered scored across every candidate N (gather depend N), filling per-cell vector central tendencies. default call path (stride = 1L, cl = NULL) exhaustive serial guaranteed bit--bit identical historical implementation (Tier-2 parity). Two opt-speed levers available: PSOCK parallelism (cl): n_locations *     n_time_points cells split across supplied cluster's workers. result bit--bit identical serial path (worker runs kernel disjoint block cells; master scatters per-cell central/quantile values back [, j] positions). FORK clusters unsafe (native reticulate/numba threads loaded post-calibration point); pass "PSOCK" cluster. Stride--refine (stride): stride > 1L, coarse grid seq(min_n, max_n, = stride) evaluated first locate best N*, refinement bracket (N*-stride):(N*+stride) evaluated, selection logic runs combined (coarse + refine) rows. bit-identical exhaustive path: evaluation_table contains evaluated N's (fewer rows), selected optimal_n may differ slightly exhaustive optimum score profile non-monotone coarse points. trade roughly O(stride) reduction number N's evaluated.

title: Optimize Ensemble Subset Size — optimize_ensemble_subset
text: Bracher J et al. (2021). Evaluating epidemic forecasts interval format. PLOS Computational Biology, 17(2), e1008618. Gneiting T & Raftery AE (2007). Strictly Proper Scoring Rules, Prediction, Estimation. JASA, 102(477), 359–378.

title: Plot Ensemble Predictions from a mosaic_ensemble Object — plot_model_ensemble
text: ensemble mosaic_ensemble object returned calc_model_ensemble. output_dir Character. Directory plots saved (CSVs, data_dir provided). Created exist. data_dir Character. Directory per-location prediction CSVs written save_predictions = TRUE. Defaults output_dir backwards compatibility. Pass separate path (e.g. 3_results/predictions) keep CSVs figures tree. Created exist. file_prefix Character. Prefix used output filenames: predictions_<prefix>_<LOC>.pdf/csv per-location outputs predictions_<prefix>_cases_all.pdf / _deaths_all.pdf multi-location overview plots. Default "ensemble". title_label Character. Leading label used plot titles ("<title_label>: <LOC>"). Default "Posterior Ensemble". save_predictions Logical. Save per-location prediction CSVs. Default FALSE. CSVs carry predicted_central (plotted series), predicted_mean, predicted_median, central_method column choice never ambiguous. central_method Central tendency plotted/scored line: "median" (default; lower calibration bias) "mean" (unbiased expected counts, never collapses sparse deaths, unmasks implied-CFR bias). Scalar per-channel c(cases=, deaths=). mask_final_deaths_step Logical. TRUE (default), blank FINAL timestep every Deaths prediction (set predicted/CI cells NA) exported CSV rendered lines. masks laser-cholera engine --one reported_deaths written [tick] array length nticks + 1, final slot never written reads artificial drop--zero. DISPLAY : underlying ensemble arrays untouched, R2/bias/likelihood computed upstream raw object unaffected. Cases written [tick + 1] affected. n_cases_warmup_mask Integer. Number LEADING timesteps every Suspected Cases prediction blank (set NA) exported CSV rendered lines. Default 2L. masks initial-condition warm-transient (seeded E/progressing new_symptomatic SEIR dynamics settle), visually dominant low-count countries. DISPLAY (raw arrays untouched). legitimate leading reporting-lag zeros Deaths (delta_reporting_deaths) REAL masked argument. Set 0L disable. score_idx_cases, score_idx_deaths Integer (1-based). Per-channel scored time-window START index (burn-/ deaths-era start). Leading timesteps strictly index blanked (set NA) exported CSV rendered lines plot shows scored window. Default 1L (blanking). NULL (typical caller pattern) value read ensemble$artifact_mask$score_idx_* plots automatically track scored window ensemble built . DISPLAY (raw arrays untouched). verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default TRUE.

title: Plot Parameter Sensitivity Ranking (HSIC) — plot_model_parameter_sensitivity
text: Computes R²-HSIC (Hilbert-Schmidt Independence Criterion) sampled parameter log-likelihood, plots ranked horizontal bar chart. HSIC measures statistical dependence without assuming linearity, monotonicity, independence among inputs, making valid importance-weighted posterior samples parameters correlated (Da Veiga 2015).

title: Plot Parameter Sensitivity Ranking (HSIC) — plot_model_parameter_sensitivity
code: plot_model_parameter_sensitivity(   results_file,   priors_file = NULL,   output_dir = ".",   max_params = 30,   n_samples = 2000,   kernel = "rbf",   test_method = "Asymptotic",   subset_col = "is_best_subset",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Plot Parameter Sensitivity Ranking (HSIC) — plot_model_parameter_sensitivity
text: results_file Path samples.parquet file containing calibration results. priors_file Path priors.json (used parameter descriptions labels). output_dir Directory write output figure CSV. max_params Maximum number parameters display. Default 30. n_samples Maximum number posterior draws use. Samples drawn replacement using importance weights. Default 2000. kernel Kernel type input parameters passed sensiHSIC. One "rbf" (default), "laplace", "dcov". test_method Significance test method. "Asymptotic" (default, fast, requires n >= 100) "Permutation" (exact, slower). subset_col Character name boolean subset-membership column used fallback importance-weight ESS degenerate. Defaults "is_best_subset". Pass "is_best_subset_opt" read optimizer-refined subset. verbose Print progress messages.

title: Plot Parameter Sensitivity Ranking (HSIC) — plot_model_parameter_sensitivity
text: data.frame columns parameter, hsic_r2, p_value, sig, description (invisibly). Writes PNG CSV output_dir.

title: Plot Parameter Sensitivity Ranking (HSIC) — plot_model_parameter_sensitivity
text: R²-HSIC index equals zero parameter log-likelihood statistically independent (RBF kernel), equals one log-likelihood deterministic function parameter alone. Significance assessed via asymptotic Gamma approximation null distribution (valid n >= 100). Bars coloured significance level.

title: Plot Parameter Sensitivity Ranking (HSIC) — plot_model_parameter_sensitivity
text: Da Veiga S (2015). Global sensitivity analysis dependence measures. Journal Statistical Computation Simulation 85(7):1283-1305. https://arxiv.org/abs/1311.2483 Gretton , Bousquet O, Smola , Scholkopf B (2005). Measuring statistical dependence Hilbert-Schmidt norms. Algorithmic Learning Theory, LNCS 3734:63-77.

text: PATHS Optional list containing output paths. provided, must include DOCS_FIGURES plot saved. symp_range numeric vector length 2 indicating lower upper shedding-duration bound (days) symptomatic infections. Default 95% credible interval canonical lognormal prior gamma_1 ~ Lognormal(-2.303, 0.5), .e. c(3.8, 26.6) days. asymp_range numeric vector length 2 indicating lower upper shedding-duration bound (days) asymptomatic infections. Default 95% credible interval canonical lognormal prior gamma_2 ~ Lognormal(-0.693, 0.4), .e. c(0.9, 4.4) days. symp_central single numeric giving central duration (typically median) symptomatic infections. Default 10 days (median gamma_1 prior). vertical solid line plot drawn . NA, falls back arithmetic midpoint symp_range. asymp_central single numeric giving central duration (typically median) asymptomatic infections. Default 2 days (median gamma_2 prior).

Default TRUE. eval Optional precomputed evaluate_rolling_cv result ($cells) source annotations ; recomputed NULL. title, subtitle, caption Optional plot labels; sensible defaults derived artifact NULL. dir_output Directory write figures . text: function generates plot visualizes suitability-dependent decay rate environmental shedding based climate-driven environmental suitability (\(\psi_{jt}\)). function generates three models decay rate \(\delta_{jt}\): \(\delta_{jt} = 1 - \psi_{jt}\): simple linear transformation \(\psi_{jt}\). \(\delta_{jt} = 1 - \frac{1}{1 + (1 - \psi_{jt})}\): nonlinear transformation \(\psi_{jt}\). \(\delta_{jt} = \delta_{\min} + \psi_{jt} \cdot (\delta_{\max} - \delta_{\min})\): linear transformation based minimum maximum decay rates. plot saved PNG file directory specified PATHS$DOCS_FIGURES.

title: Plot Vaccination Maps — plot_vaccination_maps
text: function performs following steps: Data Loading Processing: Reads vaccination data CSV file, converts date column Date objects, extracts latest cumulative doses distributed corresponding vaccination proportions country. Map Data Retrieval: Obtains map data African countries using rnaturalearth package. Data Merging: Merges vaccination data map data, identifying Sub-Saharan African countries based ISO codes provided MOSAIC::iso_codes_ssa dataset. Color Scale Definition: Defines distinct color scales cumulative doses distributed (green gradient) proportion vaccinated (purple gradient), including appropriate breaks labels. Choropleth Map Creation: Creates two choropleth maps—one cumulative doses distributed another proportion vaccinated—using ggplot2 layering map data accordingly. Barplot Generation: Constructs barplots metric, ordering countries descending values applying corresponding color scales. Plot Arrangement: Utilizes patchwork cowplot packages arrange maps barplots structured grid panel labels "" "B" adds footnote. Saving Plot: Saves final combined plot PNG file specified DOCS_FIGURES directory.

what: Plot Vibrio Decay Rate as a Function of Environmental Suitability — plot_vibrio_decay_rate
title: Plot Vibrio Decay Rate as a Function of Environmental Suitability — plot_vibrio_decay_rate
text: function generates plot visualizes suitability-dependent decay rate V. cholerae survival based climate-driven environmental suitability (\(\psi_{jt}\)). figure compares five transformation types survival time using cumulative Beta distribution: Linear: \(f(\psi_{jt}) = \text{pbeta}(\psi_{jt} \mid s_1 = 1, s_2 = 1)\) Concave: \(f(\psi_{jt}) = \text{pbeta}(\psi_{jt} \mid s_1 = 1, s_2 = 5)\) Convex: \(f(\psi_{jt}) = \text{pbeta}(\psi_{jt} \mid s_1 = 5, s_2 = 1)\) Sigmoidal: \(f(\psi_{jt}) = \text{pbeta}(\psi_{jt} \mid s_1 = 5, s_2 = 5)\) Arcsine: \(f(\psi_{jt}) = \text{pbeta}(\psi_{jt} \mid s_1 = 0.5, s_2 = 0.5)\)

what: Default prior distributions for MOSAIC model parameters — priors_default
title: Default prior distributions for MOSAIC model parameters — priors_default
text: list informative prior distributions MOSAIC model parameters, organised global (single-value) priors location-specific (per-country) priors 40 SSA / N-Africa countries. Built data-raw/make_priors_default.R (see script's block comments literature sources derivation prior). Current version: priors_default$metadata$version.

title: Default prior distributions for MOSAIC model parameters — priors_default
code: priors_default

title: Default prior distributions for MOSAIC model parameters — priors_default
text: list 3 main components. metadata Version, build date, free-text description listing changes produced version priors. parameters_global Named list 27 global priors (single value shared across locations). See Global parameters . parameters_location Named list 22 location-specific prior families. entry holds location sublist keyed ISO3 country code, e.g. parameters_location$beta_j0_tot$location$ETH gives prior Ethiopia's total baseline transmission rate. leaf entry shape list(description, distribution, parameters) parameters holds distribution-specific hyperparameters (e.g. shape1/shape2 beta, meanlog/sdlog lognormal, mean/sd//b truncated normal).

title: Default prior distributions for MOSAIC model parameters — priors_default
text: Transmission FOI structure: alpha_1 – FOI mixing exponent (/N) (Beta; 0 = mixing, 1 = well-mixed). alpha_2 – Exponent N_jt FOI denominator (Beta). 1 = frequency-dependent transmission; 0 = density-dependent. kappa – Half-saturation V. cholerae concentration environmental dose-response 50\ capacity). Environmental persistence (V. cholerae decay water): decay_days_short – Short-end decay timescale (Truncnorm, days). decay_days_spread – Spread used derive decay_days_long = decay_days_short + decay_days_spread (Truncnorm). decay_days_long derived sampling time first-class prior. decay_shape_1, decay_shape_2 – Truncnorm shape coefficients Beta-shaped mapping suitability $\psi$ decay rate (natural-infection immunity decay). Disease progression (rates, per day): iota – Incubation rate E -> (Lognormal; rate, period). Prior median ~0.71/day. gamma_1 – Symptomatic shedding-duration rate I_1 -> R (Lognormal). Prior median 0.1/day (~10-day shedding); 95\ ~3.75 - 26.6 days. gamma_2 – Asymptomatic shedding-duration rate I_2 -> R (Lognormal). Prior median 0.5/day (~2-day shedding); 95\ ~0.91 - 4.39 days. epsilon – Natural-infection immunity waning rate R -> S (Lognormal). Prior mean 3.9e-4/day -> ~7-yr immunity (King et al. 2008 + project's two-cohort re-fit). Vaccination (OCV): phi_1, phi_2 – One-dose / two-dose vaccine effectiveness moment dose delivery (Beta). omega_1, omega_2 – One-dose / two-dose vaccine-induced immunity waning rates (Gamma, per day). Symptom expression, care-seeking, surveillance: sigma – Fraction infections symptomatic (Beta). rho – Care-seeking rate: probability symptomatic individual presents surveillance (Beta; reporting fraction). rho_deaths – Surveillance capture rate true cholera deaths (Beta; random-effects meta-analysis Routh 2017, Shikanga 2009, Bwire 2013 — see MOSAIC-pkg/claude/rho_deaths_research/SYNTHESIS_REPORT.md). Consumed laser-cholera v0.13+ deaths likelihood: observed surveillance reported_deaths compared simulated reported_deaths = round(disease_deaths * rho_deaths) (lagged delta_reporting_deaths). Production prior recommended prediction-interval variant Beta(6.30, 8.52); informative variant Beta(36.95, 51.02) retained sensitivity per SYNTHESIS_REPORT sec 3.4. chi_endemic, chi_epidemic – Positive predictive value among suspected cases endemic vs epidemic phases (Beta). delta_reporting_cases – Symptom-onset--surveillance-report delay (Truncnorm, days). infection--report – incubation handled separately E compartment iota. delta_reporting_deaths – Death-event--death-report delay days (Truncnorm). time true cholera death appearance surveillance reports. symptom-onset--death interval implicit SEIR dynamics (\(\gamma_1^{-1}\) ~ 5-7 days symptomatic recovery/death). Consumed laser-cholera v0.13+ infectious.py:88-92 reported_deaths[t] = round(disease_deaths[t-delta] * rho_deaths). Environmental shedding intensity (V. cholerae cells per person per day): zeta_1 – Symptomatic shedding rate (Lognormal). zeta_2 – Asymptomatic shedding rate (Lognormal). Also first-class prior, typically derived sampling time zeta_1 / zeta_ratio rather drawn independently. zeta_ratio – zeta_1 / zeta_2 (Lognormal; ~5-OOM literature spread retained intentionally). Mobility (gravity-model exponents): mobility_gamma, mobility_omega – Distance-decay population-scaling exponents (Gamma).

title: Default prior distributions for MOSAIC model parameters — priors_default
text: carries per-iso prior parameters_location$<param>$location$<ISO3>. Distribution family across isos given parameter (noted ); hyperparameters differ. Transmission contact: beta_j0_tot – Total baseline transmission rate per location (Lognormal). beta_j0_hum beta_j0_env derived p_beta * beta_j0_tot (1 - p_beta) * beta_j0_tot; neither directly sampled. p_beta – Fraction total transmission attributable direct human--human contact (Beta). tau_i – Daily departure probability per location, gravity model (Beta). theta_j – WASH-coverage fraction per location (Beta). Seasonality (Fourier coefficients, fit cases): a_1_j, a_2_j, b_1_j, b_2_j – Annual + semi-annual Fourier coefficients (Normal). Earlier doc versions erroneously labelled b_1/b_2 "deaths" – four fitted cases. Initial conditions (per-compartment proportions population t=0): prop_S_initial, prop_V1_initial, prop_V2_initial, prop_E_initial, prop_I_initial, prop_R_initial – Beta priors per iso. Generated est_initial_S/V1_V2/E_I/R() demography, surveillance history, OCV campaigns, etc. Disease-mortality: mu_j_baseline – Per-iso baseline CFR rate (Gamma). mu_j_slope – Per-iso slope coupling CFR outbreak intensity (Gamma). mu_j_epidemic_factor – Per-iso multiplier applied mu_j_baseline epidemic phase detected (Gamma). Phase switching: epidemic_threshold – Per-iso I_sym / N threshold chi_epidemic mu_j_epidemic_factor engage (Truncnorm). Environmental-suitability calibration (used LSTM $\psi$ pipeline): psi_star_a – Gain logit-scale suitability score (Truncnorm). psi_star_b – Logit-scale offset (Normal). psi_star_z – Smoothing weight causal EWMA (Beta). psi_star_k – Time-offset (lag) parameter (Truncnorm).

title: Default prior distributions for MOSAIC model parameters — priors_default
text: Location-specific parameters cover 40 SSA / North-African countries (ISO3 codes): AGO, BDI, BEN, BFA, BWA, CAF, CIV, CMR, COD, COG, ERI, ETH, GAB, GHA, GIN, GMB, GNB, GNQ, KEN, LBR, MLI, MOZ, MRT, MWI, NAM, NER, NGA, RWA, SEN, SLE, SOM, SSD, SWZ, TCD, TGO, TZA, UGA, ZAF, ZMB, ZWE.
