title: Process AI-mined Weekly Cholera Surveillance Data into the MOSAIC Per-Source Schema — process_AI_cholera_data
text: Keep--weight filter. AI repo tags row disaggregation_method. keep: "observed" — direct weekly observations extracted reports. "fourier_*" — historical annual/quarterly totals disaggregated weekly via seasonal template. carry lower confidence_weight (typically ~0.5) downstream consumers can -weight rather treat direct observations. "documented_zero" — confirmed-absence weeks. flow merge fill genuine gaps surveillance combiner's priority de-duplication keeps higher-priority sources overlap. drop "assumed_zero" (default-zero assumptions evidentiary basis) / missing method tag. source column raw AI file tracks agent's collection method, data origin, intentionally carried ; process_cholera_surveillance_data() labels every row file source "AI" priority purposes. CRLF line endings raw files leave trailing carriage return last column (disaggregation_method); stripped read.

title: Process EM-DAT Flood Events Into a Country-Week Panel — process_EMDAT_data
text: Invisibly returns path written CSV. CSV contains one row per (iso_code, year, week) every ISO-week date_start (default 2000-01-03) latest week covered flood event raw extract, restricted iso_codes_mosaic. Columns: iso_code ISO3 country code. year ISO-8601 year week. week ISO-8601 week number (1-53). date_start Monday ISO-week. date_stop Sunday ISO-week. emdat_flood_active 1 flood event overlaps week, else 0. emdat_flood_new Number flood events start week. emdat_flood_affected log1p summed Total Affected across overlapping events (NAs treated 0). emdat_flood_deaths log1p summed Total Deaths across overlapping events (NAs treated 0).

title: Process EM-DAT Flood Events Into a Country-Week Panel — process_EMDAT_data
text: EM-DAT records carry frequent missing day--month occasional missing end-month values. Imputation rules: Missing Start Day End Day -> 15. Missing End Month -> Start Month. Missing End Year -> Start Year. resulting end_date earlier start_date (data-entry artifact), reset start_date. event expanded unique set ISO-8601 (year, week) pairs touched daily date range start_date:end_date. panel grid full Cartesian product iso_codes_mosaic ISO-weeks date_start latest event end-week; cells flood activity carry zeros, downstream left-join suitability table need impute missingness. EM-DAT records events meeting CRED's inclusion thresholds (>=10 deaths, >=100 affected, formal emergency declaration), covariate captures major flood occurrences rather continuous flood intensity. richer continuous signal lives OpenMeteo precipitation/anomaly covariates.

title: Download, Process, and Save Historical Annual WHO Cholera Data for Africa — process_WHO_annual_data
text: Compiles cholera surveillance data African countries (AFRO region) 1949 onward. Data sources precise per-year coverage tracked pipeline partial-year snapshots (current year Global Cholera & AWD dashboard) usable downstream without mislabeled full-year totals.

text: function return value writes processed CSVs PATHS$DATA_WHO_ANNUAL.

title: Download, Process, and Save Historical Annual WHO Cholera Data for Africa — process_WHO_annual_data
text: Data sources: 1949-2021: annual reports compiled World Data. See https://ourworldindata.org. 2022: Manually transcribed 2022 annual cholera report (PDF). 2023+: Downloaded Global Cholera & AWD dashboard via ArcGIS. download archived raw//annual/who_global_dashboard/cholera_adm0_public_snapshot_YYYY-MM-DD.csv files matching cholera_adm0_public_*.csv dashboard directory ingested. row's actual coverage derived first_epiwk / last_epiwk (rolling snapshots cross calendar-year boundaries – see year-labeling note ). Year labeling: ArcGIS dashboard returns one row per country summing case_total / death_total first_epiwk last_epiwk. row assigned calendar year contains majority days. coverage_days reports many calendar days covered, year_fraction = coverage_days/365.25 indicates whether row full-year observation (~1.0) partial snapshot (<1.0). multiple files row (iso_code, year) combination, row larger coverage_days kept. refresh given calendar year: place year-filtered CSV raw//annual/who_global_dashboard/ named cholera_adm0_public_YYYY.csv (downloaded dashboard UI year filter applied). Re-run process_WHO_annual_data(). Outputs (PATHS$DATA_WHO_ANNUAL): who_afro_annual.csv – current full series, columns country, iso_code, region, year, cases_total,         cases_imported, deaths_total, cfr, cfr_lo, cfr_hi, first_epiwk,         last_epiwk, coverage_days, year_fraction, source. Intermediate per-source slices traceability.

what: Process Combined Weekly and Daily Cholera Surveillance Data with Truly Square Data Structure — process_cholera_surveillance_data
title: Process Combined Weekly and Daily Cholera Surveillance Data with Truly Square Data Structure — process_cholera_surveillance_data
text: function reads processed weekly cholera data , JHU, supplemental (SUPP) sources, labels record source, combines (removing duplicate country-week entries according specified source preference), creates truly square data structure country-week combinations min max date across entire dataset, downscales combined weekly totals daily time series using MOSAIC::downscale_weekly_values integer allocation.

title: Process Combined Weekly and Daily Cholera Surveillance Data with Truly Square Data Structure — process_cholera_surveillance_data
code: process_cholera_surveillance_data(PATHS, include_ai = FALSE)

title: Process Combined Weekly and Daily Cholera Surveillance Data with Truly Square Data Structure — process_cholera_surveillance_data
text: PATHS list file paths. Must include: DATA_WHO_WEEKLY: Directory containing cholera_country_weekly_processed.csv . DATA_JHU_WEEKLY: Directory containing cholera_country_weekly_processed.csv JHU. DATA_SUPP_WEEKLY: Directory containing cholera_country_weekly_processed.csv supplemental source (may include extra columns). DATA_CHOLERA_WEEKLY: Directory combined weekly output saved. DATA_CHOLERA_DAILY: Directory combined daily output saved. include_ai Logical (default FALSE). TRUE, reads AI-mined processed file (DATA_AI_WEEKLY/cholera_country_weekly_processed.csv, produced process_AI_cholera_data) fourth source, using per-row confidence_weight disaggregation_method. FALSE, /JHU/SUPP merged. include_ai = TRUE AI file missing/empty, warning emitted merge proceeds three direct sources. output always carries confidence_weight disaggregation_method column (stable schema regardless flag): direct-source observations (/JHU/SUPP) get confidence_weight = 1.0 (fully trusted) disaggregation_method = NA; AI rows keep per-row values; square-grid cells observation get confidence_weight = NA.

title: Process Combined Weekly and Daily Cholera Surveillance Data with Truly Square Data Structure — process_cholera_surveillance_data
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Side effects: Reads weekly CSVs three sources adds source column. Cleans rows missing key grouping fields (iso_code, year, week). Harmonizes columns taking union across sources, NA-filling column missing given source (source-specific columns never silently dropped). Deduplicates iso_code date_start (actual ISO-week Monday, robust year-boundary week-1 collisions) using fixed priority > JHU > AI > SUPP completeness tie-break. Creates truly square data structure country-week combinations min max date (missing data = NA). Saves combined weekly data PATHS$DATA_CHOLERA_WEEKLY/cholera_surveillance_weekly_combined.csv. Applies trust-tier gate downscaling: weeks tagged disaggregation_method assumed_zero (surveillance silence, pure assumption) NA-blanked. observed, documented_zero, direct /JHU/SUPP rows, fourier_* (synthetic reconstructions real annual/quarterly totals) reach daily fit target carrying per-week confidence_weight (lower fourier, ~0.4-0.5), calc_model_likelihood() consumes per-observation weight – low-confidence reconstructed weeks inform fit reduced weight rather dropped. Downscales weekly cases deaths daily counts, preserving square structure (keeping days NA), carries source, disaggregation_method, confidence_weight daily row (weight replicated constant across week, never divided). Saves combined daily data PATHS$DATA_CHOLERA_DAILY/cholera_surveillance_daily_combined.csv.

title: Process Combined Weekly and Daily Cholera Surveillance Data with Truly Square Data Structure — process_cholera_surveillance_data
text: Duplicate country-week entries across sources resolved fixed priority > JHU > AI > SUPP, completeness tie-break: within key, rows complete cases deaths preferred source priority applied.

title: Run MOSAIC Calibration Workflow — run_MOSAIC
text: Complete Bayesian calibration workflow full control model specification. function accepts pre-configured config priors objects, allowing: Completely custom configs (non-standard locations, custom data) Fine-grained control model parameters Testing synthetic configurations Executes full MOSAIC calibration workflow: Adaptive calibration R-squared convergence detection Predictive batches model-based sizing ESS re-evaluation Post-hoc subset optimization Posterior quantile distribution estimation Posterior predictive checks uncertainty quantification

title: Run MOSAIC Calibration Workflow — run_MOSAIC
code: run_MOSAIC(   config,   priors,   dir_output,   control = NULL,   resume = FALSE,   cluster = NULL,   dask_spec = NULL )  run_mosaic(   config,   priors,   dir_output,   control = NULL,   resume = FALSE,   cluster = NULL,   dask_spec = NULL )

title: Run MOSAIC Calibration Workflow — run_MOSAIC
text: config Named list LASER model configuration (REQUIRED). Contains location_name, reported_cases, reported_deaths, model parameters. Create custom data obtain via get_location_config(). priors Named list prior distributions (REQUIRED). Contains distribution specifications parameters. Create custom obtain via get_location_priors(). dir_output Character. Output directory calibration run (REQUIRED). results saved . Must unique per run. control Control list created mosaic_control_defaults(). NULL, uses defaults. Controls calibration strategy, parameter sampling, parallelization, output settings. Key settings: calibration$n_simulations: NULL auto mode, integer fixed mode calibration$n_iterations: LASER iterations per simulation (default: 3) calibration$max_simulations_total: Maximum total simulations (default: 100000) sampling: parameters sample vs hold fixed parallel: Cluster settings parallel execution resume Logical (default FALSE). TRUE, run reconstructs calibration state per-sim shards already present <dir_output>/2_calibration/samples/ continues instead starting fresh. shards disk sole source truth (internal resume_checkpoint.rds restores adaptive ESS/phase state present, removed run completes; otherwise state reconstructed shards). Behaviour mode: adaptive (auto) mode run continues max(sim_id)+1 backfill interior gaps (lost/quarantined shard reduces pool); fixed mode missing id within target re-run exact target met. Resume rejected (hard error) : control$paths$clean_output = TRUE (wipe delete shards); run already completed – consolidated 2_calibration/samples.parquet exists -disk shards shrink (start fresh, remove recompute); supplied config, priors, control$likelihood, control$sampling, control$calibration$n_iterations, calibration mode (auto vs fixed) differ persisted 1_inputs/ (changes draws likelihood, making pool incomparable); current laser-cholera engine version crosses v0.13 deaths-scale boundary relative original run; likelihood-value provenance differs – .e. existing shards scored different likelihood engine implementation current session produce (e.g. R calc_model_likelihood local backend vs -worker Python scoring Dask backend, R likelihood-code change altered values). engine version determined (record / Python bound) deaths-scale check skipped warning. effect shards exist (equivalent fresh run). cluster Optional pre-built R parallel cluster make_mosaic_cluster. used dask_spec = NULL. provided, skips cluster creation teardown, reusing existing workers. Useful staged estimation multiple run_MOSAIC calls share cluster. dask_spec provided argument used; caller retains ownership cluster passed responsible stopping run_MOSAIC returns. dask_spec Optional named list specifying Dask/Coiled cluster. provided, simulations dispatched Dask workers instead local R parallel cluster. Required fields: type ("coiled" "scheduler"). Coiled fields: n_workers, software, workspace (required multi-workspace Coiled accounts), vm_types, scheduler_vm_types, region, idle_timeout. Scheduler fields: address. Optional Coiled pass-fields: timeout, environ, scheduler_disk_size, worker_disk_size, scheduler_options, worker_options, spot_policy, host_setup_script. NULL (default) local R parallel backend used.

title: Run MOSAIC Calibration Workflow — run_MOSAIC
text: Invisibly returns list : dirs Named list output directories files Named list key output files summary Named list run statistics (batches, sims, converged, runtime)

title: Run MOSAIC Calibration Workflow — run_MOSAIC
text: See mosaic_control_defaults() complete documentation. control structure contains: calibration n_simulations, n_iterations, max_simulations_total, batch_size_adaptive, etc. sampling sample_tau_i, sample_mobility_gamma, sample_mu_j, etc. parallel enable, n_cores, type, progress paths clean_output, plots targets ESS_param, ESS_best, A_best, CVw_best, etc. io format, compression, compression_level

title: Run MOSAIC Calibration Workflow — run_MOSAIC
text: Results organized structured directory tree: 1_inputs/: Configuration files (JSON format) 2_calibration/samples/: Simulation results (Parquet format) 2_calibration/diagnostics/: ESS metrics, convergence results 2_calibration/posterior/: Posterior quantiles distributions 3_results/figures/: Diagnostic, parameter, prediction plots 3_results/: Final combined results

title: Run MOSAIC Calibration Workflow — run_MOSAIC
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # === BASIC CUSTOM CONFIG ===  # Load and modify default config config <- get_location_config(iso = "ETH") config$population_size <- 1000000  priors <- get_location_priors(iso = "ETH")  run_MOSAIC(   config = config,   priors = priors,   dir_output = "./output" )  # === MULTI-LOCATION WITH CUSTOM CONTROL ===  config <- get_location_config(iso = c("ETH", "KEN", "TZA")) priors <- get_location_priors(iso = c("ETH", "KEN", "TZA"))  ctrl <- mosaic_control_defaults(   calibration = list(     n_simulations = 5000,  # Fixed mode     n_iterations = 5   ),   parallel = list(enable = TRUE, n_cores = 16) )  run_MOSAIC(config, priors, "./output", ctrl)  # === CUSTOM PRIORS FOR SENSITIVITY ===  config <- get_location_config(iso = "ETH") priors <- get_location_priors(iso = "ETH")  # Tighten transmission rate prior priors$tau_i$shape <- 20 priors$tau_i$rate <- 4  run_MOSAIC(config, priors, "./output")  # === COMPLETELY CUSTOM CONFIG ===  # Non-standard location names custom_config <- list(   location_name = c("Region1", "Region2"),   reported_cases = my_cases_data,   reported_deaths = my_deaths_data,   # ... all other LASER parameters )  custom_priors <- list(   # ... custom prior specifications )  run_MOSAIC(custom_config, custom_priors, "./output")  # === RESUME AN INTERRUPTED RUN ===  # If a calibration is killed mid-run, re-call with resume = TRUE and the same # config/priors/output directory. Completed simulations on disk are reused and # the run continues from the next sim_id (bit-identical to an uninterrupted run). run_MOSAIC(config, priors, "./output", resume = TRUE) } # }

what: Rolling-Window Forecast Validation for the MOSAIC Transmission Model — run_rolling_cv
title: Rolling-Window Forecast Validation for the MOSAIC Transmission Model — run_rolling_cv
text: Runs expanding-window (fixed-anchor) rolling-origin backtest MOSAIC transmission model. cutoff date T (1) trains environmental-suitability (psi) LSTM data T, (2) injects psi config, (3) calibrates transmission model observed cases T, (4) projects forward --sample (OOS) window.

title: Rolling-Window Forecast Validation for the MOSAIC Transmission Model — run_rolling_cv
code: run_rolling_cv(   PATHS,   iso,   n_cutoffs = 12L,   latest_cutoff = NULL,   step_months = 1L,   horizons_months = c(1, 3, 5),   embargo_weeks = 1L,   base_config = MOSAIC::config_default,   priors = MOSAIC::priors_default,   control = NULL,   optimize_subset = TRUE,   models = c("ensemble", "ensemble_opt", "medoid"),   n_reps_best_medoid = 50L,   central_method = "median",   est_suitability_spec = list(),   dask_spec = NULL,   dir_output,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Rolling-Window Forecast Validation for the MOSAIC Transmission Model — run_rolling_cv
text: PATHS Path list get_paths. iso Character; one ISO3 code vector (coupled metapopulation). n_cutoffs Integer; number monthly cutoffs (default 12). latest_cutoff Date/character NULL; -recent cutoff. NULL, computed (last scorable observed date) - embargo - max(horizons). step_months Integer; months cutoffs (default 1). horizons_months Numeric vector forecast horizons months (default c(1,3,5)); used set projection length label OOS points. largest horizon bounds latest cutoff. embargo_weeks Integer; gap stop (T) OOS start (default 1). base_config MOSAIC config (default MOSAIC::config_default); window defines anchor projection span. priors Priors list (default MOSAIC::priors_default). control run_MOSAIC control list, NULL experiment-grade cheap default (fixed n_simulations, plots ). See mosaic_control_defaults. optimize_subset Logical (default TRUE); enable run_MOSAIC's post-ensemble best-subset optimizer (control$predictions$optimize_subset). TRUE harness sets resolved control every cutoff, ensemble re-scored training-window observed series posterior driven optimizer-selected subset. Set FALSE use raw candidate ensemble. models Character vector model types score carry predictions.parquet (default c("ensemble","ensemble_opt","medoid")). "ensemble" (posterior-weighted candidate) always included. "ensemble_opt" optimizer-selected subset (emitted optimize_subset =   TRUE ensemble_optimized.rds exists). "medoid" re-simulated saved config (see n_reps_best_medoid). "best" accepted back-compat longer produced run_MOSAIC() (config_best.json); skipped warning unless older run dir still carries file. model appears value model column. n_reps_best_medoid Integer (default 50); number stochastic LASER reruns used build predictive median + intervals best medoid configs. reruns execute locally calling R process (Dask), cost scales value times number cutoffs locations. central_method Ensemble central tendency used compiled predictions -sample calibration metrics/medoid: "median" (default; lower calibration bias) "mean" (unbiased expected counts, never collapses sparse deaths). Scalar per-channel c(cases=, deaths=). predictions table carries pred_central (choice) plus pred_mean/pred_median cross-walk; WIS/coverage remain quantile-based unaffected. est_suitability_spec Named list modeling arguments passed est_suitability (e.g. architecture, feature_set, response_var, bias_correct, lstm_v2 arch_control list). Date arguments ignored (harness-owned). Deprecated v0.33 keys (n_splits, exclude_covariates) accepted ignored per-cutoff deprecation message — prefer arch_control lstm_v2 knobs. dask_spec Optional Dask/Coiled spec passed run_MOSAIC. dir_output Directory experiment artifact (created needed). verbose Logical (default TRUE).

title: Rolling-Window Forecast Validation for the MOSAIC Transmission Model — run_rolling_cv
text: Invisibly, manifest list. Side effects: writes manifest.json, predictions.parquet, runs/ dir_output.

title: Rolling-Window Forecast Validation for the MOSAIC Transmission Model — run_rolling_cv
text: function fit--forecast engine : produces calibrations, projections, one organized predictions artifact. compute evaluation metrics, baselines, skill scores — done post-hoc reading predictions.parquet. Window. -sample () start fixed config$date_start (anchor); simulation runs full config window (config$date_start .. config$date_stop). cutoff T calibration likelihood scores weeks \(\le T\) (observations T masked NA); post-T portion simulation forecast. embargo_weeks gap separates stop (T) OOS start (T + embargo); embargo week neither trained labeled OOS. Leakage discipline. psi re-fit per cutoff fit_date_stop = T; harness owns leakage-critical date arguments est_suitability overrides date keys passed via est_suitability_spec (warning). est_suitability_spec therefore controls modeling choices (target, features, architecture), cutoff window. Coupled metapopulation. iso may single country vector; vector runs coupled metapopulation (one calibration per cutoff covering listed locations). Thus one run_MOSAIC calibration per cutoff (per country). Outputs. dir_output: manifest.json (settings + per-run index status), predictions.parquet (compiled long table), runs/cutoff_<T>/ (native run_MOSAIC directory cutoff). predictions.parquet derived view — can rebuilt run directories compile_rolling_cv_predictions. predictions table one row per (cutoff x location x date x metric) columns: run_id, iso_code, anchor_date, cutoff_date, date, metric, segment (/embargo/OOS), weeks_ahead, horizon_bucket, observed, observed_source, pred_central (scored series), pred_mean, pred_median, central_method, CI columns (pi*_lo/pi*_hi). observed held-(unmasked) trusted surveillance value, OOS rows carry real target post-hoc scoring.

title: Sample from Prior Distribution (Simplified) — sample_from_prior
text: n Integer. Number samples draw (default: 1). prior List. Prior object containing 'distribution' 'parameters' slots. verbose Logical. TRUE, print warnings NULL invalid priors (default: FALSE).

title: Sample from Prior Distribution (Simplified) — sample_from_prior
text: Numeric vector length n containing samples prior distribution. Returns NA values prior NULL/invalid.

title: Sample from Prior Distribution (Simplified) — sample_from_prior
text: simplified version expects every prior consistent structure: $distribution: Character string naming distribution type $parameters: List containing distribution parameters Supported distributions: beta: parameters$shape1, parameters$shape2 gamma: parameters$shape, parameters$rate lognormal: parameters$meanlog, parameters$sdlog parameters$mean, parameters$sd normal: parameters$mean, parameters$sd truncnorm: parameters$mean, parameters$sd, parameters$(lower bound), parameters$b (upper bound) uniform: parameters$min, parameters$max discrete_uniform: parameters$min, parameters$max (returns integers) gompertz: parameters$b, parameters$eta fixed: parameters$value (genuinely converged, returns constant) frozen: parameters$value (frozen previous stage, returns constant)

title: Sample from Prior Distribution (Simplified) — sample_from_prior
code: # Sample from a beta distribution prior_beta <- list(   distribution = "beta",   parameters = list(shape1 = 2, shape2 = 5) ) sample_from_prior(n = 10, prior = prior_beta) #>  [1] 0.58842776 0.47125524 0.41759665 0.10247331 0.37815582 0.10637791 #>  [7] 0.49731949 0.07843191 0.38134901 0.21904204  # Sample from a truncated normal distribution prior_truncnorm <- list(   distribution = "truncnorm",   parameters = list(mean = 0, sd = 1, a = -2, b = 2) ) sample_from_prior(n = 10, prior = prior_truncnorm) #>  [1]  0.361730175 -0.660147606  0.165218882 -1.683158923 -0.366686670 #>  [6]  1.770533918  0.459259631 -0.288203653  1.322119592 -0.003683303

what: Sample Parameters from Prior Distributions — sample_parameters
title: Sample Parameters from Prior Distributions — sample_parameters
text: function samples parameter values prior distributions creates MOSAIC config file sampled values. supports distribution types used MOSAIC priors provides explicit control parameters sample.

title: Sample Parameters from Prior Distributions — sample_parameters
code: sample_parameters(   PATHS = NULL,   priors = NULL,   config = NULL,   seed,   sample_args = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   validate = TRUE,   ... )

title: Sample Parameters from Prior Distributions — sample_parameters
text: PATHS list containing paths various directories. NULL, use get_paths(). priors priors list object. NULL, use MOSAIC::priors_default. config config template list. NULL, use MOSAIC::config_default. seed Random seed reproducible sampling (required). sample_args Named list logical values controlling parameters sample. element named sample_[parameter] logical value. Available options include: sample_alpha_1: Population mixing within metapops (default TRUE) sample_alpha_2: Degree frequency driven transmission (default TRUE) sample_decay_days_short: Minimum V. cholerae survival (default TRUE) sample_decay_days_spread: Spread min max V. cholerae survival; decay_days_long derived short + spread (default TRUE) sample_decay_shape_1: First Beta shape decay (default TRUE) sample_decay_shape_2: Second Beta shape decay (default TRUE) sample_epsilon: Immunity (default TRUE) sample_gamma_1: Recovery rate (default TRUE) sample_gamma_2: Recovery rate (default TRUE) sample_iota: Incubation rate (default TRUE) sample_kappa: V. cholerae 50 percent infectious dose concentration (default TRUE) sample_mobility_gamma: Mobility distance decay parameter (default TRUE) sample_mobility_omega: Mobility population scaling parameter (default TRUE) sample_omega_1: Vaccine waning rate one dose (default TRUE) sample_omega_2: Vaccine waning rate two doses (default TRUE) sample_phi_1: Initial vaccine effectiveness one dose (default TRUE) sample_phi_2: Initial vaccine effectiveness two doses (default TRUE) sample_chi_endemic: PPV among suspected cases endemic periods (default TRUE) sample_chi_epidemic: PPV among suspected cases epidemic periods (default TRUE) sample_rho: Care-seeking rate (default TRUE) sample_sigma: Symptomatic fraction (default TRUE) sample_zeta_1: Symptomatic shedding rate (default TRUE) sample_zeta_ratio: Symptomatic--asymptomatic shedding ratio (default TRUE) sample_beta_j0_tot: Total transmission rate (default TRUE) sample_p_beta: Proportion human--human transmission (default TRUE) sample_tau_i: Diffusion (default TRUE) sample_theta_j: WASH coverage (default TRUE) sample_a_1_j: Seasonality (default TRUE) sample_a_2_j: Seasonality (default TRUE) sample_b_1_j: Seasonality (default TRUE) sample_b_2_j: Seasonality (default TRUE) sample_CFR_target: Per-country target reported case-fatality ratio (B2 lognormal location prior). priors object carries CFR_target location prior, gates whether CFR_target drawn (TRUE) held config default / prior median (FALSE) (default TRUE) sample_mu_j_baseline: B2 (priors object CFR_target location prior) gates derivation mu_j_baseline CFR_target * gamma_1 * rho / (rho_deaths * chi) rather independent draw; mu_j_baseline longer independently sampled location parameter. legacy priors object (CFR_target prior) gates old independent mu_j_baseline draw. (default TRUE) sample_mu_j_slope: Location-specific temporal IFR trend (default TRUE) sample_mu_j_epidemic_factor: Location-specific epidemic IFR multiplier (default TRUE) sample_epidemic_threshold: Location-specific epidemic activation threshold (default TRUE) sample_delta_reporting_cases: Infection--case reporting delay days (default TRUE) sample_delta_reporting_deaths: Infection--death reporting delay days (default TRUE) sample_psi_star_a: Suitability calibration shape/gain (default TRUE) sample_psi_star_b: Suitability calibration scale/offset (default TRUE) sample_psi_star_z: Suitability calibration smoothing (default TRUE) sample_psi_star_k: Suitability calibration time offset (default TRUE) sample_initial_conditions: Initial condition proportions (default TRUE) ic_moment_match: Derive E/observed week-1 cases sampled reporting chain (sigma, rho, chi_endemic, iota). active sample_initial_conditions TRUE. (default FALSE) NULL, parameters sampled (default behavior). verbose Logical indicating whether print progress messages. Default TRUE. validate Logical indicating whether run post-sampling validation. Default TRUE. ... Additional individual sample_* arguments backward compatibility. override values sample_args provided.

what: Update Priors from Posteriors for Staged Estimation — update_priors_from_posteriors
title: Update Priors from Posteriors for Staged Estimation — update_priors_from_posteriors
text: Merges posterior distributions completed calibration stage complete priors object, producing new priors object suitable input next calibration stage.

title: Update Priors from Posteriors for Staged Estimation — update_priors_from_posteriors

title: Update Priors from Posteriors for Staged Estimation — update_priors_from_posteriors
text: priors Complete priors object (list) path priors JSON file. posteriors Posteriors object (list) path posteriors JSON file (produced calc_model_posterior_distributions). verbose Logical. Print merge diagnostics (default TRUE).

title: Update Priors from Posteriors for Staged Estimation — update_priors_from_posteriors
text: complete priors object (list) identical top-level structure input priors. Posterior fits replace corresponding prior entries; entries preserved unchanged.

title: Update Priors from Posteriors for Staged Estimation — update_priors_from_posteriors
text: posteriors object produced calc_model_posterior_distributions pruned: contains parameters sampled successfully fitted. Parameters frozen (sampled) removed entirely. valid priors object sample_parameters must complete — must entries every parameter sampled. function merges two: posterior fits replace corresponding priors, everything else preserved original priors. Merge rules (per parameter): Posterior fitted distribution (beta, gamma, lognormal, normal, truncnorm, uniform, gompertz): replace prior. Posterior "fixed" distribution: replace prior. parameter zero posterior variance return constant sampled. Posterior "failed" distribution: keep original prior. fitting failed, prior informative failure marker. Parameter absent posteriors: keep original prior. sampled stage. function also strips residual fitted-diagnostic metadata (e.g. fitted_mode, fitted_ci) posterior parameter entries, keeping canonical distribution fields sample_from_prior reads.

title: Update Priors from Posteriors for Staged Estimation — update_priors_from_posteriors
text: returning, function checks : output metadata, parameters_global, parameters_location top-level slots. Every parameter entry distribution (character) parameters (list) fields. "failed" distribution markers remain (replaced original priors). Global parameter count matches original priors. Location-specific parameter groups location counts match.

title: Update Priors from Posteriors for Staged Estimation — update_priors_from_posteriors
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # After a completed calibration stage priors <- jsonlite::read_json("1_inputs/priors.json") posteriors <- jsonlite::read_json("2_calibration/posterior/posteriors.json")  # Merge posteriors into priors for the next stage updated_priors <- update_priors_from_posteriors(priors, posteriors)  # Use in next calibration stage run_MOSAIC(priors = updated_priors, ...) } # }

title: Weighted Statistical Functions for Posterior Analysis — weighted_var

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   sample_data <- list(     group1 = list(       value1 = rnorm(100),       value2 = runif(100)     ),     group2 = list(       message = "Hello, MOSAIC!",       timestamp = Sys.time()     )   )    # Write to a plain JSON file.   output_file <- "output.json"   write_list_to_json(data_list = sample_data, file_path = output_file, compress = FALSE)    # Write to a gzipped JSON file.   output_file_gz <- "output.json"  # Note: the function will change this to output.json.gz   write_list_to_json(data_list = sample_data, file_path = output_file_gz, compress = TRUE)    # Load the plain JSON file to inspect its contents.   json_data <- jsonlite::read_json(output_file)   print(json_data) } # }

title: MOSAIC 0.48.3
text: B1 — mu_j_baseline CFR→μ derivation re-anchored posterior-consistent chain factors (gamma_1 0.1133→0.10, chi 0.639→0.70), scaling every country’s center ×0.805 (cases-neutral). Corrects systematic deaths -prediction traced (independent 4-agent review) derivation freezing gamma_1 prior mean calibration consistently pulls anchor (implied CFR ∝ 1/gamma_1 inflated ~1.2–1.8×). mu_j_baseline magnitude (v0.13 rho_deaths correction) rho_deaths unchanged. ETH ×0.40 dwell stop-gap retained (held ≈0.00088): B1’s uniform ×0.805 -corrects low-gamma_1 countries, ETH keeps residual deferred Phase-2 dynamic per-country gamma_1-coupled derivation (B2) subsumes . beta_j0_tot per-country recenter — geometric-mean shrinkage new_meanlog = 0.5·log(prior) + 0.5·log(posterior_median) (w=0.5, width kept) toward Stage-1 posteriors 18-country data cohort, correcting ~1.6× cases -prediction (data halved beta ). Genuinely per-country (5 countries — BDI/MWI/NGA/RWA/TZA — move ); COG’s deliberate override preserved; LBR (unidentified) left default. Width intentionally tightened (warm-start ×2 beta_j0_tot inflation guard). Validation (6-country dugong recalibration, 2018 window): deaths bias dropped 6 (NER 2.81→2.25, MOZ 1.76→1.23, SSD 2.27→2.10, …), cases R² unchanged (≤0.01) — cases-neutral designed. Residual ~2× COD/SSD -design implied-CFR / median-ensemble floor (project_central_method_v038), prior issue; low-gamma_1 countries (e.g. MWI, ~unchanged B1) Phase-2 B2 case. Full devtools::test() green (FAIL 0).

title: MOSAIC 0.48.2
text: Pre-scale-package-health audit (parallel swe + maintainer review v0.45–v0.48 burst). Two Dask/Coiled-path correctness fixes (local PSOCK unaffected throughout) plus build/doc hygiene. [CRITICAL] Dask path: per-cell weight matrices longer leak per-sim JSON. .extract_sampled_params() (run_MOSAIC_helpers.R) exclude reported_cases_weight/reported_deaths_weight. matrices contain NA cells; Dask path jsonlite::toJSON(digits=NA) serializes NA_real_ string "NA", config.update(sampled) worker overwrote clean float64 base_config arrays lists carrying "NA" — np.asarray(..., dtype=float) v0.48.0 weighted scorer raised convert string float: 'NA', failing every Dask sim; also re-serialized ~1.5 MB matrices per sim (~30× JSON bloat 40K-sim scale). matrices broadcast (clean numpy) via .extract_base_config(), now excluded per-sim set. Latent v0.48.0 began consuming weights; textbook local/Dask divergence (Lesson #12) — local PSOCK JSON round-trip. [HIGH] Dask path: dtype parity as_ndarray engine fields. engine’s as_ndarray() returns incoming numpy array unchanged casts incoming list declared dtype (uint32 IC counts, float32 seasonality/transmission vectors). local PSOCK path ships lists (via reticulate::r_to_py), engine casts correctly; Dask worker pre-converted float64 numpy, as_ndarray() left float64 engine seeded stochastic state float64 instead uint32 — silent ~1–2% log-likelihood divergence local identical values + seed. new _AS_NDARRAY_FIELDS registry keeps fields lists worker (converts base_config numpy copies back lists), restoring bit-level dtype parity local. Hygiene: rewrote dead/empty dedup test test-get_location_priors.R (guarded field path longer exists → 0 assertions; now 7 real assertions); fixed two roxygen Rd cross-reference WARNINGs (calc_model_ensemble.Rd \link{}→\code{}; get_greek_unicode.Rd \link{ggplot2}→\pkg{}); added __pycache__//*.pyc (vignettes/cache|figures) .gitignore/.Rbuildignore (parity test’s source_python compiles .pyc inst/python/); converted non-ASCII glyphs plot_model_subset_optimization.R \uXXXX escapes. Known follow-ups (blockers): () CI never pip-installs laser-cholera, engine-backed parity tests — including v0.48.0 Dask loc_idx guard — run locally/VM, never CI; (b) burn_in_days=30 (default) silently clamps n_time short/outbreak windows, yielding degenerate 0-contribution scoring — set burn_in_days=0L short-window sims (guard-warning candidate change pending statistician/disease-modeler input); (c) upstream laser-cholera WIS np.sum→np.nansum gap (see claude/wis_issue_draft.md).

title: MOSAIC 0.48.1
text: Test fix (post-v0.16.0 audit): test-calc_model_likelihood_python_parity.R test #7 pinned known R↔︎Python divergence zero-prediction cumulative penalty (Python ~1.78× negative R, observed laser-cholera 0.13.1) explicit instruction convert exact-parity assertion engine aligned. laser-cholera v0.16.0’s verbatim port calc_model_likelihood aligned penalty (R Python now agree ~1e-12), tripped pinned ratio — single failure surfaced full-suite health audit. test now asserts exact parity. production-code change; early-warning test job. devtools::test(): 0 failures. (Note: built-tarball R CMD check carries known non-CRAN baseline standing ERRORs/WARNINGs/NOTEs — CRAN-incoming/examples-need-data-root/relative-source() test paths — none change; MOSAIC explicitly non-CRAN CI gates testthat::test_local(), green.)

title: MOSAIC 0.48.0
text: Dual-mode alpha_1 / alpha_2. engine now accepts either global scalar (existing behaviour, bit-identical) per-location (length(location_name)) vector FOI mixing exponents, humantohuman.py broadcasts/indexes via np.power. make_LASER_config() validation now accepts forms enforces engine’s range invariants (alpha_1 ∈ (0, 1] strict-positive, alpha_2 ∈ [0, 1]); previous validator hard-required scalar used looser [0, 1] lower bound alpha_1. Default sampling/priors remain global-scalar — per-location form accepted direct run_LASER() configs; per-location sampling (per-iso priors) intentionally deferred. scalar path unchanged end--end (serialization, local PSOCK, Dask already pass scalar untouched). Dask/Coiled likelihood now honors per-cell confidence weights (parity fix). laser-cholera v0.16.0’s calc_model_likelihood gained weights_obs_cases / weights_obs_deaths matrix arguments (ported verbatim MOSAIC’s R calc_model_likelihood). Dask worker (inst/python/mosaic_dask_worker.py) previously apply — emulated deaths-prefix zero NaN-ing deaths obs otherwise ran unweighted, diverging local PSOCK R score whenever reported_cases_weight/reported_deaths_weight non-trivial. worker now passes real (sliced, deaths-prefix-zeroed) weight matrices Python calc_model_likelihood, mirroring run_MOSAIC.R exactly, recomputes -worker whenever weights non-trivial scored window starts t=1 (engine analyzer’s model.log_likelihood never sees weights_obs_*, trusted cases). pure-default path (trivial weights, slice) still uses model.log_likelihood, bit-identical engine analyzer. new _weights_obs_matrix_trivial() helper gates decision (matrix-level analogue R’s .weights_obs_row_trivial). Worker peak-sourcing fix (shape-term parity). v0.16.0 Python calc_model_likelihood matches epidemic_peaks rows integer loc_idx column (getattr(row, "loc_idx", None)), silently dropping row lacks . raw config["epidemic_peaks"] scattered workers carries iso_code/peak_date (see .filter_epidemic_peaks()), rewritten _score_window_likelihood() zeroed every peak-timing/peak-magnitude/cumulative shape term whenever shape weights — diverging local PSOCK R scorer (dispatches iso_code). worker now sources peaks model.params["epidemic_peaks"], engine’s params.py enriches loc_idx model construction — identical engine analyzer reads. Verified numerical tolerance R local path full burn--slice + deaths-prefix + per-cell-weights + peak-timing/magnitude/cumulative case. (Shape weights default 0, latent.) Covered new test-dask_worker_score_window_parity.R. Known engine-side gap (fixed ): v0.16.0’s compute_wis_parametric_row uses np.sum rather np.nansum, single NA observation poisons entire WIS row NaN term dropped engine-side, R’s na.rm = TRUE retains — local Dask diverge WIS term weight_wis > 0 gappy surveillance data. WIS defaults 0; fix belongs read-laser-cholera engine. Resume safety: v0.16.0 intentionally added .mosaic_lc_likelihood_compatible() byte-identical allow-list {0.14.0, 0.15.0}. non-trivial obs-weights v0.16.0 Dask shard differs 0.15.0 shard, version-keyed allow-list tell whether given shard used trivial weights — 0.15.0 → 0.16.0 resume re-runs rather risk pooling weighted unweighted likelihoods.

title: MOSAIC 0.47.5
text: .validate_updated_priors() now checks per-group location names, just counts — name-drift preserves cardinality (e.g. relabelled iso) previously passed validation silently (misalignment class validator exists catch). get_location_priors() now returns locations canonical source order (matching get_location_config(), selects via (location_name %% iso)) instead requested-argument order — removing position-vs-name foot-gun multi-iso callers. Sampling name-keyed, changes order , values (result change). New test-staged_metapop_prior_ops.R exercises real 40-loc objects (previously 2-location toy fixtures): partial-fold non-target invariance, validator name-drift detection, get_location_priors/get_location_config order consistency, full-40 inflate_priors scope + lognormal mean/variance, convert_config_to_matrix/convert_matrix_to_config 40-loc round-trip. (Deferred, doc-: convert_config_to_dataframe param-set-equivalent matrix path — review item LOW-3, unused run_MOSAIC.)

title: MOSAIC 0.47.3
text: Default control$likelihood$burn_in_days changed 0 30. per-channel scored window (v0.47.0) now default: first 30 daily steps excluded likelihood R²/bias scoring. Rationale optimal-window research: seeded E/discharge cases settles ~day 14–21, deaths IC transient lags (death event + delta_reporting_deaths ≈ 5 d) still decaying ~day 28–35 — single 30-day window clears channels’ transients. KEN took cases-R² 0.338 → 0.56 kept dropping deaths bias well past 21 (3.26 → 2.50 21 → 2.22 28). Cost ~1 % 2018 window / ~2.5 % 2023 window, -op start spike (high-burden countries unaffected). Behavior change, bit-identical: like central_method change, alters scored target, config_best/subset/medoid selection differs runs comparable across boundary. Set control$likelihood$burn_in_days = 0L restore pre-v0.47.3 scoring. Note: short windows 30 may clamp toward n_time (min_obs_for_likelihood gate yields 0-contribution, fail-safe) — set 0L short-window/outbreak sims. residual deaths-bias floor (KEN ~1.6, COD ~1.27) model deaths-baseline / implied-CFR, IC transient, unaffected burn-(’s deaths_score_start/CFR lever).

title: MOSAIC 0.47.2
text: config_default (v4.3) + priors_default (v15.13) rebuilt relaxed trust-tier gate, unchanged 2023-01-01 window. Following v0.47.1 fourier-keep policy, shipped fit-target matrices now retain fourier_* reconstructions lower per-week confidence_weight instead NA-blanking . 811 fourier weeks fall 2023+ window, change material: reported_cases_weight cells (0,1) rise 10,062 → 17,073 (range 0.40–0.95), complementary drop ==1 cells — weeks now inform fit -weighted real observations rather dropped. simulation window unchanged (date_start = 2023-01-01, ncol = 1398); per-observation weighting previously-discarded weeks differs. priors build_date_start = 2023-01-01, ic_t0 = 2023-02-01 (IC seeding epoch unchanged). test-config_default_weights (21/21) test-cfr-pipeline-consistency (93/93) pass.

title: MOSAIC 0.47.1
text: Surveillance trust-tier gate relaxed: keep fourier_* reconstructions, -weighted. process_cholera_surveillance_data() now NA-blanks assumed_zero weeks (pure surveillance-silence assumption). fourier_* rows — synthetic reconstructions real annual/quarterly totals — previously hard-dropped now kept reach daily fit target carrying lower per-week confidence_weight (~0.4–0.5 vs ~0.9 observed), calc_model_likelihood() consumes per-observation weight. low-confidence--real-magnitude weeks (e.g. 2019–2022 JHU→handoff gap) inform fit reduced weight rather discarded. observed, documented_zero, direct /JHU/SUPP rows unchanged. Code-; shipped config_default/priors_default rebuilt policy follow-commit.

title: MOSAIC 0.47.0
text: burn_in_days (integer ≥ 0, default 0L) — drops leading IC-transient steps channels. MOSAIC seeds E/moment-matching date_start; seed discharges reported cases first ~1–2 weeks, producing day-1 spike forced steady state can’t match (verified KEN: medoid day-3 cases 2761 vs observed 15, ~184× observed; decays ~observed day 21). burn_in_days=21 removes scored series entirely (scored-window max 45 vs observed max 125). deaths_score_start (NULL Date/"YYYY-MM-DD", default NULL) — scores deaths later date (non-stationary observed CFR makes deaths unfittable ~2023 several countries). score_start_cases (NULL date, default NULL) — optional explicit cases start overriding burn-. Mechanism. Resolved per-channel 1-based start indices (.mosaic_resolve_score_window()), worker slices obs/est min(idx_cases, idx_deaths):n (merely -weighting — peak/cumulative shape terms don’t honor weights_time), zeros deaths residual prefix, passes sliced start date peak date_seq stays aligned. R²/bias sites drop unscored head via generalized .mosaic_mask_central_for_scoring(); calc_model_ensemble() records score_idx_* artifact_mask; plot_model_ensemble() blanks unscored head display. Bit-identical defaults. knobs defaults yield idx_cases = idx_deaths = 1 ⇒ slicing ⇒ scoring identical pre-feature runs (new test-burn_in_scoring_parity.R + unchanged tier-2/reference/obs-weights parity suites). Carried control$likelihood ⇒ byte-compared resume guard ⇒ changing window across resume correctly errors. Dask path re-injects resolved score_idx_* filters epidemic_peaks sliced start (schema-parity test extended).

title: MOSAIC 0.46.4
text: calc_model_ensemble() gains RAM-projection guard: allocating dense [loc×time×param×stoch] ensemble arrays projects concurrent peak emits loud warning (naming max_best_subset/n_iter_ensemble dial ) exceed ~80% system RAM — long-window run (e.g. 2015, ~4320-column config → ~51 GB production defaults) fails loud guidance instead OOMing orchestrator. Warn-; -op normal (2023, ~1400-col) widths. make_config_default.R now asserts resolved date_start matches installed priors_default$metadata$build_date_start (newly recorded make_priors_default.R) stop()s mismatch — closing silent config/priors window-desync reachable documented rebuild order skipped (back-compat warning older priors lacking field). ic_t0 selection relabeled actual criterion — broadest-data-coverage month (maximizes many countries seeded real surveillance rather Beta defaults), explicit note case-volume weighting considered rejected (biases epoch toward outbreak peak -seeds E/). Factored pure, unit-tested .ic_select_epoch() helper (previously untested). Doc/cosmetic nits: optimize_ensemble_subset() @param longer claims package default "mean"; plot_model_ensemble() uses adaptive date-break ladder (~10–15 ticks) instead fixed 3-month interval (~44 ticks 11-yr window).

title: MOSAIC 0.46.3
text: Build start date (date_start) generalized across config + priors builders. make_config_default.R make_priors_default.R honor MOSAIC_BUILD_DATE_START env override (single source truth; fallback 2023-01-01) start date flows , enabling back-history rebuilds (e.g. 2015-01-01 start — covariates cover ≥2015). required rebuild order documented make_config_default.R. ic_t0 tie-break now explicit (earliest month), loud guard 2023 anchor ever drifts 2023-02-01 cold-start warning far-future starts.

title: MOSAIC 0.46.2
text: Data-driven initial-condition seeding epoch (ic_t0) tracks date_start. Replaced hard max(date_start, 2023-02-01) floor — whose “2015 active-case countries” premise predated multi-source JHU/AI back-history — data-driven epoch (broadest-data-coverage month) within [date_start, date_start + 12mo]. Inert 2023 default (resolves 2023-02-01; shipped priors numerically unchanged), back-history build seeds ICs era’s data instead 8-year-mismatched 2023 state.

title: MOSAIC 0.46.1
text: Default ensemble central_method changed "mean" "median". posterior-weighted ensemble now summarizes per-cell predictive median default — conventional epidemiological-forecast point estimate, robust stochastic right-tail outliers (rationale; note right-skewed predictive lower bias--1.0 ratio mostly reflects skew, “lower bias” alone justification). Behavior change, reporting-: central_method feeds best-subset optimizer (optimize_objective = "mae"), changes selected subset, medoid, config_best.json — runs directly comparable across boundary. *_ensemble_mean *_ensemble_median R²/bias remain summary.json, mean-based implied-CFR/skew diagnostic preserved cross-walk field. default sites updated lockstep (resolver, mosaic_control_defaults, plot_model_ensemble, run_rolling_cv).

title: MOSAIC 0.46.0
text: calc_model_ensemble() now carries mask spec via two new params (n_cases_warmup_mask = 2L, mask_final_deaths_step = TRUE, defaults matching plot_model_ensemble()) records resolved spec returned mosaic_ensemble object artifact_mask = list(cases_warmup, deaths_final). central/quantile/array fields stay RAW (unmutated). new internal helper .mosaic_mask_central_for_scoring() sets artifact time-positions NA EST central matrix ; observed series stay unmasked calc_model_R2()/calc_bias_ratio() drop NA pairs pairwise. Wired every R²/bias scoring site run_MOSAIC() (canonical ensemble, dual mean/median, tier subset, medoid; windowed metrics inherit masked canonical series). medoid SELECTION distance deliberately left unmasked. CSV/prediction export plot series unchanged.

title: MOSAIC 0.45.5
text: Parallel testthat Config/testthat/start-first ordering slowest cluster-spawning files first. new setup-python.R pins thread env vars (OMP/MKL/OPENBLAS/NUMEXPR/TBB/NUMBA + ARROW = 1) BLAS threads 1 every worker Python starts, probes interpreter caches result options(). 5 tests fork (mclapply) spawn PSOCK clusters self-skip testthat callr worker (skip_if_testthat_parallel()) avoid result-IPC corruption; CI runs serial (TESTTHAT_PARALLEL=FALSE) retain full coverage. Removed gratuitous TensorFlow load test-est_suitability_dispatch.R: deprecation/unknown-arg tests now mock .est_suitability_lstm_v2 (message fires dispatch) instead falling real lstm_v2 path (~14s saved TF installed). Trimmed Monte-Carlo / whole-config work prior sampler tests: est_zeta_* n_sim 10000/5000 → 500; sample_parameters() distributional loops 200/100 → 40; shared local()-memoized .cached_sampled_config() fixture (helper-fixtures.R) replaces repeated full 301-parameter draws structural-assertion tests. Centralized skip helpers helper-skips.R (single source truth skip_if_no_python_likelihood, skip_without_tensorflow, skip_if_no_data, skip_if_few_cores, skip_if_no_rho_deaths_prior), removing duplicated inline copies across 5 files, added skip_if_slow() (gated MOSAIC_RUN_SLOW_TESTS) tier genuinely-slow non-engine tests. Shrank flood-GAM test fixture (test-impute_flood_probability.R) 5→2 synthetic ISOs (28s→19s warm); AUC signal-recovery assertion preserved always-(AUC 0.82–0.88 across seeds, clearing 0.75 threshold margin). CI workflow (.github/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml): push/PR runs fast tier; scheduled + workflow_dispatch job runs slow tier MOSAIC_RUN_SLOW_TESTS=1.

title: MOSAIC 0.44.13
text: Upgraded laser-cholera engine pin v0.15.0 (inst/python/environment.yml). v0.15.0 docs-migration + validation-hygiene release: converts assert-style checks always-/raise ValueError/AttributeError (parameter validation runs even python -O), tightens metapop CLI --validation, modernizes type hints — leaves parameter contract model.results schema (reported_cases/reported_deaths) unchanged. Verified contract-neutral: deterministic run_LASER(config_default) smoke run full test suite pass v0.15.0 changes R observation-model code, priors, config. Unlike v0.13 (deaths-scale) v0.14 (reported-cases-incidence) upgrades, prior/derivation changes required. Resume now pools across verified-likelihood-compatible engine versions Dask backend. Added .mosaic_lc_likelihood_compatible() (explicit, conservative allow-list) wired .mosaic_resume_check_inputs(): persisted current shards Python(Dask)-scored laser-cholera versions whose engine calc_model_likelihood output schema byte-identical (currently {0.14.0, 0.15.0} — v0.15.0 changed docs/type-hints), resume proceeds warning instead hard-failing likelihood-provenance guard. Non-allow-listed pairs (e.g. 0.13.0 ↔︎ 0.15.0) still hard-error, local (R-scored) path unaffected. Note: Coiled worker image / Coiled software env must rebuilt v0.15.0 keep client↔︎worker version parity (hybrid runs mismatched engine versions remain invalid).

title: MOSAIC 0.44.11
text: Integrated Phase 3 (#101) Dask worker-schema rewrite main: workers now compute likelihood -worker return per-iter {iter, seed_iter, likelihood} schema, Dask gather adapter collapses single gather--write loop, .mosaic_inject_likelihood_settings() re-injects location_name + N_j_initial casts observed surveillance matrices double scatter, test-dask_worker_schema_parity.R regression suite added. Merged top post-0.40.0 main evolution.

title: MOSAIC 0.40.0
text: New backfill_weekly_case_gaps() + compile_suitability_data(backfill_case_gaps = TRUE) repair holiday-week false zeros suitability target. Genuinely-missing weekly surveillance weeks (often Christmas/New-Year reporting lapse) turned 0 cases suitability pipeline’s NA→0 keras-target sanitiser, fabricating “transmission” week inside active outbreak. new helper linearly interpolates short interior gaps (≤ max_interp_weeks, default 2) bounded reported weeks, per-group weekly series, inside compile_suitability_data() target built. suitability-local design — canonical surveillance files calibration likelihood (correctly NA-skips missing weeks) untouched. Interpolation date-weighted (handles unevenly-spaced rows, e.g. dropped ISO W53 leaving 14-day step); reported anchors never modified (rounding/clamping apply filled cells , fractional disaggregated counts preserved); gaps shouldered reported zero left unfilled (min_anchor, default 0 — near-zero shoulder likely genuine non-outbreak week); phantom duplicate (iso, date) rows fabricated; cases_interpolated flag marks filled weeks.

title: MOSAIC 0.39.0
text: run_MOSAIC() now produces posterior ensemble medoid representative model — single best-likelihood model longer produced. Removed: best stochastic ensemble, prediction plot/CSV (predictions_best_*), Dask dispatch, config_best.json artifact, best-seed sampling fatal gate. best-subset calibration machinery (is_best_subset/weight_best, is_best_model flag samples.parquet) unchanged — still drives posterior ensemble parameter diagnostics. summary.json now reports ensemble (+ tier) fit metrics : single-model fields r2_cases/r2_deaths/bias_ratio_cases/bias_ratio_deaths removed. Run-success (outputs_ok/[RUN_SUMMARY]) keys r2_cases_ensemble; r2_cases_best/r2_deaths_best log fields dropped. run_rolling_cv() drops "best" default models (now c("ensemble","ensemble_opt","medoid")). "best" still accepted back-compat re-simulated older run dir carries config_best.json, otherwise skipped warning. Migration: scenario scripts read config_best.json repoint config_medoid.json (representative single-config model).

title: MOSAIC 0.38.0
text: New central_method control selects ensemble central tendency (default "mean"). single setting — control$predictions$central_method, "mean" (default) "median", scalar per-channel c(cases=, deaths=) — now consistently governs ensemble central trajectory used () prediction trajectory + plots, (ii) canonical *_ensemble R²/bias metrics, (iii) medoid representative member (“mean-medoid”), (iv) subset-selection objective (optimize_ensemble_subset()). weighted mean unbiased estimator expected counts (E[Σ]=ΣE) never collapses zero sparse deaths (~92% zero-days), per-tick weighted median otherwise reads zero-collapse. Cases (dense) insensitive (mean≈median). Behaviour change: default flips historical median mean, *_ensemble metrics, medoid seed, prediction plots differ pre-0.38 runs directly comparable. Set central_method = "median" reproduce historical runs bit--bit (regression-anchored path — ensemble, tier, medoid metrics, medoid seed, optimizer-selected subset N unchanged "median"). WIS objective quantile-based unaffected central_method. summary.json cross-walk: transition r2_*_ensemble_mean/r2_*_ensemble_median (matching bias_ratio_*) emitted alongside canonical r2_*_ensemble, plus central_method_cases/central_method_deaths provenance fields. Prediction CSVs now carry predicted_central (plotted/scored series), predicted_mean, predicted_median, central_method column — predicted_median always holds true median (never mislabeled). plot_model_ppc() prefers predicted_central. Rolling-CV (run_rolling_cv(), compile_rolling_cv_predictions(), evaluate_rolling_cv(), plot_rolling_cv()) threads central_method (default "mean") -sample --sample point metrics use summary. predictions table gains pred_central/pred_mean/central_method (keeping pred_median); R²/bias/MAE/RMSE/skill score pred_central WIS/coverage stay quantile-based median. Reading new numbers: default mean, canonical r2_*_ensemble/bias_ratio_* fields differ pre-0.38 -named fields (mean vs median); read central_method_cases/central_method_deaths compare across versions via r2_*_ensemble_mean/r2_*_ensemble_median. particular deaths bias ratio rise ~2× existing runs — expected unmasking posterior implied-CFR property (accepted admissible; see plan §0), regression. implied-CFR diagnostic (summary.json:cfr_implied) computed raw member arrays unchanged central_method. Medoid retarget: config_medoid.json now selected chosen central case series; scenario owners pin medoid base member re-pin. medoid distance cases-anchored (representative case trajectory), deaths-calibrated — single-member base -states across-member mean deaths, deaths-sensitive counterfactuals propagate full retained ensemble. Rolling-CV note: OOS point metrics (R²/bias/MAE/RMSE/skill) default mean -sample/--sample consistency; directly comparable externally-reported median-point forecast errors right-skewed counts. WIS interval coverage remain median/quantile-based (externally-comparable scores). Known cosmetic (sparse deaths): plotted central line weighted mean ribbon shows member quantiles, near--zero death series mean line can sit slightly 97.5% ribbon (moment vs order-statistics zero-inflated count — mathematically correct, plotting bug). proper-scoring “predictive fan” plan §7 planned follow-.

what: est_suitability(): new default lstm_v2 hierarchical-FiLM architecture (rolling-origin CV)
title: MOSAIC 0.34.0
text: est_suitability() now architecture dispatcher. v0.34 default, architecture = "lstm_v2_hierarchical_film" (“gauge_A”/“B4”), replaces v0.33 shared LSTM hierarchical-FiLM model trained expanding-window rolling-origin cross-validation — fixing v0.33 random-split temporal leak collapsed --sample forecasts near-flat line. Architecture. 3-stack LSTM trunk (128->64->32, recurrent dropout) maps 13-week covariate window shared climate-response latent z; region embedding zero-initialized country deviation embedding modulate z via two FiLM stages (tanh gains [0,2], identity init), L2 partial-pool penalty shrinking data-sparse countries toward region. BCE loss balanced_uniform sample weights (correcting zero-week imbalance) plus optional per-row confidence-weight overlay -weights AI-mined surveillance rows. step validates strictly forward time (4-week embargo); model refits full -sample data median(best_epoch); n_seeds fits combined logit scale. Output schema (Option ). prediction CSVs now carry single canonical psi column (smoothed; bias-corrected bias_correct = TRUE) consumed every psi->psi_jt reader, plus transparent diagnostics (pred_raw, pred_smooth, pred_bias_corrected, seed-dispersion quantiles q025/q25/q75/q975 — diagnostic, predictive intervals). latent v0.33 -op (bias correction wrote pred_calibrated readers used pred_smooth) fixed. Signature. New shared toggles feature_set (default "v7.3"), response_var (default "transmission_intensity"), bias_correct (renamed calibrate), architecture, single arch_control list holding lstm_v2 hyperparameters (loaded inst/fixtures/B4_rolling_cv_spec.yml). v0.33 process knobs (n_splits, seed_base, …) exclude_covariates frozen inside legacy path absorbed via ... deprecation message; calibrate mapped bias_correct. Bias correction. calibrate_psi_predictions() rewritten per-country logit-scale affine fit outbreak (non-zero observed) weeks , applied via monotone inverse logit (hard [0,1] clip). Zero-history / low-incidence countries fall back identity (uncorrected, region-FiLM-modulated psi). Region maps. arch_control$region_map selects one snf_k5 (production default; Similarity Network Fusion 4-view clustering), csv (4 admin regions; B4 reproduction baseline), seasonal_v1, hydro_v1, snf_k4. Provisional defaults. v0.34.0 defaults (response_var = "transmission_intensity", region_map = "snf_k5", bias_correct = TRUE) provisional, gated post-merge psi->LASER case-skill experiment; snf_k5 reverts csv beat , bias_correct FALSE worsens downstream case-skill. Legacy. architecture = "lstm_v1_legacy" preserves frozen v0.33 shared-LSTM path (random split + sequential fine-tuning) reproducibility/rollback.

what: Phase 3 (#101): cast observed surveillance matrices to double before scatter
title: MOSAIC 0.32.9
text: End--end Dask smoke run surfaced silent reticulate serialization bug returned -Inf every sim’s likelihood. Root cause: get_location_config(iso = "ETH") (every location’s config) ships reported_cases / reported_deaths R integer matrices — counts natively integer-valued — NA_integer_ missing surveillance weeks. reticulate::r_to_py() serializes integer matrix numpy, NA_integer_ becomes INT32_MIN (-2147483648) worker side, numpy int32 NaN representation. huge negative sentinels evaluate valid (extreme) counts calc_model_likelihood’s NB term, returning -Inf every sim. smoke run’s parquet read likelihood: -Inf 5000 sims; gather adapter’s multi-iter collapse turned 5 copies -Inf NA_real_ (finite values log-mean-exp), samples.parquet showed % finite: 0.

what: azure/Dockerfile: harden Python-env build against silent failures
title: MOSAIC 0.32.7
text: previous :latest push silently produced broken image: r-mosaic virtualenv never created (reticulate’s default r-reticulate conda env just numpy), laser-cholera wasn’t installed . Diagnosis revealed two latent bugs azure/Dockerfile combined mask failure: Failure-masking shell chain python-deps step. single trailing semicolon (; rm -rf ...) final echo short-circuited && chain — MOSAIC::install_dependencies() chained pip command failed, rm -rf && echo "..." tail still exited 0, build looked green. Fix: conjunctions now &&; conda clean wrapped (... || true) since 2>/dev/null redirect intentionally ignores warnings fail build. Verify step lenient. MOSAIC::check_dependencies() prints warnings rather erroring missing modules, half-installed venv (laser-cholera) passed previous tryCatch guard. Fix: prepend three hard assertions R-side check — test -x /root/.virtualenvs/r-mosaic/bin/python, python -c "import laser.cholera; laser.cholera import calc_model_likelihood" one-liner fails build either import missing. Confirms venv exists v0.13+ analyzer submodule reachable. RETICULATE_PYTHON pinned image level. container ENV PATH=/root/.virtualenvs/r-mosaic/bin:$PATH. reticulate’s discovery doesn’t always honor PATH — Rscript started outside MOSAIC .onLoad hook fell back uv-bootstrapped ephemeral Python /root/.cache/R/reticulate/uv/..., completely bypassing installed venv. caused devtools::test()’s pre-library() setup phase report laser-cholera missing SKIP 11 tests depend engine importable. Fix: add ENV RETICULATE_PYTHON=/root/.virtualenvs/r-mosaic/bin/python every R session image — MOSAIC-loaded — sees right Python.

what: rho (care-seeking) prior re-derived: Wiens 2-stratum RE pool
title: MOSAIC 0.32.6
text: R/get_rho_care_seeking_params.R now anchors rho prior random-effects meta-analytic pool Wiens et al. 2025 case-definition strata, replacing prior pool 12 GEMS Nasrin 2013 pediatric MSD strata + 1 Wiens severe/cholera summary. Anchors (Wiens et al. 2025, PMC12013865): - General diarrhea: 29.9% [25.3, 35.1] 122 observations - Severe diarrhea + cholera: 58.6% [39.9, 75.2] 22 observations Pooled prior: Beta(5.38, 7.10), mean 0.423, 95% CI [0.21, 0.65], ESS ≈ 12.5 (: Beta(6.81, 17.89), mean 0.276, 95% CI [0.12, 0.46], ESS ≈ 25). change: 1. Severity match. Symptomatic cholera spans full mild--severe spectrum. Anchoring severe-stratum biases rho upward (severe-dominated outbreak-response settings); anchoring general diarrhea alone biases rho downward (includes many self-resolving mild episodes). Pooling honestly reflects severity distribution. 2. GEMS already included via Wiens. Wiens 2025 dataset includes 6 GEMS-derived Study IDs covering 7 GEMS sites. prior GEMS+Wiens pool double-counted source populations. 3. Dimensional fix. prior pool weighted 12 GEMS stratum-level observations 1 Wiens meta-analytic summary — upside-vs Wiens’s 23-study underlying meta-analysis. new pool treats Wiens stratum (122 + 22 obs) one observation meta-analysis. 4. Cascading mu_j_baseline update. steady-state CFR identity mu_j_baseline = CFR × rho / (rho_deaths × chi) propagates rho change: per-country mu_j_baseline values rescale ~1.5× (0.423 / 0.276). Implied per-symptomatic-episode CFRs high-N test countries remain within [0.5%, 15%] plausibility range: MOZ 3.5%, ETH 9.3%, KEN 10.1%, COD 14.9%. Files updated: - R/get_rho_care_seeking_params.R — new derivation RE pool two Wiens strata - R/plot_rho_care_seeking_params.R — diagnostic figure updated show strata - model/input/param_rho_care_seeking.csv — regenerated Beta parameters - data/priors_default.rda + inst/extdata/priors_default.json — v15.8 (rho + per-country mu_j_baseline cascaded CFR identity) - data/config_default.rda + inst/extdata/config_default.json — v3.8 (rho + per-country mu_j_baseline updated surgically; full make_config_default.R build deferred due pre-existing unrelated psi_jt validation issue) - data-raw/make_priors_default.R, data-raw/make_config_default.R — header comments hardcoded values updated Geographic note: Wiens severe+cholera stratum draws 23 underlying studies (16 SSA + 3 LAC + 2 Bangladesh + 1 MENA + 1 South Asia non-BGD); 70% SSA-dominant. Wiens’s geographic provenance validated raw extractions github.com/wienslab/diarrhea-careseeking. Test suite: 1881 PASS / 0 FAIL / 6 SKIP (unchanged v0.32.5).

what: rho_deaths variant: production pin to informative Beta(36.95, 51.02)
title: MOSAIC 0.32.1
text: rho_deaths uses informative variant Beta(36.95, 51.02) (mean 0.42, sd 0.052, 95% CI [0.32, 0.52]) production calibrations. SYNTHESIS_REPORT §3.3 pooled-mean-CI fit. wider prediction-interval variant Beta(6.30, 8.52) retained sensitivity analysis . Rationale: within single country, deaths likelihood identifies product mu_j_baseline * rho_deaths, leaving flat factorization direction. Pinning rho_deaths near 0.42 lets mu_j_baseline posteriors carry cross-country CFR signal cleanly. three meta-analysis anchor studies (Routh 2017, Shikanga 2009, Bwire 2013) SSA outbreak settings — regime MOSAIC calibrates — pooled-mean precision operative target.

Corrupt/invalid shards read-validated quarantined; resumed runs recorded summary.json (resumed, n_simulations_reused). run also stamps likelihood-value provenance (likelihood_provenance environment.json); resume refuses pool shards scored different likelihood engine/implementation — forward hook Dask/Coiled worker-side scoring port (laser-cholera issue #47), flips engine R calc_model_likelihood -worker Python. resume = FALSE (default) byte-identical prior behavior. slim run_state.json monitoring file unchanged.

title: MOSAIC 0.30.48
text: Deep-review sweep across v0.30.33 → v0.30.47 surfaced three notable issues; release fixes . Vector reshape direction. reported_cases arrives length-T vector (e.g. single-location tests), .matrix(vec) returned T×1 column matrix; function read obs_mat[j, ] got just element [1]. Production code always passes n×T matrix branch never hit . Fixed reshaping vectors matrix(vec, nrow = 1) (single-location row). Guard clause NA propagation. gamma_1 (iota / sigma) NULL config, .finite(NULL) || NULL <= 0 yielded NA, enclosing (...) failed “missing value TRUE/FALSE needed.” Rewritten isTRUE(.finite(x) && x > 0) guard coerces NA/NULL FALSE falls back cleanly prior IC. Three unit-test expectations test-ic_moment_match.R pinned old (pre-v0.30.40) E = * iota formula also updated corrected E = Isym * gamma_1 / (sigma * iota) formula. Added tag; man/est_suitability.Rd regenerated. NEWS.md backfill v0.30.41–v0.30.47. Six version entries missing; added .

what: rho_deaths informative prior from SSA meta-analysis
title: MOSAIC 0.30.47
text: rho_deaths default prior replaced (Beta(3, 2) → Beta(36.95, 51.02)) default value drops 0.6 0.42. Methodology. Random-effects (DerSimonian-Laird, logit-scale) meta-analysis three Sub-Saharan African studies — Routh 2017 (Tanzania, EID; binomial CI 48/101), Shikanga 2009 (Kenya, AJTMH; approximate binomial CI implied ~25/73), Bwire 2013 (Uganda, PLOS NTDs; sensitivity range). Inverse-variance weighting yields Routh 53%, Shikanga 42%, Bwire 5% — Bwire’s wide CI auto--weights . Pooled mean 0.419 (essentially identical earlier ad-hoc 3:2 quality-weighted mean 0.417). Informative variant. Beta fit 95% CI pooled mean (prediction interval) gives Beta(36.95, 51.02), mean 0.420, 95% CI [0.319, 0.524], ESS ~88. Methodology mirrors R/get_rho_care_seeking_params.R (cases-side rho), establishing parallel statistical machinery two observation-model detection probabilities. Previous attribution Finger et al. 2024 corrected — paper editorial without quantitative anchor; 23–96% range cited belonged Pampaka et al. 2025 described place death, surveillance capture. Full provenance: claude/rho_deaths_research/SYNTHESIS_REPORT.md.

title: MOSAIC 0.30.40
text: Two findings disease-modeling deep review survived re-validation MOSAIC-docs project history: moment_match_E_I steady-state formula corrected. optional ic_moment_match feature using E_count = I_count * iota, dimensionally inconsistent (count x 1/day) -seeded E ~5x prior medians. laser-cholera engine E -> Isym rate sigma * iota per E Isym -> R rate gamma_1, steady-state balance E = Isym * gamma_1 / (sigma * iota). prior medians (gamma_1 = 0.1/d, sigma = 0.24, iota = 0.71/d) corrected E/Isym ratio ~0.59 instead ~0.71. I_count variable renamed Isym_count internally reflect reporting chain (cases = Isym * rho * chi_endemic) observes symptomatic compartment. Flag still defaults FALSE; fixes formula caller turns . epsilon prior 2x variance-inflation removed. variance-inflation step data-raw/make_priors_default.R:127-128 doubling sd natural-immunity waning rate 2.0e-4 4.0e-4, pushing 95% CI immunity duration roughly [1.9, 53] years – upper-tail value biologically implausible inflation documented rationale. inflation removed, sd back 2.0e-4 (95% CI rate [1.7e-4, 1.03e-3], corresponding immunity duration CI ~[2.7, 16] years) – range supported cited King et al. 2008 project’s two-cohort re-fit (~7 yr mean). priors_default metadata version bumped 15.2 .rda re-built.

title: MOSAIC 0.30.39
text: Series focused fixes five-agent deep-review package. model-behavior changes; package hygiene, correctness edge cases, consistency documented design. v0.30.29 — Add rlang DESCRIPTION Imports (NAMESPACE import(rlang) dep missing, producing R CMD check ERROR). Declare column names referenced recent plot edits R/globals.R silence “visible binding” NOTEs. v0.30.30 — Repo hygiene: remove tracked zero-byte = file, delete stray MOSAIC.Rcheck/, ..Rcheck/, .Rd2pdf*/, root Rplots.pdf, model/input/*.bak{,_*} disk. Expand .Rbuildignore (vm/, CLAUDE.md, .Rcheck/, =, .RData, .Rhistory, …) .gitignore (.bak, .Rd2pdf/). v0.30.31 — data-raw/make_* builders patched .json outputs (grepl("\\.json$")) .json.gz siblings shipping without zeta_ratio decay_days_spread, breaking downstream zeta_2 = zeta_1 / zeta_ratio derivation. Switch regex \\.json(\\.gz)?$, read via read_json_to_list(), write via write_list_to_json() right compress flag. Regenerated four .json.gz artifacts pair now content-identical. v0.30.32 — get_default_config() get_default_LASER_config() hardcoded file.path(PATHS\$ROOT, "MOSAIC-pkg", ...) – works developer checkout. Switch system.file("extdata", ..., package = "MOSAIC"); PATHS retained backward compatibility. v0.30.33 — Introduce .mosaic_set_all_thread_env(n) single source truth six canonical thread-env vars documented CLAUDE.md (OMP_NUM_THREADS, MKL_NUM_THREADS, OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS, NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS, TBB_NUM_THREADS, NUMBA_NUM_THREADS). fallback branch .mosaic_set_blas_threads setting 3, calc_model_ensemble.R 5, make_mosaic_cluster.R 5 two places. Route every site helper. v0.30.34 — plot_vaccination_maps.R: ggsave(plot = print(...)) saving invisible return value double-printing active device. Replaced plot = final_combined_plot. v0.30.35 — calc_model_likelihood() peak-shape terms: .min(abs(date_seq - peak_date)) always returns -range index, peaks whose date fell outside [date_start, date_stop] silently snapped t=1 / t=N, biasing peak-timing peak-magnitude likelihoods. Filter peak_date [date_seq[1], date_seq[N]] snapping, three sites (main path + two legacy helpers). v0.30.36 — calc_model_ensemble() weighted mean biased toward zero simulation failures: sum(values * w, na.rm=TRUE) drops NA term redistribute weight mass. Filter valid divide sum surviving weights, matching behavior weighted_quantiles(). v0.30.37 — props_to_counts() integer-rounding fallback leave compartment negative sum 1 edge cases. Replace per-compartment round() + residual--S Hamilton (largest-remainder) apportionment single per-location pass; stopifnot() asserts sum == N counts >= 0. v0.30.38 — moment_match_E_I() location-invariant due unlist(reported_cases) flattening matrix; every country got identical initial infections. Compute first-week--positives window per location row. (E_count = I_count * iota formula left unchanged inline note next biology pass; flag default.) v0.30.39 — Update epidemic_peaks docstring match actual code defaults (28-day smoothing, 10-day comparison, 75-day separation, 8% prominence) backfill NEWS v0.30.27/0.30.28 entries silently missed.

text: Three blocks derived covariates computed persisted cholera_country_weekly_suitability_data.csv even though est_suitability()’s covariates_all list intentionally excludes every one . Computing saving wasted IO + foot-gun future contributor re-enables without realising excluded. Vaccination downstream intervention – vaccines deployed response cholera, anticipation environmental suitability – non-causal predictors suitability model. Epidemic-memory block (8 columns): r_t, cum_cases_4w/8w/12w/52w, nonzero_ratio_52w, weeks_since_major_outbreak, peak_cases_52w, seasonal_outbreak_risk, plus epidemic_week alias. case-derived; predicting case-derived target (cases_binary) function cases target leakage even forecast-safe memory_lag. Spatial-import block (7 columns): import_vulnerability, export_potential, weighted_import_connectivity, connectivity_degree, betweenness_centrality, eigenvector_centrality, import_export_balance.
