Kept backward compatibility.

what: Flood-prob GAM: select=TRUE shrinkage + precip-window tensor product
title: MOSAIC 0.30.25
text: Two changes validated independently software-engineer agent statistical-modeler agent running parallel experiments separate sandboxes. converged (1); modeler additionally identified (2): Replace iterative p-value pruning loop single bam() fit using select = TRUE. Smoothness null-space shrinkage mgcv’s principled mechanism driving uninformative smooths’ coefficients toward zero. v0.30.24 -sample-p-value pruning multicollinearity-sensitive (docstring already documented caveat) produced fragile drop sequences. select=TRUE delivers equivalent CV AUC (0.851 -> 0.852 SWE experiments) +2.6 +4.2 percentile-point lift worst-detected cyclone (Kenneth 2019, 71.6 74.2 production change). Removes prune_p_threshold prune_max_iter args + pruning_log diagnostic. Single ~10-second fit replaces iterative ~30-60s loop. Replace s(precip_sum_4w) + s(precip_sum_24w) te(precip_sum_4w, precip_sum_24w, k = c(10, 10)). “heavy 4-week rain already-saturated catchment (6-month antecedent)” interaction genuinely jointly nonlinear; tensor captures univariates plus existing precip_x_soil_anom approximated crudely. Modeler-agent experiment: cyclone median percentile rank 91.6 -> 93.8 (+2.2), top-5% hits 3 -> 4. End--end production cyclone benchmark (re-measured): Idai MOZ 2019-W11 pctile = (run pending) Idai MWI 2019-W11 Idai ZWE 2019-W11 Kenneth MOZ 2019-W17 Eloise MOZ 2021-W04 Ana MOZ 2022-W04 Ana MWI 2022-W04 Gombe MOZ 2022-W11 Freddy-1 MOZ 2023-W08 Freddy-2 MOZ 2023-W11 Tested rejected agents: nthreads (-op macOS), method = REML/ML/GCV.Cp (metric change impractically slow), bs = "ad" adaptive smoothers, k = 20+ precip windows (overfit), bs = "cr" cubic regression (change), per-country = iso_code_f smooths (overfit), nested random effects, cloglog link, quasibinomial, betabinomial, Tweedie log1p(affected), smoothed +/-2w binary target, class weighting (trades AUC cyclone recall). Caveat surfaced agents: country-stratified 5-fold CV AUC drops 0.72 (vs 0.85 random folds). model’s lift comes country random effect; climate predictors less work random-fold CV suggests. model fail badly country training data. Worth keeping mind interpreting imputed flood_prob --sample country setting.

what: impute_flood_probability() gains iterative p-value pruning loop
title: MOSAIC 0.30.24
text: GAM fit, term (smooth parametric) highest -sample p-value  (default 0.05) dropped GAM refit. cycle repeats every remaining prunable term significant, fewer 5 prunable terms remain,  (default 30) iterations reached. country random-effect smooth region-conditional  block treated structural never dropped. trace written  diagnostics directory. Pruning behavior v0.30.24 production run dropped 5 precip-block terms whose contribution absorbed collinear neighbors:  (p=0.74),  (p=0.65),  (p=0.37),  (p=0.21),  (p=0.18). model retains , region-conditional smooth, plus soil-moisture / SPEI / humidity / wind / ENSO / IOD terms. End--end cyclone benchmark (median percentile rank across 10 catastrophic cyclones MOZ/MWI/ZWE) unchanged 91.1 vs 93.0 pre-pruning. Idai loses ~1.3 pts MOZ/MWI/ZWE; Ana Freddy gain 0.3-2.1 pts. Top-5% hits stay 3 10. Median seasonal-variance share unchanged 0.398. Caveat: -sample p-values predictive-importance tests sensitive multicollinearity. loop produces smaller, cleaner model whose remaining smooths -sample significant; verify cyclone benchmark + rolling-year CV major covariate changes. Set  disable pruning entirely (recovers v0.30.23 behavior). Wall-time impact: pipeline runs ~69 sec (vs ~41 sec v0.30.23), extra 28 sec covering 5 additional GAM fits pruning loop performs.

what: Flood-prob imputer: revert to enriched binomial after cyclone benchmark
title: MOSAIC 0.30.23
text: v0.30.22 Tweedie--severity formulation made Cyclone Freddy stand MOZ’s #1 historical week (headline visual goal) 10-cyclone benchmark across Idai 2019 (MOZ/MWI/ZWE), Kenneth 2019, Eloise 2021, Ana 2022 (MOZ/MWI), Gombe 2022, Freddy 2023 (x2 landfalls) showed Tweedie variant -detects catastrophic events – median percentile rank 81.4, minimum 77.8. root cause: Tweedie target chases EM-DAT’s logged Total Affected, Idai’s Total Affected logged smaller real impact warranted, model -amplified . release reverts family + target v0.30.21 (binomial logit emdat_flood_active 0/1) keeping four physics-driven predictor enrichments v0.30.22 S2 exploration found load-bearing: s(precip_sum_24w) – ~6-month basin saturation memory (inline-computed) s(precip_sum_52w) – annual antecedent precipitation (inline-computed) s(precip_sum_4w, = region_f) – region-conditional 4-week precip smooth s(precip_x_soil_anom) – joint precip-anomaly x soil-moisture-anomaly index Result: median cyclone percentile rank 93.2 (vs 81.4 Tweedie, 90.8 v0.30.21 baseline), minimum 79.1 (every cyclone-week top 21% country’s history). Three cyclones top 5%. Trade-vs v0.30.22: Freddy’s headline rank MOZ drops back #1 top-5%, every catastrophic cyclone reliably detected. right call cholera forecasting care flood events, just one outlier. Output now naturally [0, 1] logit link – global-max scaling needed. Test thresholds restored binomial-appropriate AUC > 0.80 mean(hi)-mean(lo) > 0.3 discrimination tests.

what: Flood-prob imputer: Tweedie severity target + enriched predictors
title: MOSAIC 0.30.22
text: v0.30.20 binomial--active formulation produced imputed emdat_flood_prob didn’t separate major events seasonal climatology – Cyclone Freddy (MOZ, Feb-Mar 2023) reached probability 0.503, barely MOZ Feb-Apr p90 0.396 -time MOZ max. Four parallel strategies explored (Tweedie severity target; enriched predictor set; xgboost; two-stage hurdle). release adopts hybrid two winners: Tweedie family raw Total Affected. Encodes flood SEVERITY rather just presence/absence. Tweedie compound-Poisson-gamma mixture handles zero-inflated continuous target natively. Enriched predictor set (computed inline imputer, upstream compile changes required beyond existing region column): precip_sum_24w precip_sum_52w – long-memory antecedent precipitation capturing basin saturation. largest single contributors interannual variance rebuild experiments. s(precip_sum_4w, = region_f) – region-conditional 4-week precipitation smooth (4-region classification: Central / East / Southern / West Africa). s(precip_x_soil_anom) – joint precip-anomaly x soil-moisture-anomaly index “-wet already-saturated” regime. Output normalization: predictions raw Tweedie scale non-negative, heavy-tailed continuous. Normalized global maximum [0, 1] – rank-normalization tested rejected erases long-tail major-event amplification Tweedie target chosen . End--end measured impact (full AFRO panel, real climate + EM-DAT data): Cost: 0.05 AUC 0.02 Spearman held-binary-target metrics (unavoidable switching binomial--binary Tweedie--continuous). Acceptable trade-given 6.4x lift Freddy benchmark 63% reduction seasonal-variance share. Strategies tested rejected: xgboost (sharper peaks worsened seasonal share); two-stage hurdle (Freddy peak actually dropped Total Affected Freddy isn’t global outlier).

what: Two correctness fixes in the flood-prob pipeline
title: MOSAIC 0.30.21
text: Surfaced internal review EM-DAT integration: Gate / GAM-contract drift fixed. (include_flood_prob && ...) gate compile_suitability_data() still checking v0.30.19-era predictors (temp_anom, elevation, urban_population_pct) removed GAM v0.30.20, failing check 9 new columns rebuilt GAM actually requires (precipitation_sum, precip_sum_2w/4w/8w, precip_extreme_p90_count, soil_moisture_0_to_10cm_mean, relative_humidity_2m_mean, rh_mean_12w, wind_speed_10m_max, ENSO3, ENSO4). result: missing required column caused hard stop() deep inside impute_flood_probability() instead skipped cleanly else branch. impute_flood_probability() gate now source canonical required-column list single internal helper .impute_flood_probability_required(), eliminating drift surface entirely. emdat_flood_prob_anom historical baseline fixed. per-country mean used anomaly baseline computed full series including forecast-window rows, whose probabilities GAM-extrapolated. silently leaked small amount forecast information historical-period anomaly used LSTM training feature. baseline now uses rows emdat_flood_active non-NA (historical EM-DAT panel coverage).

what: Flood-probability GAM: rebuild around hydrological drivers + ENSO/IOD
title: MOSAIC 0.30.20
text: Variance decomposition v0.30.19’s imputed emdat_flood_prob showed highest-flood-activity countries (NGA 92%, TCD 83%, SSD 83%, MLI 75%, ETH 75%) heavily dominated seasonal pattern, summary(model) revealed cyclic-week country-RE terms plus raw 12-week rainfall sum carried virtually explanatory power every anomaly/teleconnection term shrunk select = TRUE penalty. release rebuilds GAM formula around variables ostensibly linked flood physics, removes seasonal / static-baseline channels crowding climate-driven interannual signal: Removed s(week, bs = "cc") (cyclic seasonality), elevation, urban_population_pct. country random effect alone absorbs remaining country-level baseline differences. Removed s(temp_anom) — temperature isn’t direct flood driver AFRO (snowmelt). Added every precipitation channel: precipitation_sum, precip_anom, precip_sum_2w/4w/8w/12w, plus binary precip_extreme_p90_count extreme-rain trigger. Added soil moisture (raw + anomaly), atmospheric humidity (raw + 12w rolling), storm/cyclone proxy wind_speed_10m_max. Heavy ENSO/IOD bench: ENSO34 current + 8/16/24-week lags, current ENSO3 ENSO4 (Eastern Western Pacific regions), IOD current + 8/16/24-week lags. Lag-N columns computed inline. Removed select = TRUE — smooth-shrinkage penalty mechanism suppressed exactly teleconnection terms now want emphasise. Without smooths keep unpenalized REML weight; rolling-year CV arbiter.

what: Code-review fixes in compile_suitability_data and est_suitability
title: MOSAIC 0.30.19
text: Round correctness fixes surfaced internal code review: B1 — est_suitability() date auto-detection referenced d_all$date_start / enso_complete$date_stop, columns compile_suitability_data() drops saving. min(NULL, na.rm=TRUE) silently returns Inf propagated bogus dates. Fixed use d_all$date. B2 — Fine-tuning splits trained wrong target scale. model output head activation = "linear", name = "logit_head" first split correctly fed qlogis()-transformed targets. Subsequent fine-tuning splits (default n_splits = 10) fed raw [0, 1] probabilities, pushing linear head toward wrong range silently undoing first split’s training. Now applies qlogis(pmax(eps, pmin(1-eps, ...))) consistently. B3 — seasonal_outbreak_risk computed mean(cases_lagged) inside group_by(iso_code) %>% mutate(...), producing single per-country mean rather week--year climatology comment claimed. Now uses stats::ave(cases_lagged, week, FUN = mean) give intended weekly seasonality. B4 — weeks_since_major_outbreak incremented counter memory_lag warm-window (default 17 weeks forecast mode), producing fake 1, 2, 3, ... values lag target NA. Now stays NA first real observation. B5 — ISO week 53. Investigated documented. Two distinct things happening: () legitimate W53 2015 2020 dropped (~80 country-weeks, 0.2% training data); (b) upstream bug process_open_meteo_data emits spurious W53 entries labelled calendar year ISO years 2005/2010/2016/2021. drop--W53 filter robustly handles (b). Added comment block explaining trade-; upstream fix process_open_meteo_data structural follow-(separate ticket). B6 — Vaccination weekly key paired lubridate::isoweek(date) format(date, "%Y") (calendar year). Dates near year boundary (e.g., 2024-12-30, ISO 2025-W01) joining (year=2024, week=1), silently missing right upstream row. Now uses lubridate::isoyear(). B7 — pivot_wider climate ENSO values_fn, residual duplicate (iso_code, year, week, variable) row silently produce list-columns break every downstream merge. Now passes values_fn = mean. B8 — est_suitability() plotting block gated (T) (always ) comment claiming “disabled default”, par(mfrow = c(2,1)) leaked caller’s device state. Replaced real plot_country_diagnostics = FALSE argument .exit(par(old_par)). B9 — Positional column renames (names(x)[3] <- "...") replaced dplyr::rename() upstream column reorder can’t silently corrupt downstream values. B10 — Negative case counts est_suitability() now flagged warning clamped 0 (previously silent). B11 — Duplicate identical df <- data.frame(...) block est_suitability() removed. B12 — Dead first covariates_all assignment est_suitability() removed; comprehensive (live) assignment remains.

what: Speed up flood-prob GAM and clean the suitability covariate list
title: MOSAIC 0.30.18
text: impute_flood_probability() now fits mgcv::bam(method = "fREML", discrete = TRUE) rather mgcv::gam(method = "REML"). formula, essentially identical fitted values; wall-time real ~30k-row training set drops ~13 min ~10 sec. Rolling-year CV also capped 3 recent fully-observed years (previously open-ended). End--end pipeline now runs ~12 sec instead ~100 min. est_suitability() covariate list cleaned: removed log1p_cum_vaccine_doses (vaccination intervention — correlated outbreaks deployment follows cholera, including teaches non-causal shortcut breaks forecast time) raw year / month / week timestamps (let model memorise period-specific cholera waves; sin/cos cyclic terms already list carry seasonality cleanly). Added comment block covariates_all documenting rationale. End--end validation real data: mean rolling-year CV AUC 0.848 2023/2024/2025 held ; forecast-window emdat_flood_prob distribution shape-similar historical (mean 0.095 vs 0.062), confirming GAM uses climate signal rather collapsing constant.

what: Impute country-week flood probability for use in est_suitability()
title: MOSAIC 0.30.17
text: EM-DAT observed-— forecast-window weeks, raw emdat_flood_active binary added v0.30.16 identically zero, creating distribution shift training inference suitability LSTM. release replaces raw binary continuous imputed flood probability populated identically historical forecast weeks: New: impute_flood_probability() — fits binomial GAM (mgcv::gam, logit link, REML, select = TRUE) observed emdat_flood_active events using climate anomalies, cyclic seasonality, country random effects, inline-computed ENSO34_lag20 / IOD_lag16 predictors. Predicts non-NA emdat_flood_prob ∈ [0, 1] every (iso × week) row. diagnostics = TRUE (default) writes smooth-term plots, decile-calibration plot, rolling-year CV metrics CSV, summary(model) <PATHS$DOCS_FIGURES>/flood_imputation/. Warns mean CV AUC < 0.65. compile_suitability_data() gains include_flood_prob = TRUE (default). , calls impute_flood_probability() existing NA-imputation block adds four rolling-window aggregates: emdat_flood_prob_4w_max, emdat_flood_prob_12w_max, emdat_flood_prob_12w_sum, emdat_flood_prob_anom. previous zero-fill raw EM-DAT join removed forecast-window rows remain NA GAM impute. est_suitability() consumes five emdat_flood_prob* columns place four raw emdat_flood_* columns added v0.30.16. raw columns remain suitability dataframe audit fed LSTM. new dependencies (mgcv already Imports dep).

John’s gilesjohnr link already correct.

what: Sync rho_deaths into MOZ data-raw, JSON sidecars, and simulation configs
title: MOSAIC 0.30.3
text: Cleanup pass independent review v0.30.2 surfaced three medium-severity gaps left targeted .rda rebuild. v0.30.2 rebuilt top-level .rda binaries surgically left several auxiliary data-raw scripts inst/extdata JSON sidecars sync — re-running regressed rho_deaths plumbing. release closes gaps source--truth artifacts agree: MOZ data-raw scripts. data-raw/make_priors_default_MOZ.R adds Beta(3, 2) rho_deaths prior block (metadata bumped 3.0 → 3.1); data-raw/make_config_default_MOZ.R adds rho_deaths = 0.6 (metadata bumped 2.5 → 2.6). LASER config templates. data-raw/make_default_LASER_config_files.R, make_simulation_endemic_LASER_config_files.R, make_simulation_epidemic_LASER_config_files.R add rho_deaths = 0.6. inst/extdata/ JSON sidecars. six (default_parameters{,_MOZ}.json, sim_endemic_parameters.json, simulated_parameters.json, priors_default{,_MOZ}.json) regenerated rho_deaths; .gz mirrors refreshed. two priors JSONs also bumped versions 15.1 3.1. Simulation .rda binaries. config_simulation_endemic.rda config_simulation_epidemic.rda rebuilt rho_deaths = 0.6. Version metadata sync. priors_default_MOZ.rda inherited wrong version (15.1) v0.30.2 surgical rebuild; corrected MOZ-track 3.1 match source script. Refs #100. Phase 1 calc_model_likelihood Python port (laser-cholera#47) now complete consistent across source--truth artifacts.

what: Add rho_deaths plumbing for death detection model
title: MOSAIC 0.30.2
text: Adds MOSAIC-side wiring rho_deaths parameter introduced laser-cholera#49 (death detection probability, analogous rho cases). Folded Phase 1 paramfile work (issue #100 addendum): laser-cholera 0.12.x’s permissive validator silently tolerates new key paramfile; 0.13 ships #49, engine consumes produces reported_deaths. Defaults. data-raw/make_config_default.R sets rho_deaths = 0.6 (mean Beta(3, 2); Finger et al. 2024 documents ~60% surveillance capture true cholera deaths). data-raw/make_priors_default.R adds Beta(3, 2) prior block; priors_default metadata version bumped 15.0 → 15.1. Targeted .rda rebuild config_default, config_default_MOZ, priors_default, priors_default_MOZ. Avoids re-running full data-raw pipeline (depends paths external data files). Sampling control. mosaic_control_defaults()$sampling$sample_rho_deaths = TRUE R/run_MOSAIC.R. R/sample_parameters.R adds sample_rho_deaths = TRUE defaults; includes rho_deaths validate_sampled_config global params; included disease_only disabled-flag-resolution list alongside sample_rho. make_LASER_config(). New rho_deaths = NULL argument [0, 1] validation supplied; pass-params; @param documented. Round-trip schema. R/convert_config_to_matrix.R, R/convert_config_to_dataframe.R, R/get_param_names.R, R/plot_model_parameters.R include rho_deaths relevant params/keep lists appears samples.parquet posterior plots. Test coverage. New tests/testthat/test-sample_parameters_rho_deaths.R confirms prior exists shapes Beta(3, 2); sampling sample_rho_deaths = TRUE produces draws (0, 1); sample_rho_deaths = FALSE holds rho_deaths config_default; empirical mean ≈ 0.6 200 draws. Tests skip cleanly MOSAIC root rebuilt prior unavailable. Refs #100, laser-cholera#49.

what: Wire likelihood control + epidemic_peaks into Dask paramfile
title: MOSAIC 0.30.1
text: Phase 1 calc_model_likelihood Python port (laser-cholera#47, tracked issue #100). Additive wiring Dask path laser-cholera analyzer can score -worker Phase 2 (laser-cholera 0.13) Phase 3 (Dask worker schema flip, MOSAIC-pkg #101) land. Local PSOCK/FORK path unchanged. Inject helper. New .mosaic_inject_likelihood_settings() R/run_MOSAIC.R flattens 12 likelihood-control keys (weight_cases, weight_deaths, weights_time (= .weights_time_resolved), weights_location, nb_k_min_cases, nb_k_min_deaths, weight_peak_timing, weight_peak_magnitude, weight_cumulative_total, weight_wis, sigma_peak_time, sigma_peak_log) plus epidemic_peaks (trimmed iso_code/peak_date) plus calc_likelihood = TRUE onto config. Dask preflight. run_MOSAIC() calls inject helper .extract_base_config(), keys ride along scattered base_config land model.params analyzer read. Uses .weights_time_resolved (rescaled--sum-n_t vector), raw control$likelihood$weights_time. Keep list extension. R/run_MOSAIC_helpers.R::.extract_base_config() now keeps 13 new keys plus calc_likelihood. Local-path configs (inject) keep none construction — keep filter membership-based. Defensive guard. .mosaic_run_simulation_worker() explicitly sets params_sim$calc_likelihood = FALSE LASER call stray user-set TRUE local path can’t trigger analyzer un-flattened keys. Unit tests. New tests/testthat/test-inject_likelihood_settings.R covers inject helper (forwards .weights_time_resolved, additive , NULL handling) .extract_base_config keep list (presence Dask path, absence local). 14 assertions, green. Refs #100.

title: MOSAIC 0.30.0
text: Previously plot_model_ensemble(save_predictions = TRUE) wrote per-location CSVs (predictions_<type>_<LOC>.csv) output_dir, run_MOSAIC() sets 3_results/figures/predictions/ — mixing data files tree meant figures. combining step wrote predictions_<type>_all.csv 3_results/predictions/, byte-identical duplicate run covers single location. release: Separate data/figures trees. Added data_dir argument plot_model_ensemble(). provided, per-location CSVs written instead output_dir. run_MOSAIC() passes data_dir = dirs$res_predictions three call sites (best, medoid, ensemble). figures/predictions/ now holds PDFs . Combining step reads predictions/ (new canonical location) instead figures/predictions/. Regex excludes *_all.csv combine pass ignores output. Single-location runs longer emit _all.csv. one per-location CSV exists, canonical file per-location CSV — file.copy() byte-identical _all.csv. _all.csv written multi-location runs ’s genuine concatenation happening. plot_model_ppc() now reads dirs$res_predictions rather dirs$res_fig_pred (auto-discovery still works; function’s docstring example updated match). Backwards-compatibility: plot_model_ensemble() defaults data_dir = NULL, falls back output_dir — external callers function keep working unchanged.

what: Prior/posterior panels: draw mode vlines for every method in every panel
title: MOSAIC 0.29.9
text: Dashed central-tendency lines distributions_*_Prior_Posterior.pdf panels now: Mark mode plotted density (argmax displayed curve) rather mean (v0.29.4 earlier) median (v0.29.8). log-scale axis mode corresponds exp(meanlog) lognormal (= median X, visible peak log10-density bell). linear axis mode corresponds classical mode X (e.g. (s1-1)/(s1+s2-2) Beta s1>1 s2>1). Computed directly x[.max(y)] grid, always aligns visible peak regardless axis. Draw every method every panel, just ones whose center falls inside data range. previous line_val >= x_range[1] && line_val <= x_range[2] gate occasionally suppressed line mean skewed distribution fell outside plotted support. Lines drawn unconditionally now — ggplot extends axis mode sits outside density’s effective range. Near-delta distributions demoted fixed_values vlines (v0.29.8) still render solid lines median, prior/posterior marks remain visually distinct one method effectively point estimate.

what: Fix vertical-line alignment, near-delta posteriors, and add beta_j0_tot to log scale
title: MOSAIC 0.29.8
text: Three follow-ups log-scale posterior plotting v0.29.6/0.29.7: Mean vertical lines misaligned visible peak log-x. wide lognormal arithmetic mean exp(meanlog + sdlog²/2) sits many decades right density peak log10 axis (peak median exp(meanlog)). zeta_ratio prior mean ~15,000× right visible peak. Fix: log-scale params dashed central-tendency line now drawn median (qbeta(0.5, ...) Beta, exp(meanlog) lognormal). Linear-axis params still use mean . prop_E_initial / prop_I_initial prior curves collapsed flat lines. posterior Beta(62199, 6e11) 0.008-decade effective support — near-delta distribution whose peak density log10-axis ~600× larger prior’s. shared y-axis wider prior rendered flat line. Fix: distributions log10(q_0.99) - log10(q_0.01) < 0.05 log-scale axes demoted solid vertical line median (matching fixed_values styling), letting wider prior/posterior set y-axis scale. Added beta_j0_tot log-scale list. beta_j0_tot lognormal(meanlog=-10.8, sdlog=2.0), 3.4-decade span — natural log-scale candidate, missed v0.29.6.

what: Fix collapsed posteriors on log-scale prior/posterior plots
title: MOSAIC 0.29.7
text: v0.29.6 log-scale switch plot_model_distributions() plotted dlnorm(x, ...) (density w.r.t. x) log-x axis. Linear-space densities wide lognormals shrink scale — e.g. zeta_ratio posterior MOZ_s1_explore_v2 peak dlnorm 9.9e-7 vs prior 8.1 (7 orders magnitude smaller). shared linear y-axis posterior flattened line zero. Fix standard change variables density log axis: Y = log10(X), f_Y(log10(x)) = f_X(x) · x · ln(10) Applied lognormal beta branches whenever param log-scale. lognormal, equivalent dnorm(log10(x), meanlog/ln(10), sdlog/ln(10)) — symmetric-bell convention used est_kappa_prior.R:281. fix, zeta_1 zeta_ratio posteriors MOZ run now render visible curves rather collapsed lines. Peak heights comparable across prior/posterior (0.2–0.4 range zeta_* log10 density scale) regardless mass sits x-axis.

what: Log-scale x-axis for wide-span parameters in global prior/posterior plots
title: MOSAIC 0.29.6
text: plot_model_distributions() renders prior vs. posterior densities global parameters distributions_global_Prior_Posterior.pdf. Parameters multi-order--magnitude support (lognormal kappa, zeta_1, zeta_2, zeta_ratio; heavily left-skewed Beta priors prop_E_initial, prop_I_initial) previously rendered linear x-axis — distribution looked like spike zero invisible right tail, wasting panel hiding posterior shape. release switches six panels scale_x_log10() annotation_logticks(sides = "b"), matching styling already used est_kappa_prior.R:375 kappa_prior.png forest/density plots. Density grids params now log-spaced (exp(seq(log(x_min), log(x_max), ...))) rendered curve smooth across full log range rather linear-clumped right edge. Log-scale params: kappa, zeta_1, zeta_2, zeta_ratio, prop_E_initial, prop_I_initial. Unchanged: Beta priors [0,1] probabilities, truncnorm priors bounded absolute ranges, narrow-span lognormal/gamma priors still use linear x.

what: Best/medoid prediction plots: independent n_iter and parallel execution
title: MOSAIC 0.29.5
text: Best medoid single-config prediction plots previously reused ensemble_n_sims_per_param (default 5, sequential) refactored calc_model_ensemble() 0.29.2 without giving iteration control. tight stochastic CI envelopes best/medoid plots, users bump ensemble count — paying cost across N posterior parameter sets. release splits two: control$predictions$n_iter_ensemble (default 10L) — stochastic runs per posterior parameter set weighted ensemble. Renamed ensemble_n_sims_per_param. control$predictions$n_iter_best (default 100L) — stochastic runs best medoid single-config plots. Applied identically models. Best/medoid now also run parallel: calc_model_ensemble() invoked parallel = control$parallel$enable n_cores = control$parallel$n_cores (previously hardcoded parallel = FALSE). Parallelization uses internal PSOCK cluster — infrastructure posterior ensemble already uses non-Dask path. Dask calibrations, best/medoid step still uses local PSOCK since Dask cluster closed posterior-ensemble sims. Breaking rename: control$predictions$ensemble_n_sims_per_param → control$predictions$n_iter_ensemble. User scripts (vm/, azure/, vignettes) updated accordingly. Existing scripts setting best_model_n_sims (previously orphaned -op control field) migrate n_iter_best now wired .

what: Unified stochastic-median R² and bias across best, medoid, and ensemble
title: MOSAIC 0.29.3
text: Follow-0.29.2. 0.29.2 fix routed best medoid prediction plots calc_model_ensemble() + plot_model_ensemble() plot captions report R²/bias stochastic median. separate "Best model R²" / "Medoid model R²" log lines still came single deterministic lc$run_model() call, producing two different numbers thing (log vs plot caption). Red-team review flagged inconsistency. release eliminates separate deterministic LASER calls best/medoid sources three sets reported metrics — best, medoid, ensemble — stochastic median respective mosaic_ensemble objects: R/run_MOSAIC.R best-model block reordered: calc_model_ensemble(configs = list(config_best), ...) called ; R²/bias computed best_ensemble$cases_median / $deaths_median passed log line downstream summary.json. refactor applied medoid block. Log format now: "Best model R² (1 params x N stoch): cases = X (bias=Y), deaths = ..." — mirrors existing ensemble log format. Removes two per-run LASER calls (single deterministic best_model <- medoid_model <- runs) longer needed; best/medoid now run n_ensemble_stochastic_per LASER sims total (default 10), 0.29.2 fix plus plot minus deterministic R² helper run. Retires lc <- reticulate::import(...) import main run_MOSAIC body — calc_model_ensemble handles import internally. public API changes.

what: Fix best/medoid prediction plots: reported_cases, stochastic CI, unified naming
title: MOSAIC 0.29.2
text: plot_model_fit() (used best-model medoid-model plots run_MOSAIC()) rendered model$results$expected_cases — back-calculated burden new_symptomatic / rho, typically 5-10x inflated vs surveillance-comparable cases. Every part pipeline (likelihood, R², ensemble) used model$results$reported_cases = Isym * rho / chi_eff. v0.14.22 commit renamed expected_cases → reported_cases sibling plotting functions missed fourth file; bug latent v0.22.15 re-wired plot_model_fit() best-model block, real v0.22.15 v0.29.1. Fix consolidates best/medoid plots existing calc_model_ensemble + plot_model_ensemble pipeline — functions posterior ensemble uses — single-config mode (one parameter set × N stochastic reruns). One codepath three plot types eliminates parallel-implementation drift caused original bug. Changes: R/plot_model_ensemble.R: new file_prefix (default "ensemble") title_label (default "Posterior Ensemble") parameters; hardcoded filename title strings replaced; single-param-set subtitle branch added. R/run_MOSAIC.R: best medoid plot calls replaced calc_model_ensemble(configs = list(config_<...>), n_simulations_per_config = n_ensemble_stochastic_per, envelope_quantiles = c(0.025, 0.975)) + plot_model_ensemble(file_prefix = "best" | "medoid", ...). Medoid output moved 2_calibration/best_model/ 3_results/figures/predictions/ alongside ensemble plots. Explicit file_prefix = "ensemble" added main ensemble plot call self-documentation. Prediction-CSV combining loop extended iterate c("ensemble", "best", "medoid", "stochastic") filename pattern renamed all_predictions_<type>.csv → predictions_<type>_all.csv consistency plot naming. R/plot_model_fit.R: deleted (retired). function single source drift bug internal callers refactor. Output naming (3_results/figures/predictions/ unless noted): predictions_<prefix>_<LOC>.pdf + .csv per-location (prefixes: ensemble, best, medoid) predictions_<prefix>_cases_all.pdf, predictions_<prefix>_deaths_all.pdf faceted multi-location overviews predictions_<type>_all.csv combined across locations (3_results/predictions/) Lesson recorded CLAUDE.md (item 11): renaming field across sibling functions, grep exhaustively; skip temporarily-unused functions; prefer consolidating one shared code path maintaining N parallel implementations must updated lockstep.

what: Bias corrections + zeta_ratio channel switch (follow-up to 0.29.0)
title: MOSAIC 0.29.1
text: Two changes landed together patch: zeta_ratio default switched combined (C) direct literature-anchor channel (). combined precision-weighted fit median 2.16e5 pulled high derived--marginals channel (~1.15e6), overestimating per-day asymptomatic:symptomatic shedding asymmetry vs modelling-convention + household-transmission evidence (Smith 2026 ~1.6x, Chao/Finger ~10). direct channel now make_priors_default.R make_priors_default_MOZ.R write priors_default$parameters_global$zeta_ratio. combined derived fits remain available via est_zeta_ratio_prior()$diagnostics$fit_combined $fit_derived. Bias corrections zeta_1. Code review identified several compounding upward biases v0.29.0 zeta_1 fit. addressed ; zeta_1 median drops 3.72e11 1.39e11 (mode ÷66). Corrections applied: * R/est_zeta_1_prior.R: V_sev central value lowered 8 L/day 4 L/day (time-averaged 1-2 week clinical course rather first-24-h peak rate Harris 2012); V_mod 4 -> 2 L/day; V_mild 500 -> 300 mL/day. Mild concentration lowered 10^6 10^5 cells/mL (non-rice-water stool). Nelson 2020, Kaper 1995, Harris 2012 downweighted 0.50 0.10 (reviews cite Nelson-era primary data, independent measurements). Endemic outbreak severity-weighted pool rows given weight 0 (derived quantities rows 1/4/5 including triple-counting severe class). * R/est_zeta_2_prior.R: Kaper rows downweighted 0.25 0.10 (review overlap). * R/est_zeta_ratio_prior.R direct channel: Nelson 2009 paired weight 1.00 -> 0.30 (value 10^5 stool concentration ratio, per-day rate ratio - unit-inconsistent zeta_ratio). Kaper Harris paired rows 0.25 -> 0.10 (review overlap). Net prior shifts (main = MOZ): * zeta_1: LN(26.64, 1.69) -> LN(25.65, 2.46); median 3.72e11 -> 1.39e11; mode 2.15e10 -> 3.29e8 (config point estimate uses mode). * zeta_2: LN(12.69, 2.00) -> LN(12.30, 2.00); median 3.23e5 -> 2.20e5. * zeta_ratio direct channel: LN(6.64, 4.81) -> LN(4.31, 4.39); median 763 -> 74.7 (config point estimate uses median; mode pathological sdlog=4.39). config_default config_default_MOZ placeholders updated reflect new modes/medians. Test updates: tests/testthat/test-sample_parameters_zeta.R coverage range zeta_1 widened (1e9, 1e14) (1e8, 1e14) match wider bias-corrected sdlog. 18 zeta tests pass.

title: MOSAIC 0.29.0
text: zeta_1, zeta_2, zeta_ratio priors re-estimated literature meta-analysis (R/est_zeta_1_prior.R, R/est_zeta_2_prior.R, R/est_zeta_ratio_prior.R). new priors encode biological scale V. cholerae shedding (cells per infected person per day) rather Frame-B LASER count-scale used prior defaults. ~6 order--magnitude upward shift zeta_1 (prior median moves 70 000 ~1e11-1e12 cells/person/day) corresponding re-centring zeta_ratio. previous defaults LN(log(70 000), 0.8) LN(log(300), 1.2) replaced weighted-MLE lognormal fits primary-source anchors (Nelson 2009, Merrell 2002, Harris 2012, Smith 2026 medRxiv, Kaper 1995, etc.). zeta_2 becomes first-class prior. priors_default$parameters_global$zeta_2 now populated literature-derived lognormal. sample_parameters() still derives sampled zeta_2 = zeta_1 / zeta_ratio sampling time (guarantees zeta_1 > zeta_2 algebraically); stored zeta_2 prior reference distribution used validation downstream diagnostics. Existing calibration posteriors invalidated. current zeta_1 posterior centred ~48 k effectively probability 0 new prior. Every existing calibration artefact MOSAIC-Mozambique/output/calibration/ must re-run new priors use. config_default.rda scale shift. make_config_default.R placeholder constants (zeta_1, zeta_2, .zeta_ratio_default) updated new prior medians. code reads config_default$zeta_* expecting old numeric scale behave differently. config_default_MOZ.rda scale shift. placeholder constants make_config_default_MOZ.R updated. MOZ project override (stand-alone MOSAIC-Mozambique) uses zeta_ratio centre (50) independent pkg default. override unaffected; MOZ team decides adoption . LASER reservoir storage precision. new biological scale (zeta_1 ~ 1e11, I_sym ~ 100), daily Poisson mean contribution W reaches ~1e13 cells - far float32’s exact-integer limit (~1.7e7). laser-cholera/src/laser/cholera/metapop/environmental.py must widen W / W_next float64 release can merged. EXTERNAL (READ-) laser-cholera change tracked pending prerequisite; dtype change lands, running run_MOSAIC() new priors silently accumulate rounding error reservoir update tick.

title: MOSAIC 0.29.0
text: est_zeta_1_prior(PATHS, severity_mix) - weighted-MLE lognormal symptomatic shedding anchors. est_zeta_2_prior(PATHS) - weighted-MLE lognormal asymptomatic shedding anchors hard sdlog >= 2.0 floor. est_zeta_ratio_prior(PATHS, zeta_1_fit, zeta_2_fit, n_sim, seed) - precision-weighted combination direct-literature derived paired-Monte-Carlo channels.

title: MOSAIC 0.29.0
text: Rebuild priors: source("data-raw/make_priors_default.R"). Rebuild MOZ priors: source("data-raw/make_priors_default_MOZ.R"). Rebuild configs: source("data-raw/make_config_default.R") source("data-raw/make_config_default_MOZ.R"). Downstream MOSAIC-docs figures zeta_* axes must regenerated. Re-run calibration every production configuration using outputs interventions analyses.

title: MOSAIC 0.28.13
text: Added Tony Ting, Dejan Lukacevic, Meikang Wu package authors contributors (ctb) DESCRIPTION pkgdown site.

title: MOSAIC 0.24.1
text: process_open_meteo_data() now renames soil_moisture_0_to_7cm_mean soil_moisture_0_to_10cm_mean raw ERA5 historical parquets splicing climate-model projections. Upstream open-meteo-pipeline issue #5 switched ERA5 requests 0-7 cm band (ERA5 Historical API silently returned -null 0-10 cm band), without rename rbind() historical + climate frames produced mismatched columns every downstream soil-moisture feature compile_suitability_data() / est_suitability() became NA. Users cached outputs upstream fix run process_open_meteo_data(PATHS, force = TRUE) force regeneration; cache check compares source vs. output mtimes otherwise pick upstream schema change.

title: MOSAIC 0.24.0
text: control$predictions$optimize_subset = TRUE now drives canonical posterior artifacts. Previously optimized subset parallel reporting track: produced ensemble_optimized.rds *_optimized metrics summary.json, posteriors.json, posterior_quantiles.csv, ensemble plots, chained downstream priors computed tier-selected subset. release, flag optimized subset written new is_best_subset_opt / weight_best_opt columns samples.parquet every posterior-consuming function reads columns. tier-selected subset remains is_best_subset / weight_best provenance. summary.json field rename. previous r2_cases_ensemble_optimized / r2_deaths_ensemble_optimized / bias_ratio_*_ensemble_optimized / n_ensemble_params_optimized fields renamed *_ensemble_tier / n_ensemble_params_tier. canonical r2_cases_ensemble (etc.) now holds optimized metrics optimize_subset = TRUE tier metrics flag ; new *_tier fields preserve tier-subset metrics side--side comparison (NA flag ). Downstream consumers reading old _optimized field names must updated. Ensemble construction moved earlier run_MOSAIC(). Dask reconnect + stochastic sims + calc_model_ensemble block now runs posterior quantile/distribution/sensitivity construction optimizer (enabled) can refine posterior. Best-model PPC ensemble metrics/plots remain posterior construction. Users relying old behavior (tier subset drives posteriors regardless flag) set control$predictions$optimize_subset = FALSE.

title: MOSAIC 0.24.0
text: calc_model_posterior_quantiles(), plot_model_parameter_correlation(), plot_model_parameter_sensitivity(), plot_model_posteriors_detail() gained subset_col weight_col arguments defaulting "is_best_subset" / "weight_best". Existing callers see change. optimize_ensemble_subset() gained optional seeds argument returns optimal_seeds callers can map optimized subset back samples.parquet without duplicating internal sort logic. calc_convergence_diagnostics() gained optional n_best_subset_optimized argument; supplied, JSON output includes new metrics$B_size_optimized entry summary$n_best_subset_optimized field.

title: MOSAIC 0.24.0
text: control$predictions$optimize_min_n raised 4L 30L. Four statistical minimum per Fox et al. (2024), posterior density estimation optimized subset needs samples; warning logged optimizer selects < 30.

title: MOSAIC 0.24.0
text: Added .mosaic_active_subset_cols(results, control) helper returns canonical subset/weight column names plus "tier" / "optimized" source tag. Used run_MOSAIC() thread correct columns posterior-consuming calls.

title: MOSAIC 0.13.21
text: Problem: plot_model_fit_stochastic() plot_model_fit_stochastic_param() hardcoded use parallel = TRUE, ignoring user’s control$parallel$enable setting Solution: Changed function calls run_MOSAIC() use parallel = control$parallel$enable instead hardcoded TRUE Impact: Users can now disable parallel execution ensemble predictions setting control$parallel$enable = FALSE, useful debugging parallel execution causes issues Files modified: R/run_MOSAIC.R (lines 1444, 1478)

title: MOSAIC 0.13.20
text: Problem: Numba (used laser-cholera) Intel TBB library cause threading conflicts R forks parallel workers, resulting “Attempted fork non-main thread” warnings potential deadlocks hangs Solution: Set threading environment variables 1 cluster creation worker across functions use parallel execution TBB_NUM_THREADS = "1" - Intel Threading Building Blocks NUMBA_NUM_THREADS = "1" - Numba JIT compiler OMP_NUM_THREADS = "1" - OpenMP MKL_NUM_THREADS = "1" - Intel MKL OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS = "1" - OpenBLAS R/run_MOSAIC.R - Main calibration workflow R/plot_model_fit_stochastic_param.R - Ensemble predictions (causing hangs “Generating ensemble predictions”) R/plot_model_fit_stochastic.R - Stochastic predictions R/calc_npe_diagnostics.R - NPE SBC diagnostics Environment variables set main process cluster creation BLAS thread limiting workers Environment variables set worker Impact: Prevents fork-related threading conflicts across entire package, ensures stable parallel execution laser-cholera simulations, fixes hangs ensemble predictions Files modified: R/run_MOSAIC.R, R/plot_model_fit_stochastic_param.R, R/plot_model_fit_stochastic.R, R/calc_npe_diagnostics.R

title: MOSAIC 0.13.5
text: Problem: Previously converted NAs 0, artificially introducing “cases” observations mislead model Solution: Linear interpolation within location preserves temporal trends Uses approx(method = "linear", rule = 1) interpolate interior NAs rule = 1 prevents extrapolation beyond data range (preserves boundaries) sets start/end NAs 0 interpolation impossible (surrounding data points) Applies independently location multi-location data Handles cases deaths time series Interior NAs interpolated: position 5 → 25 (20 30), position 8 → 45 (40 50) Boundary NAs set 0: positions 1-2 (first data), position 10 (last data) Impact: accurate representation missing data, better model training quality Reports number NAs interpolated vs. set 0 “Interpolated: X” shows successful linear interpolation “Set 0 (start/end): Y” shows boundary NAs Files modified: R/npe_posterior.R

title: MOSAIC 0.13.4
text: Problem: PyTorch silently propagates NaN/Inf values network, causing cryptic training failures Root cause: validation input data (X, y, weights) tensor conversion Impact: parameters observations contain NAs/Infs (corrupted files, invalid samples, bugs), propagate silently cause losses become NaN Fix: Add explicit validation checks X (parameters), y (observations), weights tensor conversion (line 170-266 npe.R) anyNA(X) (!.finite(X)) - parameters matrix anyNA(y) (!.finite(y)) - observations matrix anyNA(weights) (!.finite(weights)) - weight vector data structure failed (X, y, weights) Whether NAs Infs found Affected rows columns (first 5-10 shown) Likely sources corruption Specific solutions diagnose fix Catches data corruption training starts (saves time) Clear diagnostic messages pinpoint exact problem Prevents silent NaN propagation network Helps identify upstream bugs data preparation Files modified: R/npe.R Note: addresses user’s concern NA/NaN errors 25k evenly weighted samples (binary_retained) due numerical instability, rather data corruption

title: MOSAIC 0.13.3
text: Root cause: Validation loss became NA/NaN training, causing (val_loss < best_val_loss) comparison fail cryptic error Error: Error wrapup: missing value TRUE/FALSE needed followed recursive error abort Trigger: Low ESS (Kish: 1.2, Perplexity: 3.0) continuous_retained weight strategy causing numerical instability Fix: Add explicit NA/NaN checking train_loss val_loss early stopping comparison (line 384-405 npe.R) Impact: Now provides clear, actionable error message diagnostic information solutions epoch failed loss values Common causes (low ESS, weight concentration, architecture complexity) Specific solutions (try ‘continuous_best’, use ‘light’ tier, reduce learning rate) Files modified: R/npe.R Related: error masked recursive error handling - actual issue numerical instability degenerate weight distributions

title: MOSAIC 0.13.2
text: Root cause: Used jsonlite::fromJSON(..., simplifyVector = FALSE) loading config/priors disk, keeping JSON arrays R lists instead converting vectors Error: Even v0.13.1 fix get_npe_observed_data(), config$reported_cases still list, creating list columns data frames Fix: Replace jsonlite::fromJSON() read_json_to_list() (MOSAIC standard loader) 3 locations run_NPE.R (lines 244, 264, 284) Uses MOSAIC codebase standard JSON loading (consistent functions) Properly simplifies JSON arrays R vectors (simplifyVector = TRUE default) Supports gzipped JSON files maintainable consistent Impact: Resolves persistent CSV write errors run_NPE() standalone mode Files modified: R/run_NPE.R Verified: JSON loading, get_npe_observed_data(), CSV write succeed

title: MOSAIC 0.13.1
text: Root cause: config$location_name config$iso_code list (common LASER config format), single-bracket extraction location_names[1] returned list length 1 instead scalar, creating list columns data frames Error: Error utils::write.table(...): unimplemented type 'list' 'EncodeElement' writing observed_data.csv run_NPE() Fix: Convert location_names character vector using unlist() function start (line 1008) Fix: Changed location_names[index] location_names[[index]] scalar extraction (lines 1032, 1055, 1134) Impact: NPE workflow now handles list-type location identifiers correctly Files modified: R/npe_posterior.R (get_npe_observed_data function) Verified: CSV write succeeds, data frame structure correct, existing tests still pass

title: MOSAIC 0.13.0
text: Complete NPE workflow extracted standalone function R/run_NPE.R (1000+ lines) Embedded mode: Runs inside run_MOSAIC() -memory objects (disk /O) Standalone mode: Runs independently calibration completes, loading disk Automatic mode detection based arguments provided Custom output_dir support experimenting multiple NPE strategies Root directory auto-detection getOption('root_directory') Full control object support NPE hyperparameters Complete error handling validation Post-hoc NPE without re-running expensive BFRS calibration Experiment different weight strategies (continuous_best, continuous_retained, etc.) Cleaner, maintainable code architecture Reusable custom workflows run_MOSAIC.R refactored: Replaced 300+ lines inline NPE code clean run_NPE() call (lines 1500-1523) Standalone examples added: vm/launch_mosaic.R now includes post-hoc NPE usage examples (lines 201-233) See function documentation: ?run_NPE

title: MOSAIC 0.10.25
text: Fixed gsub order parameter name variant generation seasonality params Root cause: Wrong order gsub() calls generated incorrect variant “a1j” instead “a1” Original: gsub("_j$", "", gsub("_", "", "a_1_j")) → “a1j” (WRONG) Fixed: gsub("_", "", gsub("_j$", "", "a_1_j")) → “a1” (CORRECT) Impact: Prior/BFRS use a1, NPE uses a_1_j - variant “a1j” didn’t match either Plotting function now correctly finds seasonality params three JSONs Fixed lines 515-516 plot_model_distributions.R Result: three curves (Prior, BFRS, NPE) now appear seasonality parameters

title: MOSAIC 0.10.23
text: Added uniform distribution handling .fit_distribution() NPE posterior processing Root cause: Function handled beta, gamma, lognormal, normal - uniform dist_type=“uniform”, function fell normal case, setting mean/sd instead min/max Result: NPE posteriors.json {distribution: "uniform", parameters: []} Plotting function couldn’t plot uniform without min/max parameters → NPE curves missing Solution: Calculate min/max 1% 99% quantiles (robust outliers) + 1% buffer Fixed lines 1516-1528 npe_posterior.R Impact: NPE posteriors now appear distribution plots (Prior vs BFRS vs NPE)

title: MOSAIC 0.10.13
text: Implemented automatic detection tiny values (< 0.001) posterior distributions Applied scientific notation formatting x-axis labels values < 0.001 Increased padding 10% 30% better visibility tiny value distributions Fixed 8 panels: Prior, Retained, Retained Weighted, Best Unweighted, Best Weighted, Caterpillar, Distributions (Empirical), Distributions (Theoretical) Root cause: E_initial I_initial epidemiologically correct tiny values (~10⁻⁷ proportion, representing ~50-60 people population 126M), appeared flat lines plotted wide [0,1] axis Solution: Tight x-axis limits scientific notation (e.g., “2.0e-07”, “4.0e-07”, “6.0e-07”) Fixed throughout plot_model_posteriors_detail.R (lines 380-769) See claude/initial_EI_plotting_issue.md complete analysis

title: MOSAIC 0.10.10
text: Removed density-based weighting caused double-weighting artifact NPE samples drawn directly posterior p(θ|x), uniform weights correct Using density weights creating effective distribution [p(θ|x)]² -emphasized high-density regions -represented uncertainty Now uses uniform weights (NULL) proper posterior predictive sampling Fixed lines 1679-1686 run_MOSAIC.R Impact: NPE ensemble predictions now appropriate uncertainty bands Theory: posterior predictive p(y|x) ≈ (1/N) Σ p(y|θᵢ) θᵢ ~ p(θ|x) Density weights correct importance sampling q(θ) ≠ p(θ|x) See claude/npe_weighting_analysis.md complete theoretical analysis
